title: Bug: Cannot delete boards or cards (partially)

triage.skip_reason: Requires database schema investigation and environment-specific repro; the missing 'fileinfo' relation suggests a migration issue that needs manual verification
triage.summary: Deleting boards created from templates or the first card on a board fails with HTTP 500 due to missing 'fileinfo' database relation, indicating an incomplete or missing database migration.
triage.test_hint: Check database migrations for the 'fileinfo' table; verify the delete board and delete card API endpoints handle the case where 'fileinfo' relation does not exist; add integration test for deleting template-derived boards and first cards.

title: Bug: Fail to upload a plugin [migration error]

triage.skip_reason: Migration conflict requires careful analysis of DB state and idempotency logic; no clear repro environment and touches data integrity
triage.summary: Plugin upload fails on PostgreSQL due to duplicate key violation on focalboard_boards_pkey during migration, suggesting a non-idempotent migration step that tries to insert/copy board rows that already exist when upgrading to v9.0.4.
triage.test_hint: Reproduce by installing Focalboard plugin on a PostgreSQL instance that already has existing focalboard_boards data; check migration scripts for INSERT/SELECT INTO statements that lack conflict handling (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING) on the boards primary key.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Go to board
2. Select existing card (or create a new one)
3. Add checkbox or write in existing checkbox list
4. Try adding a new checkbox item or move an existing item up / down
5. see error - when trying to add a new one it deletes a previous checkbox, or even a checkbox in a different list; when trying to move them up/down it moves other checkboxes instead of the selected one. Sometimes the entire card freezes and have to refresh page

## Expected behavior

I expect to be able to add a new checkboxes without this affecting the rest of the content in the box. 
I expect to be able to move an item up/down when clicking "move up/move down" instead of seeing checkboxes moving randomly when selecting these functions.

## Screenshots (optional)

If applicable, add screenshots or a screen recording to elaborate on the problem.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal desktop and browser (have tried and experienced the same issue in both)
 - Version: v.8.0 on desktop
 - Browser and OS: Chrome on Windows 11

## Additional context (optional)

Add any other context about the problem here, and any notes about the severity:
* Sev 2: Affects major functionality with a difficult or non-obvious workaround - I can work around it by rewriting the content of the checkboxes but this is time consuming, non-obvious and non permanent either because the next time I need to move them or create a item the existing ones might get messed up again. I use this functionality for team meetings where we definitely need checkboxes for agenda and things to-do so it is an essential tool.
title: Bug: checkboxes in card not working properly

triage.skip_reason: No repro steps detailed enough to isolate root cause; likely a complex ordering/indexing bug in checkbox block management requiring investigation
triage.summary: Checkbox items in card editor behave incorrectly: adding a new checkbox deletes or corrupts existing ones, move-up/move-down reorders wrong items, and the card can freeze requiring a page refresh.
triage.test_hint: Write a test that adds multiple checkbox items to a card, verifies each item's index/key, then adds a new item mid-list and confirms no other items are removed or reordered; also test move-up/move-down on a specific item and assert only that item changes position.

title: Bug: No longer able to access board via channel permission

triage.skip_reason: Requires investigation into channel-based board permission logic and backend access control; no clear repro steps for root cause, likely a regression in permission checking code
triage.summary: Users joined to a channel with a linked board receive 403 'access denied to board' errors and cannot view the board via channel permission. The board does not appear in the right panel and direct card/board links are also restricted. Affects Focalboard plugin 9.0.0 on Mattermost 9.11.3.
triage.test_hint: Verify that a non-admin user who is a member of a channel linked to a board can successfully GET /api/v2/boards/{boardId} and see the board in the channel sidebar; check that channel membership is correctly used to grant board read access in the permission middleware.

title: Bug: Unable to input text in non-latin keyboard layout

triage.skip_reason: Requires investigation into keyboard event handling and React DOM reconciliation for IME/non-latin input; no clear repro path for automated fix without UI testing setup
triage.summary: Non-latin keyboard input (e.g. Russian) in the card comment field causes a React DOM reconciliation error ('Node.removeChild: The node to be removed is not a child of this node'), which triggers navigation away from the card and displays 'This card doesn't exist or is inaccessible.' The root cause is likely improper handling of IME composition events or keydown/keyup listeners that intercept composition keystrokes and trigger navigation actions before the input can process them.
triage.test_hint: Write a test that simulates compositionstart/compositionupdate/compositionend events on the comment input and verifies no navigation or DOM errors occur; also check keydown handlers for keyboard shortcuts that may fire on non-latin keypresses.

body:
After uploading the latest version of the mattermost plugin, compiled on my computer following the installation documentation, I stumbled upon a rather annoying bug : cards or templates can’t be deleted and templates can’t be edited (at least they are not saved after edition).

## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Go to your mattermost team space, select Boards (mattermost plugin, compiled on my computer from the repo)
2. Go to any board
3. Select a card 
4. Click on ... and select delete
5. The card isn’t deleted

AND

1. Go to any board
2. Click on the down arrow next to New
3. Select an existing template, click on Edit
4. Change name and close modal
5. Go back to templates list
6. The name hasn’t been changed

## Expected behavior

Being able to delete cards and edit templates names

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Mattermost Boards (plugin)
 - Version: Latest
 - Browser and OS: Brave on MacOS

## Additional context (optional)

* Sev 1: Affects critical functionality without a workaround
title: Bug: Unable to delete cards or templates, unable to edit templates names in Boards (focalboard mattermost plugin)

triage.skip_reason: No repro steps with a released build; self-compiled plugin makes root cause ambiguous (permissions, API mismatch, build artifact issue). Needs server logs and network traces to diagnose.
triage.summary: Cards cannot be deleted and template names cannot be saved in the Mattermost Boards plugin when self-compiled from latest repo. Delete action silently fails; template name edits are not persisted.
triage.test_hint: Verify DELETE /api/v2/boards/{boardId}/cards/{cardId} returns 200; check PATCH for template block returns updated name; confirm websocket or REST response propagates UI state change.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Create linked board
2. For most channel members the linked board is not visible; those channel members, for whom the linked board is visible, are automatically listed as editors in the access permissions list

## Expected behavior

The linked board should be visible for all channel members as defined by the general access rules "everyone in team abc is editor" and "everyone in channel xyz is editor".

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Mattermost Boards (plugin)
 - Version: v8.0.0
 - Mattermost server version: v10.1.2

## Additional Context

For some existing linked boards this behaviour changed recently, thus, this issue does also occur for some already existing linked boards without any additional actions done by the board admins in the access permissions list.

## Workarounds

1. Add channel users manually as editors to the access permissions list.
2. Re-linking the board might help but not reliably for all channel members.
title: Bug: Some linked boards are not displayed anymore for most channel members

triage.skip_reason: Complex permissions/access control regression affecting channel-linked boards; requires deep investigation of backend permission evaluation logic and recent changes; no minimal repro steps or code pointer provided
triage.summary: Linked boards are not visible to most channel members despite team/channel-wide editor access rules being configured. Only users explicitly listed as editors in the permissions list can see the board. Regression introduced around v8.0.0/v10.1.2.
triage.test_hint: Test that a user who is a channel member but NOT explicitly listed in the board's ACL can view a linked board when 'everyone in channel is editor' rule is active; verify permission evaluation queries correctly resolve implicit channel membership grants.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. I migrated mattermost to postgreSQL from MySQL.
2. I followed all the steps mentioned on this page: https://docs.mattermost.com/deploy/postgres-migration.html
3. After mattermost migration from MySQL to Postgresql boards are behaving strangely 
4. On a team, i get the boards on my desktop app and on other team i am getting error of You are about to join a private board.
    But the same board which worked on my desktop app is showing joining private board message on web version. 
5. For the one which works on my desktop app here is the url
  https://mattermost-server/plugins/focalboard/api/v2/teams/w8orwn8u7bg4x8da9gqosuomna/users
6. On web, i am getting `{"error":"User IDs are empty","errorCode":400} ` error

7. Other errors: That  is something like this

URL: https://mattermost-server/plugins/focalboard/api/v2/boards/b8dy7huuhtpdftx385htrmn4agh/blocks?all=true

Response: `{"error":"{boards} not found","errorCode":404}`

## Expected behavior

All the boards should work on both web and desktop and all the users must be able to see the same output.

If applicable, add screenshots or a screen recording to elaborate on the problem.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Desktop / Personal Server / Mattermost Boards (plugin)
 - Version: [v8.0]
 - Browser and OS: Chrome on Mac
title: Bug: After mattermost migration from MySQL to Postgresql boards are behaving strangely

triage.skip_reason: No clear repro steps reproducible in isolation; requires MySQL-to-PostgreSQL migration environment, likely involves data integrity or schema migration issues in the backend with no minimal test case provided
triage.summary: After migrating Mattermost from MySQL to PostgreSQL, Focalboard boards behave inconsistently: some boards are inaccessible (404 'not found'), users get 'User IDs are empty' (400) errors, and private board join prompts appear incorrectly. Likely a data migration or schema mapping issue introduced during the DB migration.
triage.test_hint: Verify board and block table data integrity post-migration; check if board IDs, team IDs, and user IDs were correctly migrated without truncation or encoding issues; inspect API handlers for PostgreSQL-specific query differences vs MySQL.

body:
**Version:**
`0.6.7`, standalone

**Steps to reproduce the behavior:**
1. Create a "Personal Tasks" board
2. Add an entry in the Todo-Column
3. Enter another entry in the Todo-Column
4. Add a property Due-Date to said entry
5. Check the other entry: it now also has a due date
6. delete that due date property on the other entry
7. Check the second entry: its due date is now gone

**Expected behavior:**
Properties should be task-specific.

**Platform:**
 - Linux
title: Bug: Properties are not task-specific

triage.skip_reason: Core data model issue — property schema vs property values are conflated; requires deep architectural understanding of how card properties and board property definitions interact
triage.summary: Property definitions (schema) are shared across all cards on a board, but property values should be per-card. Adding or deleting a property type on one card affects all cards because the property schema is stored at the board level and deletion of a property removes it from the schema entirely rather than just clearing the value on that card.
triage.test_hint: Add a property to one card, verify other cards on the same board do not gain the property value; delete a property value on one card, verify other cards retain their values.

body: After updating the Mattermost Windows Desktop application to version 6.0.2, the application fails to load and remains stuck on the blue loading screen. This issue occurs when connecting to a Mattermost server running version 10.5.14. The application launches successfully, but the user interface never completes loading and no error message is displayed. The issue persists even after restarting the application and the Windows system. As a result, users are unable to access Mattermost via the desktop application. The web version of Mattermost continues to function normally.
title: Bugs: Mattermost Windows v6.0.2 Stuck on Blue Loading Screen (Server 10.5.14)

triage.skip_reason: No repro steps, no logs, UI hang with unknown root cause requires investigation across desktop/frontend boundary
triage.summary: Mattermost Windows desktop v6.0.2 stuck on blue loading screen when connecting to server 10.5.14; web version works fine
triage.test_hint: Test with Windows desktop v6.0.2 against server 10.5.14, check electron console logs and network requests during loading phase

body:

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Normal installation of Ubuntu 24.04 LTS on a Dell computer with Intel. Normal use for one year.
2. Download version v7.10.6, the file "focalboard-linux.tar.gz", unzip it, and double-click on the executable.
3. Nothing happens, no error message or anything else. → Problem detected.  
4. Launch via command line with `./focalboard-app`.
5. Error message: `./focalboard-app: error while loading shared libraries: libwebkit2gtk-4.0.so.37: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory`.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Desktop
 - Version: V7.10.6
 - Browser and OS: Firefox 134.0.1 (64 bits) ; Ubuntu 24.04 LTS last update.

## Problem Resolution

I use this link : [https://www.chippiko.com/installing-libwebkit2gtk-4-0-ubuntu](https://www.chippiko.com/installing-libwebkit2gtk-4-0-ubuntu)

1. `sudo nano /etc/apt/sources.list`
2. `deb http://gb.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu jammy main`
3. `sudo apt update`
4. `sudo apt install libwebkit2gtk-4.0-37 `

## Expected Behavior

I would have liked an error message informing me that the library is not installed, possibly with commands to resolve it / a link to this issue, or even the automatic installation of the library.
title: Bug: Installation of the libwebkit2gtk library

triage.skip_reason: Requires UI/UX decision on error messaging and potential build system changes; also touches desktop packaging which needs human judgment on whether to bundle libs or update documentation
triage.summary: Focalboard desktop app silently fails on Ubuntu 24.04 LTS because libwebkit2gtk-4.0.so.37 is not available; the library was removed from Ubuntu 24.04 default repos. App should either display a meaningful error message, bundle the dependency, or document the workaround clearly.
triage.test_hint: Launch focalboard-app binary on a clean Ubuntu 24.04 install and verify error handling or dependency resolution behavior

Apache2

```conf ServerName myURL.com Redirect permanent / https://myURL.com/ ServerName myURL.com ProxyPreserveHost On AllowEncodedSlashes NoDecode SSLEngine On ProxyPass /ws/ ws://127.0.0.1:8082/ ProxyPassReverse /ws/ ws://127.0.0.1:8082/ Require all granted ProxyPass http://127.0.0.1:8082/ ProxyPassReverse http://127.0.0.1:8082/ ProxyPassReverseCookieDomain 127.0.0.1 myURL.com SSLCertificateFile /etc/letsencrypt/live/myURL.com/cert.pem SSLCertificateKeyFile /etc/letsencrypt/live/myURL.com/privkey.pem SSLCertificateChainFile /etc/letsencrypt/live/myURL.com/chain.pem ProxyPass / http://localhost:8082/ nocanon ProxyPassReverse / http://localhost:8082/ ```

## Expected behavior That the focal board setup works with apache2 and is responsive and doesn't require a refresh for any change done. A best solution would be that a script for apache2 is provided. ## Edition and Platform - todays git patch and ubuntu 22.04
title: Bug: ws requests don't come through in apache2 reverse proxies.

triage.skip_reason: Requires UI/UX decision on documentation approach and potentially server-side WebSocket path changes; depends on deployment environment specifics
triage.summary: WebSocket connections fail when Focalboard is deployed behind an Apache2 reverse proxy. The WS upgrade requests are not properly proxied, causing real-time updates to break and requiring manual page refreshes. User needs correct Apache2 config with proper mod_proxy_wstunnel setup and RewriteEngine rules for WS upgrade handling.
triage.test_hint: Verify WebSocket connectivity via browser network console when running Focalboard behind Apache2 with mod_proxy_wstunnel enabled and correct RewriteRule/ProxyPass directives for ws:// upgrade.

## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Run a Docker container using the latest image. (`docker run focalboard:latest`)
2. Register a user. Since the page allows arbitrary registration, HTTP basic auth is implemented.
3. Add a user to OpenResty.
4. Open the address, verify the HTTP basic auth, and return to the login page.
5. Enter the username and password, click the login button, and the message "login failed" is displayed.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: mattermost/focalboard:7.11.4 (docker IMAGE NAME)
 - Version: 7.11.4
 - Browser and OS: Chrome on Mac

## Additional context (optional)
1. Add the ability to turn off registration. I couldn't find it in the docs, so if it's already there, just ignore this.
2. Make sure HTTP basic auth doesn't mess with the login user.

**Best regards.**
title: Bug: HTTP basic auth causes user login failure, with the error message: login failed.

triage.skip_reason: No clear repro in CI environment; requires HTTP basic auth proxy setup (OpenResty/nginx); root cause unclear — could be frontend sending Authorization header that conflicts with backend auth, needs investigation
triage.summary: When HTTP basic auth is configured on a reverse proxy (e.g., OpenResty), logging into Focalboard fails with 'login failed'. The browser's basic auth credentials or headers likely interfere with Focalboard's own login API request, causing authentication to fail on the backend.
triage.test_hint: Check if the login fetch/XHR request includes an Authorization header populated by the browser's basic auth session; verify backend login handler does not reject requests that carry a basic auth Authorization header; test with nginx/OpenResty basic_auth and confirm the /api/v2/login endpoint receives and processes credentials correctly.

title: Bug: The Focalboard app sends requests to the localhost externally

triage.skip_reason: No clear repro steps beyond 'open app'; involves network/localhost binding behavior in desktop app that may be OS/browser-engine specific; requires investigation of Electron webview or similar renderer configuration
triage.summary: Focalboard desktop app sends requests to localhost externally (visible to external proxy) and fails to load its own localhost UI; works in Firefox and curl but not in the app's embedded renderer or GNOME Web, suggesting a webview/renderer policy or localhost loopback binding issue on Linux
triage.test_hint: Check Electron webview network configuration and whether localhost loopback is being treated as external; verify Content Security Policy and network isolation settings for the embedded browser context on Linux

**Summary:**

This doesn't seem reproducable if you follow the steps first time but if you repeat the steps and scroll up and down in the menus you'll eventually see what's in the screenshot.

2. Click on "Set Theme" and mouse over options
3. Mouse over some other area on the board and then repeat 1) and 2) until you finally see what's in the screenshot

**Expected behavior:**
Menus remain separate and don't get into the overlapped state

**Platform:**
 - Browser and version: Safari/Big Sur & Chrome
 - OS and version: Safari v11 & Windows 10

body:
**Summary:**
OSX Native spellchecking not respected. When I'm writing in the description of a focalboard card, if i misspell a word and it does the red underline, and then if i proceed to right click on it to select the correct spelling, the content doesn't update with the new correct spelling.. instead its ignored.

**Steps to reproduce the behavior:**
1. Go to a cards description and misspell a word, once its underlined in red
2. Right click on the red underlined misspelled word, and select the correct option

**Expected behavior:**
The word would update with the correctly spelled word.

**Observed behavior:**
The word remains the original incorrect spelling.

**Screenshots:**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/31866506/118680291-9bcc4900-b7cc-11eb-8930-726a455b10b6.png)
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/31866506/118680377-ac7cbf00-b7cc-11eb-8078-d0ffb81904e3.png)

 - OS and version: Latest version of OSX on my macbook

title: Bug: Native macOS spell check does not work correctly in card descriptions

triage.skip_reason: Likely requires understanding of how the rich text editor (e.g., Draft.js or Slate) intercepts native spell-check context menu events on macOS/Chrome; fix involves editor internals and may need manual testing on macOS
triage.summary: Native macOS spell-check context menu replacement does not apply the corrected spelling in card description editor; the editor state is not updated when a suggestion is selected via right-click.
triage.test_hint: Manually test on macOS with Chrome: misspell a word in a card description, right-click the red-underlined word, select a suggestion, and verify the word is replaced. Check if the editor intercepts or swallows the native input event from the spell-check menu.

body:
**Summary:** I'm running Focalboard in docker behind the Traefik2 Reverse Proxy that has some middlewares set up. When trying to register I'm presented with an "Invalid registration link, please contact your administrator" error. There are no log messages written. The curious part is, that *any* Traefik2 middleware seems to cause this issue, but leaving them out works. I've tested with a Ratelimit middleware, a Headers middleware that just inserts a same-origin referrer policy and a basic-auth middleware. All of them cause the same issue, which makes a configuration mistake on my side very unlikely. **Steps to reproduce the behaviour:** 1. clone the repo 2. add traefik labels to docker-compose config (full configs below) 3. add db and app containers to network (full configs below) 4. docker-compose build and up 5. go to the domain configured in traefik 6. try to create a account **Expected behaviour:** Being able to create a account. **Platform:** - Browser and version: Firefox 87.0 - OS and version: Server: Ubuntu 20.04 LTS, Client: Windows 10 **Additional context:**
docker-compose.yml

Docker compose with added networks and Traefik2 labels. ```yaml version: "3" services: app: build: context: ./ container_name: focalboard depends_on: - focalboard-db environment: - VIRTUAL_HOST=focalboard.local - VIRTUAL_PORT=8000 volumes: - "./config.json:/opt/focalboard/config.json" networks: - internal - t2_proxy labels: - "traefik.enable=true" - "traefik.docker.network=t2_proxy" ## HTTP Routers - "traefik.http.routers.focalboard-rtr.entrypoints=https" - "traefik.http.routers.focalboard-rtr.rule=Host(`focalboard.domain.com`)" - "traefik.http.routers.focalboard-rtr.tls=true" ## Middlewares - "traefik.http.routers.focalboard-rtr.middlewares=focal-test-chain@file" ## HTTP Services - "traefik.http.routers.focalboard-rtr.service=focalboard-svc" - "traefik.http.services.focalboard-svc.loadbalancer.server.port=8000" focalboard-db: image: postgres:latest container_name: focalboard-postgres restart: always volumes: - "./data:/var/lib/postgresql/data" networks: - internal environment: POSTGRES_DB: boards POSTGRES_USER: boardsuser POSTGRES_PASSWORD: boardsuser-password networks: internal: t2_proxy: external: name: t2_proxy ```

traefik2 ratelimit middleware

Traefik2 middleware config used ```toml [http.middlewares] [http.middlewares.middlewares-rate-limit] [http.middlewares.middlewares-rate-limit.rateLimit] average = 100 burst = 50 [http.middlewares.focal-test-chain] [http.middlewares.focal-test-chain.chain] middlewares = [ "middlewares-rate-limit" ] ````

Request HAR json

This is the HAR from the request that is sent when i click the register button. Domains have been replaced with domain.com and IPs with 0.0.0.0. The error occurs whether or not i enter anything in the form, here i've left it empty. ```json { "log": { "version": "1.2", "creator": { "name": "Firefox", "version": "87.0" }, "browser": { "name": "Firefox", "version": "87.0" }, "pages": [ { "startedDateTime": "2021-04-11T19:20:33.089+02:00", "id": "page_4", "title": "Focalboard", "pageTimings": { "onContentLoad": -1, "onLoad": -1 } } ], "entries": [ { "pageref": "page_4", "startedDateTime": "2021-04-11T19:20:33.089+02:00", "request": { "bodySize": 51, "method": "POST", "url": "https://focalboard.domain.com/api/v1/register", "httpVersion": "HTTP/2", "headers": [ { "name": "Host", "value": "focalboard.domain.com" }, { "name": "User-Agent", "value": "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:87.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/87.0" }, { "name": "Accept", "value": "application/json" }, { "name": "Accept-Language", "value": "en-GB,en;q=0.5" }, { "name": "Accept-Encoding", "value": "gzip, deflate, br" }, { "name": "Referer", "value": "https://focalboard.domain.com/register" }, { "name": "Content-Type", "value": "application/json" }, { "name": "Authorization", "value": "" }, { "name": "X-Requested-With", "value": "XMLHttpRequest" }, { "name": "Origin", "value": "https://focalboard.domain.com" }, { "name": "Content-Length", "value": "51" }, { "name": "DNT", "value": "1" }, { "name": "Connection", "value": "keep-alive" }, { "name": "Pragma", "value": "no-cache" }, { "name": "Cache-Control", "value": "no-cache" }, { "name": "TE", "value": "Trailers" } ], "cookies": [], "queryString": [], "headersSize": 522, "postData": { "mimeType": "application/json", "params": [], "text": "{\"email\":\"\",\"username\":\"\",\"password\":\"\",\"token\":\"\"}" } }, "response": { "status": 401, "statusText": "Unauthorized", "httpVersion": "HTTP/2", "headers": [ { "name": "content-type", "value": "application/json" }, { "name": "date", "value": "Sun, 11 Apr 2021 17:20:02 GMT" }, { "name": "content-length", "value": "12" }, { "name": "X-Firefox-Spdy", "value": "h2" } ], "cookies": [], "content": { "mimeType": "application/json", "size": 12, "text": "{\"error\":\"\"}" }, "redirectURL": "", "headersSize": 136, "bodySize": 148 }, "cache": {}, "timings": { "blocked": 0, "dns": 0, "connect": 0, "ssl": 0, "send": 1, "wait": 26, "receive": 1 }, "time": 28, "_securityState": "secure", "serverIPAddress": "0.0.0.0", "connection": "443" } ] } } ```
title: Bug: Unable to register when running behind traefik reverse proxy with any middleware.

triage.skip_reason: Requires reverse proxy environment to reproduce; likely involves token/header validation or origin checking that is affected by middleware — needs investigation of registration link validation logic
triage.summary: Registration fails with 'Invalid registration link' error when Focalboard is deployed behind a Traefik2 reverse proxy with any middleware enabled. The issue appears to be in how the backend validates registration tokens or request headers when proxied, as removing all middlewares resolves the issue.
triage.test_hint: Check registration token validation logic in the backend — look for origin, referer, or host header checks that may fail when headers are modified or added by Traefik middlewares. Reproduce by spinning up Focalboard behind a minimal Traefik setup with a single middleware (e.g., rate-limit) and attempting to register.

body:
**Summary:**
A two factor authentication method should be added to greatly improve the security of the application, in addition to that, receiving a email when someone tries to log into our account would be very good as it would allow us to know whether our password got leaked. All of these should be features that the user can enable or disable.

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: High
Security is a fundamental pillar in today's web applications

Use cases:
1. Prevent unauthorized access
2. Get notified when someone tries to access your account using your password

**Additional context / similar features:**
Almost all websites have a two factor authentication option.
A basic implementation of this would be to support the following methods:
* Authenticator app
* Email
* Backup codes
title: Feature Idea: Two factor authentication

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring significant backend auth changes, UI decisions, and security review; not suitable for automated implementation.
triage.summary: Request to add two-factor authentication (TOTP/authenticator app, email, backup codes) and login notification emails as optional user-configurable security features.

body:
**Summary:**
What I really miss right now is the ability to see what is happening on boards I'm participating in. Have people updated descriptions, added comments? The only way to detect these things (even to see if a card has a comment or not) is to regularly open every single one and do a "visual diff." This is not great, it would be really cool to have a view where you can see recent changes to a board, or even get notified about ones about cards I care about.

**How important this is to me and why:**
Pretty important. Right now I end up manually notifying people about my comments out of band (on Mattermost messaging).

Importance: High

Use cases:
1. Better monitor board activity
2. Know when people ask questions in comments

**Additional context / similar features:**
Any examples of good implementations of this capability.

Any type of timeline view would be helpful, if I'd have to pick between notifications that are pushed to me (somehow) or just a stream view (question if this should be per board or per workspace — I'd say per workspace) I'd pick the latter. But notifications could also be posted (optionally, perhaps filtered) on the Mattermost channel the boards are connected to. That could be a "simple" MVP implementation perhaps. "Comment added to card X by Y."
title: Feature Idea: Notifications/event stream

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring significant backend event infrastructure, notification delivery system, and UI design decisions; no concrete spec defined.
triage.summary: Users want an activity/event stream and notification system to track changes (comments, edits) on boards they participate in, with optional Mattermost channel integration as a potential MVP.

body:
**Summary:**
Due date field type, a la "created" and "updated" but meant to track the date (and possibly time) a task is due.  As with Trello, the colour of the card can change depending on the current amount of time until (or past) the given timestamp we are.

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: High

Use cases:
1. Completely general for both personal and professional use.  If something needs to be done today, or within the next hour, it's valuable to have a program let you track that and remind you of it.

**Additional context / similar features:**
This is a basic feature of Trello for the above reason.

To the extent that the "due date" seen in the screenshots suggests such functionality, it's misleading as currently implemented: The "due date" field in the template turns out to be only a text label.  I guess that's technically sortable if you stick to ISO timestamps / datestamps, but there's no inherent date functionality, i.e. the software cannot display a calendar view, or calculate how far away a task is, or sort correctly if you prefer a 12-hour clock (e.g. `"11:00 AM" > "1:00 PM"`).

(It would make sense that the import scripts would take this data from Trello, Asana, etc. once implemented.)
title: Feature Idea: Actual due dates

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring new field type, calendar UI, color-coded card logic, sorting, and potential notification system — too broad for auto-attempt.
triage.summary: Add a native Date field type with calendar picker, correct sorting, and visual indicators (color coding) for upcoming/overdue tasks, similar to Trello's due date functionality.
triage.test_hint: Verify date field renders a calendar picker, sorts correctly across AM/PM and ISO formats, and card color updates based on proximity to due date.

body:
## Summary

As stated by @wiggin77 in this [comment](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/3852#issuecomment-1283984863) notifications by the notification bot will only work if each (non-creator) person involved into a card has clicked "Follow" or has been mentioned manually.

From our point of view:
We have multiple cards that travel along a process of reviews etc. and need to be passed between members of the team.
Therefor we use the Type: "Multiple Persons" and Type: "Person".

Now it seems that people added to this field are not auto subscribed to the card and do not receive notifications until we mention those people in the comments or they subscribe themselves.

There should be a flag for "auto subscribe" for those to field types, so that we can enable automatic notifications for "traveling" cards, which we assign to other people.

## How important this is to me and why
Without notifications the boards are not maintained by the people and tasks will sit around for ages until the person who is responsible remembers to have a look at the boards and filter to cards assigned to them.

Importance: High

Use cases:
1. Chained of task states that are passed between people
2. Centrally managed notifications on assigned people

## Additional context/similar features
Any other Kanban software does automatically follow new assigned persons afaik.
title: Feature Idea: Set Card Owner and Auto Enable Following

triage.skip_reason: Feature request requiring UI decisions and backend notification logic changes; no clear spec on edge cases (unassign, re-assign, opt-out).
triage.summary: Auto-subscribe users to a card when they are added to a Person or Multi-Person property field, so they receive notifications without needing to manually follow or be @mentioned.
triage.test_hint: Verify that adding a user to a Person/Multi-Person property triggers a follow subscription; verify removing them does not orphan the subscription; verify existing manual followers are unaffected.

body: **Summary:** Include other properties in the CSV Export besides Title & Category **How important this is to me and why:** Importance: Medium Use cases: 1. Navigating to the one of the cards on the board shows the following: which includes the Title, Status, Category, Due Date, and Who properties 2. When exported to CSV, it only shows the **Title** and **Category** columns. **Additional context / similar features:** It will be nice to have the rest of the properties column exported in the CSV file as well to capture the entire information of the cards in the board.
title: Feature Idea:  Include other properties in the CSV Export besides Title & Category

triage.skip_reason: Requires understanding of board property schema, CSV serialization logic, and potentially complex property type handling (dates, selects, users); needs design decisions on column ordering and multi-value properties
triage.summary: CSV export only includes Title and Category columns, omitting all other card properties (e.g. Status, Due Date, Who). Users expect all visible card properties to be exported.

body:
**Summary:**
Add configuration options: 1) enable proxy auth, 2) proxy auth http-header (e.g. `X-Authenticated-User`).

If proxy auth is enabled, user login is bypassed and the user that is present in the specified header is used.

Optional: if no such user exists, automatically create one.

**How important this is to me and why:**
Some on-premise hosting approaches put everything behind a reverse proxy like Traefik and let authentication be handled there. See e.g. [here](https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/middlewares/http/forwardauth/). This feature would enable this kind of deployment.

Importance: High - because it might be really easy and therefore a quick win.

Use cases:
1. on-premise hosting with reverse-proxy auth
2. personal self-hosting

**Additional context / similar features:**
Example: [Filebrowser](https://filebrowser.org/configuration/authentication-method#proxy-header)

Thanks a lot, cheers!
title: Feature Request: Add support for authentication by reverse proxy

triage.skip_reason: Large auth feature requiring security review, config changes, middleware, and optional user provisioning — too broad for auto-attempt
triage.summary: Add reverse proxy authentication support: config flags to enable proxy auth and specify a trusted header (e.g. X-Authenticated-User) that bypasses normal login, with optional auto-provisioning of new users.
triage.test_hint: Test that proxy auth header correctly identifies/creates users, that it is disabled by default, that missing/empty headers are rejected when enabled, and that normal auth still works when proxy auth is off.

body:
#### Summary
This pull request significantly enhances the Trello import process by including comprehensive support for card attachments, images, and comments. It also introduces `createdBy` metadata tracking and addresses previously identified issues with checklist imports. Additionally, it ensures new items are properly linked to their respective boards and cards for a smoother integration.

_Enhancements_:

- Implemented the import of attachments, images, and comments to cards for a full-fidelity transfer.
- Added createdBy fields to track the creation time of cards and comments, preserving important metadata.
- Fixed existing issues related to checklist imports to ensure data consistency.
- Established robust links for newly added items with their corresponding boards and cards, enhancing data structure integrity.
title: Import from trello: comments, images, attachments

triage.test_hint: Run Trello import with a board containing cards that have attachments, images, comments, and checklists; verify all are imported correctly with proper board/card linkage and createdBy metadata.

body:
**Summary:**

The mac app currently bundles a server and starts it on launch. It'd be great, if I could also connect the app to an external server. I know I could simply use the browser to connect but the native app is nicely separated from my myriad of browser tabs which makes it a lot easier to switch back and forth between Focalboard and other apps.

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: Low

Use cases:

1. You run a Focalboard server at home and want to connect to it from different devices, one of them being a mac
2. You run a Focalboard server at work and want to connect to it with a native(-ish) app that doesn't live inside a browser tab

**Additional context / similar features:**

I've had this same use case for Jira for years. Unfortunately, when they finally made a native mac app, it turned out to not have the same set of features that the website had. The Focalboard native app being an exact copy of the web UI is a huge plus for me.

I believe this would also be a prerequisite for generalizing the mac app to run on iOS as well (#160).

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring significant architecture changes to desktop app; involves UI decisions for server configuration and has cross-platform implications (iOS #160); needs product/design input.
triage.summary: Allow the native macOS desktop app to connect to an external Focalboard server instead of only the bundled local server, enabling use cases like remote team servers or home servers accessed from multiple devices.
triage.test_hint: Verify app can switch between local bundled server and a remote server URL; confirm authentication flow works against external server; ensure local-only mode still functions when no external server is configured.

**Summary:**
Lookup column/data type to relate cards/items in different boards.
This lookup column should be clickable to open up the card in the other board directly.
There should also be the possibility to add additional columns from the other board to the board with the lookup column.


**How important this is to me and why:**
Medium

Use cases:
1. Ability to have a board with e.g. Clients where each card contains lots of client information. Then have a projects board where the client can be specified in a lookup column, with an easy link back to the card in the Client board.
2. Ability to add additional cards from e.g. the Clients board (e.g. Client contact person) to the projects board, therefore eliminating the need for having to open the entire Client card.

**Additional context / similar features:**
Similar to lookup columns in SharePoint.

This might be loosely related to issue mattermost/mattermost-server#23273 but when reading that issue I felt these two warranted their own issues.

## Summary

Currently, there is no way to know if a card has a "comment" or a "description" without clicking on it.
That would be super neat to have an icon that tells me so at first glance (e.g. a three-dots "..." or an ℹ️  on the bottom right of a card). If there is this clue, I know I have to click the card to see the full context. If not, I know clicking it won't give me any additional info.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: High (to me at least :) )

Use cases:
1. I usually use cards for work-to-do-later. Sometimes the description is enough for me to remember everything two months later. Sometimes I add ideas or URLS in the comments, and I usually have forgotten them two months later. But sometimes I forgot that _I even added_ these details, and I never click the card. So I don't retrieve the hints my past-self left for my current-self, and as a consequence I waste time to re-do what I did previously. If I had a visual clue, that would not happen

## Additional context/similar features

I know very little about web development, so I'm afraid I cannot do this myself, even though I think that should not be super complex to do.
Unless you have very clear instructions or hints, and I might try to do this myself?

Thanks!

body:
**Summary:**

Currently the comment section renders above the description section. I personally find this a little inconvenient when working on a board alone because I rarely ever use the comment functionality but I very frequently edit or read the description section.

Even in multi-user scenarios, I think it would be more intuitive to have the two sections swapped. When looking at a card, it seems more likely that you first want to understand all the details about the card before reading other people's comments. As an example, Jira (not that it would be the pinnacle of UX) displays a task's summary, description and attachments before the comment section.

Instead of (or in addition to) swapping the sections, there could maybe also be an option to disable comments or to freely reorder the two sections per card (though that might be overkill).

![Screenshot 2021-04-27 at 14 43 13](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1137962/116243493-48d01a80-a767-11eb-9d54-ae6b89d46c72.png)

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: Low

Use cases:

1. I want to parse the information on a card without other people's comments getting in the way
2. I'm a single user and don't want to use the comments
title: Feature Idea: Swap order of comment and description sections

triage.skip_reason: UI layout decision requiring design approval
triage.summary: Request to swap the order of description and comment sections in card view, placing description above comments for better readability and alignment with common tools like Jira.
triage.test_hint: Verify description section renders above comment section in card detail view across all card types.

body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing a pull request! Here are a few tips to help you:

1. If this is your first contribution, make sure you've read the Contribution Checklist https://developers.mattermost.com/contribute/getting-started/contribution-checklist/
2. Read our blog post about "Submitting Great PRs" https://developers.mattermost.com/blog/2019-01-24-submitting-great-prs
3. Take a look at other repository specific documentation at https://developers.mattermost.com/contribute
-->

#### Summary
There were three Possible Choices to Implement a Mount for a Config File in Docker:

1) docker run -v:
You could specify a Volume at the Start of a Container, but it has the Problem that the Folder on the Container, gets overwritten by the Host. So the Config File would have needed to be Included Explicitly. Additional, it would have needed to be separated from the Other files, because they would have been deleted otherwise in the Process of mounting. 

2) Docker Volume:
Instead of creating it at runtime, it could be created beforehand, which would solve the Above Issue and would Allow for Sharing of Files from the Container with the Host. But this would complicate the Setup needed even further. Therefore, three was chosen. 

3) Dockerfile Volume:
As the focalboard Database already gets its own Volume Created in the Dockerfile to persist, I added a Volume Creation for the Config File as well in the Dockerfile. This requires less Setup, while still allowing for Easy Access of the Config File.

The Config File Source on the Host System can be found via the following Command:
`docker inspect *Container Name* | grep -B 1 "/opt/focalboard" | grep "Source"`
Be Aware that with a WSL 2 Docker Setup under Windows, the Folder Location differs from the one displayed.

(Note: The Container needs to be Restarted for the Updates to take Place)

#### Ticket Link
Provides Solution too https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/1889
title: Added Docker Config Volume

triage.skip_reason: Stale PR with UI/infrastructure design decisions; requires human review of Docker volume strategy and security implications of exposing config files via volumes.
triage.summary: Adds a Docker named volume for the Focalboard config file in the Dockerfile, mirroring the existing database volume, to allow easier config file access and persistence.
triage.test_hint: Build the Docker image, run the container, verify config volume is created, modify config via host-side volume mount, restart container and confirm changes are picked up.

## Summary

Currently database is stored at the /Documents/Focalboard directory in windows, presumably similar in MacOS and Linux. This proposal suggests adding a custom database location.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

I like focalboard as it's a fully portable app, but not being able to have the database in the same dir as the executable makes it more cumbersome to move across devices.

Use cases:
1. Having a focalboard executable and database in a pendrive, so it's fully portable
2. Having a folder with differente databases, being able to tell focalboard at anytime what database to load

## Additional context/similar features

None

Any examples of good implementations of this capability: In the settings menu, add an entry called "Set Database location" that opens a native file explorer dialog and allows to choose an existing .db or a directory where it will be created. This setting is persistent. The focalboard path should be stored in a file next to the executable so focalboard can remain fully portable.
title: Feature Idea: Custom Database location

triage.skip_reason: UI decisions required (native file dialog, settings menu design), cross-platform path handling complexity, and no clear spec for all edge cases (multi-DB switching, migration of existing data).
triage.summary: Allow users to configure a custom database file/directory location via a settings UI, enabling fully portable usage (e.g., running from a USB drive). Config path would be stored next to the executable.
triage.test_hint: Verify custom path is persisted across restarts; test on Windows, macOS, Linux; test with invalid/read-only paths; test migration from default location.

body:
**How important this is to me and why:**

body: **Summary:** We should consider adding a `Check for Updates...` section in the Focalboard menu. Refer to the screenshot below to know where it exactly needs to be added: **How important this is to me and why:** Importance: Medium Use cases: 1. If a user haven't installed Focalboard from the App Store, and have instead installed by downloading zip/tar.gz from [releases](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/releases) he/she can check for updates periodically using this feature. **Additional context / similar features:** Wondering if this feature should be added to Mattermost Desktop App as well, since I know a lot of them install Mattermost directly from the website or github repo. (/cc @Willyfrog)
title: Feature Idea: Add a "Check for Updates..." section in the Focalboard menu.

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX decision required; involves desktop app menu integration and update-check logic with unclear spec
triage.summary: Add a 'Check for Updates...' menu item in the Focalboard desktop app menu to allow users who installed via zip/tar.gz to manually check for new releases.
triage.test_hint: Verify menu item appears in Focalboard menu, triggers update check against GitHub releases API, and shows appropriate result dialog for both up-to-date and new-version scenarios.

body:
Submitted on behalf of contributor `ewwollesen`

**Summary:**
Add "Confirm / Cancel" options when adding a Card

**How important this is to me and why:**
Medium

I didn't find this to be super intuitive because when you click to create a new card I would think you'd expect to see a confirm and/or cancel dialog, but those don't exist. I clicked the X on the top left of the card thinking that would cancel the creation and it just took me back to the board with the card having been created. Same with clicking off of the card, I would have expected that to cancel the creation but it created a new card anyway.
title: Feature Idea: Add "Confirm / Cancel" options when adding a Card

triage.skip_reason: Requires UI/UX design decision on confirmation flow and behavior changes to card creation lifecycle
triage.summary: Users expect a Confirm/Cancel dialog when creating a new card. Currently, clicking X or clicking outside the card dialog still saves the card, which is counterintuitive. Request is to either add explicit confirm/cancel buttons or treat dismiss actions as cancellation for empty/new cards.
triage.test_hint: Verify that dismissing a new card dialog without input does not persist the card; verify explicit save action creates the card; regression test existing card editing flow.

body:
Every single LSIO docker container has this option which I think is amazing: https://docs.linuxserver.io/general/understanding-puid-and-pgid

I would like to run Focalboard as a particular user, which would solve a lot of permission issues. In all LSIO containers I just set the PUID and PGID environment variables and boom, the containers runs as that user.

Would it be possible to add such a feature? It makes managing Docker containers much easier.
title: Feature Idea: Add PUID and PGID environment variables to run docker container as non-root user

triage.skip_reason: UI/infrastructure design decision; requires changes to Docker entrypoint scripting and possibly base image; no clear spec for implementation details
triage.summary: Add PUID/PGID environment variable support to the Focalboard Docker image so the container process runs as a specified non-root user, similar to LinuxServer.io containers.
triage.test_hint: Run container with PUID/PGID env vars set to a non-root UID/GID and verify the focalboard process runs as that user and can read/write its data directory.

body: **Summary:** Add some up and down arrows in card view that would allow scrolling to "previous" and "next" cards. **How important this is to me and why:** Because "notes" is not visible in table view, it would be nice to scroll to next, previous cards without leaving list view. Especially from a table view where the order of cards is obvious. Importance: High/Medium/Low Medium Use cases: 1. Reviewing cards detail for list of cards 2. Updating cards detail 3. **Additional context / similar features:** Any examples of good implementations of this capability. Jira, when viewing a "filter list" allows the detail pane to scroll through list.
title: Feature Idea: Allow Card View to scroll between cards.

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX decision required; navigation behavior and design need product agreement
triage.summary: Add previous/next navigation arrows in card detail view to scroll between cards without closing the modal, similar to Jira's filter list navigation.
triage.test_hint: Open a board with multiple cards, open a card detail view, verify prev/next arrows navigate to adjacent cards in the current view order.

body:
**Summary:**
Make it clear where the drag target will land

**How important this is to me and why:**
Right now it is unintuitive when sorting a card or a group where the drag target will end in context to the other items. The problem with the current implementation is that I have to drag on top of an existing element. Therefore, I have no Idea where the existing element will go (or if I even replace it).
 
Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Dragging a card
2. Dragging a group

**Proposal cards:**
Show during drag a marker (blue-line in the screenshot) where the new card will end up.

![order-cards](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/992878/115936537-ce1cac00-a495-11eb-87af-90b93d5f9cc2.png)

**Proposal groups:**
Show during drag a marker (blue-line in the screenshot) where the new group will end up.

![order-group](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/992878/115936550-d83eaa80-a495-11eb-961b-f0bd09ab6744.png)

**Additional context / similar features:**
* [Material design system](https://material.io/components/lists#behavior:~:text=it.-,Items%20can%20be%20dragged%20to%20reorder%20a%20list.) (this is even better, but harder to implement because of the transitions)
title: Feature Idea: Improve visual feedback during drag

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX design decision with non-trivial frontend interaction changes; requires visual design judgement and drag-and-drop library integration work
triage.summary: Add a visual drop indicator (blue line marker) during drag-and-drop operations for both cards and groups, so users can see exactly where the dragged item will be placed before releasing.
triage.test_hint: Test drag reorder of cards within a board and drag reorder of groups; verify blue line indicator appears between items at the correct insertion position; test edge cases (drag to first position, last position, same position).

Font size adjustment in Electron desktop app version.

Much like the Mattermost Chat Electron App, this project is largely unusable on larger screens due to the fixed font size. body:
**Summary:**

Currently the views in a board are sorted alphabetically in the left pane. It would be nice if the views could be sorted manually through drag and drop. Particularly when using emoji as the first character in view names, the alphabetical sorting tends to produce non-intuitive results.

![Screenshot 2021-04-20 at 08 59 12](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1137962/115351857-fe72fa80-a1b6-11eb-8d6b-d490481cf2e2.png)

Importance: Low

Low importance because I'm not using terribly many views (yet) so it's a minor inconvenience only.

Use cases:

1. I'm using emojis as the first character in view names which breaks intuitive alphabetical sorting
2. I want certain views to appear at the top / bottom of the list because I use them more / less frequently
title: Feature Idea: Manual sorting board views

triage.test_hint: Verify drag-and-drop reorders views, order persists after reload, emoji-named views sort correctly per user intent, and alphabetical fallback still works for new boards.

body:
**Summary:**

It would be nice if comments were editable. Right now, once I sent a comment, I can only change it by deleting it and creating a new one.

![Screenshot 2021-04-20 at 08 38 58](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1137962/115349366-0c734c00-a1b4-11eb-9451-86fd6d1bf1f2.png)

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: Low

Low importance because deleting and recreating provides the needed functionality in most cases, only with less convenience.

Use cases:

1. I notice a typo in a comment that I just sent
2. I forgot to add something into a comment that I just sent
3. Another comment was added after the one I want to edit. Deleting and recreating/updating my comment will now change the order of comments.

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX decisions required for inline editing flow; involves both frontend comment component changes and backend API updates

triage.test_hint: Verify edited comment updates in place, timestamp/order preserved, edit UI accessible, and changes persisted after page reload

body:
**Summary:**
As this is a tool that will work best once one has spent a lot of time and effort setting everything up just as one want a good backup would be nice to have. I know there's an export function but as always that is only as strong as the person behind it's routines. As there are a export function I however can't imagine the feature would be hard to build.

My proposal is that the server can run an export to a local folder on the server every x time. Then that folder can be synced to users backup of choice with whatever backup program he or she wants. This feature could either be built into the GUI or it could just be a small script that one can run through cron. 

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: Medium
Work around, take manual backups. Would be nice to get around this in time though. 

Use cases:
1. Backup database to other media in case of emergency.
title: Feature Idea: Backup

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX decisions required for configuration interface; scope unclear (cron script vs GUI vs server-side scheduler); no concrete spec
triage.summary: Request for automated backup functionality: scheduled server-side export to a local folder at configurable intervals, usable via cron or GUI, to complement the existing manual export feature.
triage.test_hint: Verify scheduled export produces valid archive files, respects configured interval, and handles errors gracefully without data loss.

body:
This adds support for displaying inline and displayed Latex equations using Katex in markdown cells, fixing #1509.

The conversion happens before each paragraph is converted from markdown to HTML. At the moment, any parsing error thrown by KaTeX is caught and the chunk ignored. In other words, the following `$\thisisinvalid$` will render as-is, including the surrounding `$`.

# Demonstration
## Markdown editor
![Screenshot from 2021-11-04 11-02-18](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5411875/140302935-a7862292-e741-4d44-aed6-b8b316f678e7.png)

## Automatic rendering when leaving the editor
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5411875/140302787-e84775a8-d6f2-444d-95e1-572d35762489.png)
title: [GH-1509] Add support for Latex rendering in markdown cells

triage.skip_reason: UI/rendering feature requiring visual validation and dependency addition (KaTeX); needs design/product review and thorough testing of edge cases
triage.summary: Adds KaTeX-based LaTeX rendering support for inline and block math expressions in markdown cells, with graceful fallback on parse errors.
triage.test_hint: Verify inline ($...$) and block ($$...$$) LaTeX renders correctly in markdown cells; confirm invalid LaTeX falls back to raw text; check KaTeX bundle size impact.

body:
#### Summary

This PR adds persistence for the user settings in the native Linux app. The settings are written to `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/focalboard/settings`. If `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is unset, the app falls back to `$HOME/.config`.

The change hooks into the existing settings export already used in the native macOS app which means the settings are persisted immediately on change.

Unfortunately, the golang webview package uses a custom native binding technology and doesn't allow to define WebKit message handlers. As a result, a dedicated message handler function was added to the existing `NativeApp` object. In the native macOS app, said handler is short-circuited to `window.webkit.messagehandlers.[NAME].postMessage`. This has the benefit that the web app remains agnostic of the particular native binding mechanism.

I have virtually zero experience with Go so while this appears to be working in my testing, the code very likely is an abomination. I'm looking forward to candid feedback. 🙂 

I'd also appreciate some more testing. When running the Linux app on a mac, I _once_ got a blank screen on launch but didn't manage to reproduce again. When testing on Linux, I didn't observe any issues so far.

#### Ticket Link

Relates to https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/314.
title: [GH-314] Persist user settings in native Linux app

triage.skip_reason: Requires native Linux desktop testing, Go webview binding review, and UI/UX validation; intermittent blank screen regression needs investigation before merge
triage.summary: Adds XDG-compliant user settings persistence for the native Linux Focalboard app by hooking into the existing macOS settings export mechanism via a new NativeApp message handler.
triage.test_hint: Launch native Linux app, change user settings, restart app and verify settings are persisted in $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/focalboard/settings (or ~/.config/focalboard/settings); also test with XDG_CONFIG_HOME unset; verify no blank screen on launch across multiple restarts on both Linux and macOS.

body:
Summary

Enable a mirroring card feature across multiple boards — when a card is updated on one board, all its mirrored instances on other boards automatically sync and reflect the same changes (title, description, comments, attachments, due dates, etc.).

This would allow teams working across different boards to stay aligned without manual duplication or updates.



How important this is to me and why

Importance: High

Keeping cards synchronized across multiple boards is essential for cross-functional collaboration. Currently, duplicating cards and manually updating each one is error-prone and time-consuming. This feature would reduce friction, ensure data consistency, and improve visibility across teams or departments.

Use cases:

Cross-departmental projects: A Product card on the roadmap board is mirrored in the Design and Engineering boards — updates on progress or specs reflect everywhere.

Stakeholder dashboards: A leadership overview board shows key mirrored cards from multiple teams, always up to date.

Templates or shared tasks: Standard operating procedure cards mirrored across projects stay consistent when updated.

Additional context / similar features

Examples of similar implementations:

Notion: Linked database views automatically reflect updates from the original source.

Trello Power-Up “Mirror Cards”: Allows partial mirroring (limited sync capabilities).

ClickUp “Synced Tasks”: Updates to fields sync across mirrored tasks.

Asana “Multi-homing”: One task appears in multiple projects simultaneously.

Future improvements (beyond MVP):

Granular control over what fields are mirrored (e.g., status, due date, or comments).

Sync direction options (one-way vs. two-way).

Visual indicators showing mirrored relationships.

Permissions and visibility control for mirrored cards.
title: Feature Idea: Mirroring Card in different board

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring significant architectural design for cross-board sync, no concrete specs or acceptance criteria, involves backend data model changes, UI decisions, and conflict resolution strategy.
triage.summary: Feature request to support mirroring/syncing cards across multiple boards so that updates to a card (title, description, comments, attachments, due dates) propagate automatically to all mirrored instances on other boards.

body:
**Summary**
A set of enhancements for the "Company Goals & OKRs" template and card functionality to improve usability and flexibility:

1. Allow users to add and delete main Goal cards in the "Company Goals & OKRs" template.

2. Remove or provide an option to disable the automatic emoji assignment for cards.

3. Add rich text formatting (bold, lists, underline, strikethrough) to the card description field.

### How important this is to me and why
### Importance: High

### Use cases:

1. Dynamic Goal Management: Companies have more than three goals per quarter/year. The current static template is unusable for real-world planning, forcing users to look for alternatives.

2. Professional Appearance and Clarity: Automatically assigned, irrelevant emojis make boards look unprofessional and chaotic. Manually correcting them is a significant waste of time.

3. Effective Communication: Without text formatting (lists, bold), it is impossible to structure information clearly within a card description, leading to messy and hard-to-read notes.

**Additional context/similar features**
1. Goal Management: Any other task management tool (like Trello, Asana, ClickUp) allows users to create and delete items freely. The inability to do so for the main goals in this template is a critical limitation.

2. Emoji Handling: The best practice is to make emojis optional or allow users to set a default one. Forced automation in this case reduces usability.

3. Text Formatting: This is a standard feature in any modern text editor and most productivity apps (Notion, Trello, Asana). The description field currently feels like a plaintext field from the 1990s.
title: Feature Idea: Enhance Company Goals & OKRs template and card formatting

triage.skip_reason: Multiple distinct feature requests bundled together (template editing, emoji behavior, rich text editor); requires significant UI/UX decisions and architectural work across frontend and possibly backend
triage.summary: Request to enhance Company Goals & OKRs template with: (1) ability to add/delete goal cards freely, (2) option to disable automatic emoji assignment, (3) rich text formatting (bold, lists, underline, strikethrough) in card description field.

body:
## Summary

If you don’t register an account, how can you achieve real-time synchronization and storage of information? Without real-time synchronization, how can you use it for remote collaborative work? If it doesn’t provide real-time network synchronization, there’s a risk that if your computer experiences a sudden system failure one day and doesn’t automatically save, you could lose data, resulting in significant losses. Therefore, having account synchronization functionality and mobile apps is essential. Thank you.

## How important this is to me and why
Real-time synchronization is an essential feature for both individuals and enterprise teams collaborating remotely. Additionally, having mobile apps is equally crucial. 

Those who have the capability should not be restricted to working solely on PCs. For instance, during travel or while on the move, it’s unlikely that one would carry bulky electronic devices like a PC at all times.

Importance: High
title: Feature Idea: Without registering an account, it is impossible to achieve real-time synchronized storage and updates for services like. This lack of security is concerning. I hope that these platforms can also support mobile apps.

triage.skip_reason: Multi-part feature request (account sync, mobile apps) with no concrete specs; requires product/UX decisions and significant engineering effort
triage.summary: User requests account-based real-time sync for data safety and remote collaboration, plus native mobile app support for iOS/Android

body:
**Summary:**
Add a theme editor, where one could define whatever colors they would like for different elements.

When creating new themes there should already be defined usage for different colors, so all different elements uses e.g. "Primary color", "Secondary color" etc. However, if the user would want to override the color for a specific element, it should be possible to do so as well.


**How important this is to me and why:**
Low

Absolutely not essential.
Though, with that said, the first thing I thought when switching to the dark theme was, "Hmm, I wonder if I can easily change the colors". The reason simply being that the colors does not match my perception of a dark theme, but this is of course just personal preference (but therefore the feature request).

Use cases:
1. Create your own themes.
2. Possibility to easily share themes with other users via import/export functionality.

**Additional context / similar features:**
I am not familiar with how the current themes have been implemented, but I assume there is a `.css` file or similar for each theme, so this theme editor would just create additional of these theme files.

For the import/export functionality I am thinking of just having that being a simple `.json` file, having it easily shareable with others and only text. There could even be a text input where one could paste the entire JSON string to import a theme, instead of having to download a file and browse for it.
title: Feature Idea: Theme editor

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring UI design decisions, new theme architecture, import/export system, and color management — too broad and open-ended for automated attempt
triage.summary: Add a theme editor allowing users to define custom colors for UI elements using semantic color tokens (primary, secondary, etc.), with per-element overrides and JSON-based import/export for sharing themes.

body:
**Summary:**
Sync contents between application instances.

Maybe this is solved with the server edition, but I understand the server edition more about being able to create multiple users and such. My use case is more along the lines of being a single user, so still only personal use, but being able to use the same database instance on both my desktop computer, multiple laptops etc.

**How important this is to me and why:**
High. For me, this is absolutely essential for this type of application.

Use cases:
1. Being able to switch between computers.
2. Pave the way for mobile apps as requested by issue #160.

**Additional context / similar features:**
I see this could be implemented in a couple of different ways.
- Perhaps storing the `focalboard.db` and `files` folder, as mentioned [here for backups](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/253#issuecomment-822765544), in a synced folder, e.g. Dropbox, OneDrive, Google Drive etc. would be enough?
If so, then simply adding an option in the settings for specifying the location of these files would be enough.
- If the above would not be reliable, e.g. conflicts or locks of having the database open in multiple instances at once, then some check for changes would need to be implemented.
However, if going this route I guess just using the server edition would be the better choice, but that would of course require a server to be always on.
title: Feature Idea: Sync contents between application instances

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring architectural decisions about sync strategy, conflict resolution, and multi-instance coordination; no clear spec and involves UI decisions.
triage.summary: User requests ability to sync Focalboard data (database + files) across multiple desktop instances for a single user, enabling use on multiple computers without a full server setup.

body:
It would be very useful to be able to set a WIP limit (work in progress limit), that enables us to set a limit of how many items can be in one "column" (probably most useful for "doing column", but also for other colums).

This is important since it is one if the central ideas of Kanban board.
See [here](Kanban_boards):
> As described in books on Kanban for software development,[4][3] the two primary practices of Kanban are to visualize your work and limit work in progress (WIP).

Importance: High

Use cases:
1. Managing (mental) workload
2. Helps keep task list [SMART](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SMART_criteria) 
3. If used in physical production, helps minimize resources and therefore capital kept "in production"
title: Feature idea: WIP limits

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring UI design decisions, data model changes for per-column WIP limit configuration, and enforcement logic; not suitable for automated attempt
triage.summary: Add WIP (Work In Progress) limit support to Kanban board columns, allowing users to configure a maximum card count per column with visual indicators when the limit is exceeded.
triage.test_hint: Verify that setting a WIP limit on a column shows a warning/indicator when the card count meets or exceeds the limit; verify limit persists across sessions and applies correctly when cards are moved between columns.

**Summary:**
Today when I add a checkbox it seems like I can just add one item. To add multiple check boxes one thus needs to add multiple content boxes. This might be a bug on my system, or it's just not fully implemented.

Example

Packing list:

- [ ] Clothes
   - [ ] Jacket
   - [ ] T shirts
   - [ ] underwear

body:
**Summary:**

This app could be really nice to use with something like emacs org mode or markdown files. This makes using this program in a serverless setup (e.g. via syncthing) very easy and would make it ultimately even possible to have a scrum board checked in with the source code for colaboration. 

Having a slim and content (Blocks and Comments/Atachments) oriented (Go) Interface in order to make it easier to add a new storage  backend would certainly be a good first step. A generalised storage backend plugin could use shell scripts as adaptors for reading file based storage backends (converting eg. between org-mode and json two ways). 

**How important this is to me and why:**

I personally have been looking for an application like this for a long time but keep all my textural data file based and searcheable with grep. If I could make this application could fit into my existing file based notes, dates workflow I'd  start using it immediately. 

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Accessing and editing the board both from IDE and Focalboard app
2. Serverless Collaboration between people via SMB / Dropbox / Syncthing folders.
3. Generally easier integration into existing development ecosystems and pipelines.  

**Additional context / similar features:**

While using one file-per-board, file-per-lane or even file-per-block would certainly improve the problem of edit collisions (as compared to project-per-sqlite-db ) when working on a shared storage it doesn't completely solve the problem of conflicts. This situation has been addressed by: https://github.com/39aldo39/DecSync 

I offer to help with the implementation of such a feature If it finds wider appreciation.
title: Feature Request: File based storage backend

triage.skip_reason: Large architectural feature requiring a new storage backend abstraction layer, file format design decisions, and conflict resolution strategy. No clear spec, involves significant design choices.
triage.summary: Request for a file-based storage backend (e.g., markdown/org-mode files) to enable serverless collaboration via Syncthing/Dropbox/SMB and integration with file-based workflows. Would require a generalized storage backend interface in Go.

body:
## Context

Earlier this week Trello announced [new collaborator limits](https://www.atlassian.com/blog/trello/new-collaborator-limits) on Free workspaces, with 10 members being the cut-off limit for the Free plan.

This change is disrupting the work pipelines in some of the non-profit projects I am involved with, and I am looking for alternatives.
It's not just me; there are a few popular topics on Reddit from people who've faced the same problem ([tap](https://www.reddit.com/r/selfhosted/comments/1b85rq3/alternative_to_trello/), [tap](https://www.reddit.com/r/trello/comments/1b7g4sm/completely_free_alternatives_to_trello/)).

Focalboard keeps popping up -- followed by a note about "but it's discontinued, so.."
Either way, currently this repo attracts attention from folks considering to use or self-host a Trello alternative.

## Opportunity

This could be an opportunity to bootstrap a community effort around Focalboard.
The crowd might big enough to run an "official community fork" and start working on it.
Otherwise the effort could be duplicated, or people will move on to other projects.

## Call for action

Please comment if you're willing to contribute. 
Please do mention areas you'd like to support (i.e. front-end, back-end, UX etc) and anything else you consider relevant.

## Personal story

IMO Focalboard has a friendly codebase which is easy to navigate.
I don't have much experience with Typescript or Go. Still, I was able to enhance the Trello importer with some medium effort (#4874, not converted into a PR yet), adding import support for custom fields etc.
title: Trello is enforcing more limits, could this be an opportunity for Focalboard? Official community fork?

triage.skip_reason: Community/governance discussion, not a concrete technical issue; requires human decision-making about project direction and forking strategy
triage.summary: User requests an official community fork of Focalboard in response to Trello's new free-tier collaborator limits driving migration interest
