body:
Hi team,

I wonder if the September 15th, 2023 move to "fully community supported" model and the "We encourage the community to fork this repository for continued development and contributions." statement warrant an update of LICENSE.txt?

Currently it [outlines a mix of MIT, GNU AGPL v.3.0 and commercial license](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/blob/main/LICENSE.txt), as follows:

```
You are licensed to use compiled versions of Focalboard produced by Mattermost, Inc. under an MIT LICENSE

-	See MIT-COMPILED-LICENSE.md included in compiled versions for details

You may be licensed to use source code to create compiled versions not produced by Mattermost, Inc. in one of two ways:

1. Under the Free Software Foundation’s GNU AGPL v.3.0, subject to the exceptions outlined in this policy; or
2. Under a commercial license available from Mattermost, Inc. by contacting commercial@mattermost.com

You are licensed to use the source code in Admin Tools and Configuration Files (webapp/html-templates/, app-config.json,
config.json, webapp/i18n/, server/model/, plugin/ and all subdirectories thereof) under the Apache License v2.0.

We promise that we will not enforce the copyleft provisions in AGPL v3.0 against you if your application (a) does not
link to Focalboard directly, but exclusively uses Focalboard's Admin Tools and Configuration Files, and
(b) you have not modified, added to or adapted the source code of Focalboard in a way that results in the creation of
a “modified version” or “work based on” Focalboard as these terms are defined in the AGPL v3.0 license.
```

I am not sure to what extent it reflects the "community supported" model. It sounds like patched and self-hosted deployments may need to be careful in using the Focalboard name / logo and/or any © signs if they are displayed somewhere in the app(s).

Any advice?

P.S. I am a developer, not a lawyer.
title: Does "fully community supported" warrant an update of LICENSE.txt?

triage.skip_reason: Legal/licensing decision requires human judgment from Mattermost legal team; not a code change that can be automated
triage.summary: License.txt may need updating to reflect the September 2023 transition to a fully community-supported model, as the current text still references commercial licensing, Mattermost-produced compiled versions, and AGPL enforcement policies that may no longer apply.

body:
As of August 28th, 2024 this repository now only contains the standalone Focalboard project. The Mattermost plugin has been moved to [mattermost/mattermost-plugin-boards](https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost-plugin-boards) where it will be maintained separately.

This project is not currently being maintained and we are looking for community members, with past experience maintaining open source projects, who are interested in becoming maintainers of this project.

If you're interested, please reply to this issue and we'd be happy to discuss.
title: Call for Maintainers - Standalone Focalboard

triage.skip_reason: Community/organizational call-for-maintainers post, not a technical task that can be automated or coded.
triage.summary: Call for community maintainers for the standalone Focalboard project following separation from Mattermost plugin.

body:
## Summary

I often find myself searching for a couple of different cards in quick succession and a constant friction that I notice is having to remove the existing text when I do an alternate search. It would be nice if the search box had a clear button, that I could click to both bring focus to the search box and clear the existing search query. Here's an example of my suggestion/request:

Currently:
![screenshot](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/assets/818633/84977e19-d1f6-4c1d-a281-1eb368458015)

With a search clear button:
![screenshot-clear](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/assets/818633/3b08c5b1-be43-4300-87f3-3a9e97e90cbb)

Obviously there are workarounds e.g. `<Ctrl>`-`<a>` to select all text, then start typing (when focus is already in the search bar), or if focus is elsewhere, then `<Ctrl>`-`<f>` will achieve the same ends. But this inconsistency is part of my friction. I can't just do a consistent single action to clear the search box.

Another workaround with the mouse is to select all search text and start typing, but that involves getting the selection right, which requires more thought and/or mouse accuracy than say, clicking a button :grimacing: .

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium to Low (annoyance)

## Additional context/similar features

IME a search "clear" button is common and almost ubiquitous, anywhere a search box is found. E.g. google search.

I suspect that the implementation would be relatively trivial and I'd potentially be open to having a go myself. However, I don't really know go or typescript/javascript (beyond the very basic basics - and I suspect the latter would be required, possibly the former too?). I'm familiar with a number of other languages, so I'm sure I could fudge my way through it and at least string together a PoC. But without some pointers on where I should start, it feels pretty overwhelming...

Plus there's also the issue that even if I do the work and implement this myself, now focalboard is "community supported", it's unclear when or even if it would get merged?!
title: Feature Idea: Search box "clear" button

triage.summary: Add a clear (×) button to the search box that clears the query text and returns focus to the input, improving UX for repeated searches.
triage.test_hint: Render the search component with non-empty input, verify clear button appears, click it, assert input value is empty and input has focus.

body:
## Summary

For the filters section, instead of asking for a fixed date or fixed person, make it customized to the time period or whoever is logged in. 

This allows for pages of boards to be created to view outstanding issues due in a short period of time and triage tasks relevant to the person that is looking at the board 

## How important this is to me and why
There are 3 use cases that I can outline. In my view, this feature would greatly reduce confusion and increase the usability of the boards. Because of my current project setup, the number of people and the tasks on the board are too difficult to consume. Not everyone is well versed in changing the filtering to their liking, and once they do, it changes for everyone. Creating views per person is not practical and the boards are unusable without selective filtering. 

Importance: High

Use cases:
1. Filter down large boards to cards relevant to the user 
2. Create dynamic views based on date 
3. When dynamic filters are built, this will allow filters to be present for all users
 

## Additional context/similar features

Any examples of good implementations of this capability.
title: Feature Idea: Dynamic Filters

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring significant design decisions around dynamic/contextual filter evaluation (current user, relative dates). Needs product and UX input before implementation.
triage.summary: Add dynamic filter options such as 'current user' for person fields and relative date ranges (today, this week, etc.) for date fields, so board views can be shared without requiring per-user manual filter adjustments.
triage.test_hint: Verify that a filter set to 'current user' resolves to the logged-in user's ID at render time and updates correctly on login change; verify relative date filters (e.g. 'next 7 days') evaluate against current date on each board load.

4. See error:

title: Bug: Application crash when following steps outlined in the documentation "Personal server setup guide".

triage.skip_reason: Requires environment setup and dependency verification; may also involve documentation update decision
triage.summary: Server crashes on startup with 'unknown driver sqlite3' error when following personal server setup guide on Windows; likely missing CGO/sqlite3 build tag or driver import in the dev build configuration

body: - I have been looking at Mattermost for similar issues, but I could not find one myself. - It does not involve security issues - I pretty new to this type of stuff, so try to forgive me the stupid questions I will ask :) #### Summary After updating `Mattermost/mattermost-team-edition:release-7.11` to `Mattermost/mattermost-team-edition:release-9.4` suddenly Mattermost Boards is acting weird. Team boards are dissapearing in one account and they are still available in an other account. And downgrading does not resolve the issue (Or I am not downgrading in the correct way) #### Steps to reproduce `Mattermost/mattermost-team-edition:release-9.4` `postgres:15` 1. Log:`The database was created using collation version 2.31, but the operating system provides version 2.36.` 2. I solved this message, by export and build a new database with a new version. #### Expected behavior Assuming it was a DB issue: after building a new database, I expected that this issues was not happening anymore Assuming it is a Mattermost update issue: After downgrading it should be resolved. #### Observed behavior (that appears unintentional) What did you see happen? Please include relevant error messages and/or screenshots. All boards started moving to the category `boards` and then they started to disappear Suddenly Boards is not a `plugin` anymore, but a `product` #### Possible fixes I really don't know where to look. title: Mattermost Boards is acting abnormal after update to 9.4

triage.skip_reason: No clear repro steps, involves major version migration (7.11 to 9.4), plugin-to-product architecture change, possible data loss symptoms, and insufficient log details to diagnose root cause
triage.summary: After upgrading Mattermost from 7.11 to 9.4, Boards boards disappear or move to default category for some users; Boards transitioned from plugin to product architecture may be causing data visibility issues post-migration
triage.test_hint: Verify board migration scripts run correctly when upgrading across the plugin-to-product boundary; check user-specific board category assignments in DB after migration

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

Run

`docker run -it -v <local path>:/opt/focalboard/data -p 3000:8000 --restart unless-stopped mattermost/focalboard`
A local path, for ex, /home/xelab04/fbdata is used instead of a docker volume

## Expected behavior

The database and all required data is intialised, using the local path as persistent storage. Focalboard initialises correctly if a volume is specified for mounting instead of a path.

## Screenshots (optional)
![image](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/assets/61658620/ab5aa1c0-740c-4596-8f99-394130592874)

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: OpenSUSE server
 - Version: OpenSUSE tumbleweed, Focalboard version latest

## Summary

Emphasizing the use of websockets in the Docker Personal Server documentation could prevent issues in the future.

## Link to documentation page

[Docket setup guide](https://www.focalboard.com/download/personal-edition/docker/)

## (Optional) Additional context and/or screenshot

Add additional context and/or a screenshot of the product feature you'd like explained in documentation.

I am a bit new to self hosting, and am running the Personal Server with NGINX Proxy Manager. I didn't enable websockets support which [caused issues](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/2183#issuecomment-1831272394).

The Ubuntu setup guide mentions that websockets are used, but those setting up with Docker would likely not see this.

body:
I'm pretty sure that this is a shortcoming of the current documentation<s>, although there is a chance that it's a bug</s>.

I'm using Focalboard as a Mattermost plugin and have just recently started playing with the API.

It took me a while to get it going as the docs are a bit suboptimal (I want to complain, but I work in open source too and I get that there are only so many hours in the day).

FWIW these 2 issues are essential reading for getting started with the Focalboard Mattermost plugin API IMO:
- https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/1116
- https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/4420

Plus obviously the actual API docs:
https://htmlpreview.github.io/?https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/blob/main/server/swagger/docs/html/index.html#api-Default-getCards

On face value it all appeared to be working well. Then I noticed that there were some cards missing! When I dug in a little deeper, I realised that it was only returning 100 results<s>?!</s>.

I note that the docs (at least for `cards`) show the suffixes `?page=56&per_page=56`

So I tried checking with `?page=1` and that shows <s>one of</s> the missing cards, so on the right track. Then I tried with `?page=2` and got no results<s>?!</s> I also tried with `?page=0` and that just gave me the same 100 results I was getting initially (so <s>I assume that</s> **with no suffix it defaults to `?page=0&per_page=100`**!<s>?</s>).

After a bit of trial and error, I ended up getting all the cards via a `?page=0&per_page=200` suffix (I have less than 200 cards). So whilst I'm now unstuck, I do have some remaining questions:

- Why does `?page=2` give no results?! Shouldn't whatever doesn't fit on page 1 spill over to page 2 and so on?
  - To answer my own question: Yes. `page=0` is the first page of results, `page=1` is the second page, `page=2` returns no results as I have less than 200 cards!
- <s>How do I ensure that I'm getting all cards (without just increasing `per_page` until it gives no extra results)? Is there a way to page through all results?</s>
- And/or is there a way to force the API to return all results (i.e. turn off pagination)?
title: Doc: How to properly use API pagination?

triage.summary: API pagination docs are unclear: page is 0-indexed, default is page=0&per_page=100, and this is not explained in the Swagger docs. Users are confused and missing data as a result.
triage.test_hint: Verify Swagger/API docs for getCards clearly document that pagination is 0-indexed, the default per_page is 100, and provide an example of iterating all pages.

title: Bug: Cannot find module './en-us'

triage.skip_reason: No repro details for the missing module root cause; likely a bundling or i18n dynamic import issue requiring investigation of webpack/rollup config and locale loading logic
triage.summary: Opening a card/task on a board causes a crash with 'Cannot find module ./en-us', redirecting to a blank page. Appears to be a dynamic locale import failure during card detail rendering.
triage.test_hint: Reproduce by starting Focalboard via Docker, creating an account, opening a template board, and clicking any card. Check browser console for module resolution errors related to locale imports and verify the en-us locale bundle is included in the build output.

body:
#### Summary

Eg. I want to sort possible statuses for a task:
![Screenshot 2023-10-31 at 12 20 22](https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/assets/7556217/6ab9d903-0f4a-460c-8815-4f960a3c1fc8)

I can't move newly created "PAUSED" state to be between "TO DO" and "NEXT UP" states

#### Steps to reproduce
- Create new status
- Try to put it before existing

#### Expected behavior

- Any option to reorder dropdown list

#### Observed behavior (that appears unintentional) 

- Drag and Drop not work
- Switching board to `Group By: Status` and reordering columns also makes no effect on dropdown list

#### Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Desktop / Personal Server / Mattermost Boards (plugin)
 - Version: v7.11.3
 - Browser and OS: OSX
title: Enhancement: Can not sort options in select dropdown

triage.skip_reason: UI interaction decision required for drag-and-drop reordering of select options; involves both frontend and backend changes
triage.summary: Users cannot reorder options in select/multi-select dropdown fields. Drag-and-drop does not work and reordering columns in board view has no effect on the dropdown option order.
triage.test_hint: Verify that select options can be reordered via drag-and-drop in the property editor, and that the new order persists and is reflected in all views.

Can not swap columns when grouping by Person is enabled

body:
## Summary

Hello, and thank you for amazing project!
There is one feature missing that is blocking me to start using it:


Sub-groups, the same way as notion do:
![picture](https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/assets/7556217/be0c1478-0ef8-4ce3-b83f-40884ab57cbb)

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: High

Use cases:
Personally I use boards to track processes in my team.
I have a single board with my team members and category of task:

```
           +-------+-------+-------+
           |member1|member2|member3|
    Inbox  +=======+=======+=======+
           | task4 |       |       |
Unplanned  +-------+-------+-------+
           |       |       | task5 |
           |       |       | task7 |
     ASAP  +-------+-------+-------+
           | task1 | task2 |       |
           |       | task3 |       |
  Planned  +-------+-------+-------+
           | task8 |       | task6 |
           | task9 |       |       |
           | task0 |       |       |
           +-------+-------+-------+
```

We have daily meetings to track the process for each task

## Additional context/similar features

Read the notion announce: 
- https://www.notion.so/releases/2021-10-19

Thanks!
title: Feature Idea: sub-groups

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring significant UI and data model changes to support nested/sub-grouping in board views; requires product design decisions.
triage.summary: Request to support sub-groups in board views, similar to Notion's multi-dimensional grouping feature, enabling a 2D matrix layout grouped by two properties simultaneously.

title: Bug: App starts but window doesn't render

triage.skip_reason: Rendering failure tied to NVIDIA GBM/DRM driver interaction on Linux; requires environment-specific testing and likely an Electron/WebKit rendering flag fix or bundled library update — no clear minimal code change
triage.summary: App window renders blank on Linux with NVIDIA drivers due to GBM buffer allocation failures (nv_gbm_bo_create / DRM_IOCTL_NVIDIA_GEM_ALLOC_NVKMS_MEMORY errors) in the bundled Electron/WebKit layer

triage.summary: Board group colors appear too bright on initial load when using a custom Mattermost theme. Re-saving the theme fixes the issue until Boards is reopened, suggesting theme variables are not applied correctly on first render.
triage.test_hint: Open Boards with a custom dark theme applied, verify board group label/background colors match the expected theme colors without requiring a theme re-save.

triage.summary: Panic (slice bounds out of range) in retrieveFileIDFromBlockFieldStorage when deleting a board that contains blocks with malformed/missing file attachment data imported via importTrello. body:
## Summary

I wanted to install Focalboard on my Oracle Cloud Free Tier VM (which has an arm64 cpu) to use it with my team but in the tags information on Docker Hub I've seen that the provided docker image is just for amd64 architectures.
However, from Issue #124 of this repository I understand that now also ARM64 architectures are supported.
Therefore, I would like to know how could I spin up Focalboard on an arm machine and, since this kind of machines is getting quite popular among servers, I think it would be useful to add those informations to the install instructions of the project (which at the moment are not that exhaustive, for example not reporting how to have persistent storage of Focalboard container data either)

## Link to documentation page
https://www.focalboard.com/download/personal-edition/docker/
title: Doc: Add documentation on how to run Focalboard with docker (or docker-compose) on ARM64 machines

triage.summary: Add documentation on running Focalboard with Docker/docker-compose on ARM64 machines, including multi-arch image usage and persistent storage configuration.
triage.test_hint: Verify the documented docker run / docker-compose commands work on an ARM64 host (e.g. Oracle Cloud Ampere or Raspberry Pi 4) and that persistent volume mounts retain data across container restarts.

body:
## Summary

When working with a Personal Desktop on windows I see a Port listed in my application window header. I have confirmed that if I go to my http://localhost:{port} I do reach a login page for focalboard but cannot create a user or sign in with any default credentials that I can find. I see in the files unzipped from the raw download there is a config.json that has port 8088 defined which differs from the port in the header and is also non-functioning by default. What is the purpose of that file?

What I would like to do is have the ability to load the tool on a machine and share the webview URL where the board could be reviewed perhaps over a team meeting live to make updates. This ideal scenario would be better if it were possible to use local tag-like Assignees instead of different account holders if its not possible to create accounts on the local/webview interfaces. 

If this is not possible; could some comment or documentation be added regarding the webview's access from the personal desktop application and its non-functionality? Something that if someone else discovers in the future they can confirm is desired behavior vs a bug.

As a final note: I am unable to use the server edition of your software as only the personal desktop is accessible FOSS for internal usage. 

## Link to documentation page
N/A

## (Optional) Additional context and/or screenshot

Add additional context and/or a screenshot of the product feature you'd like explained in documentation.
![image](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/assets/70120966/286cb050-1cf3-497b-8265-40d40c5325b0)

triage.test_hint: Verify that updated docs accurately describe the personal desktop webview behavior, the config.json port setting, and limitations around multi-user/assignee support without a server edition.

## Summary

Right now notifications for cards that a user follows seem to ignore any notes made unless the user is tagged. After an explicit follow, this is not what I expect, as by indicating follow I'm interested in any change to a card.
Alternatively, it could be a toggle connected to the follow action that asks for notification of any changes compared to all the ones it notifies for now.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:

## Additional context/similar features

-
title: Enhancement: Notifications to any change of a card

triage.test_hint: Verify that following a card triggers notifications for all card mutations including comment additions, property changes, etc., without requiring an @mention.

body:
## Summary

I am currently in the process of moving from Asana to Focalboard. One feature that I know I'm going to miss is the custom rule sets and automation. One key missing feature is the moving tasks (or change status) based on the date. If the Due Date is coming up, there should be an option that allows you to change the status.

## How important this is to me and why
This will allow you to use this tool more of a to-do list as well. It will effectively force you to look at the task as it will move the task in front of your eyes.

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. I know I need to do Errand A on Monday, When Monday comes, it would be great if this task will go to the top of my table.

## Steps to reproduce the behavior

- I'm running stand-alone Focalboard 7.10.4 in Docker (left screenshot)
- I spun up a Mattermost instance in Docker with Boards 7.11.2 (right screenshot)
- I exported my board from Focalboard to a `boardarchive` file
- When importing the `boardarchive` to Mattermost, all of the timestamps are wrong
- Also, the order of the items in the columns is lost (unsure if this is related to the timestamp changes)

Below you can see the left (Focalboard) has the correct time of 10 months ago, while the right (Mattermost import) has the incorrect time of 12 days ago
![Screenshot_20230829_115834](https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/assets/4955337/52bb70e5-7724-4423-9202-bfc4ba26abc0)

Here is the JSON from the Focalboard export for that entry.
```
{"type":"block","data":{"id":"ayiaef857sjy1tb5psu3hkp9pka","parentId":"cukrxnfw8n3dmjnbgjc1n3uz3wa","createdBy":"u1b6q77ixji8mtg84agcfbfp6ew","modifiedBy":"u1b6q77ixji8mtg84agcfbfp6ew","schema":1,"type":"comment","title":"I think this is done, but Ansible playbooks will need to be redone to ensure it's done for the correct user","fields":{},"createAt":1667328548338,"updateAt":1667328548338,"deleteAt":0,"boardId":"bfy769tekmtgw9gcuzc8b5cqsne"}}
```

You can see the timestamp is `1667328548338`, which was 10 months ago (the correct time). I'm unsure how Mattermost is getting 12 days ago...
![Screenshot_20230829_120249](https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/assets/4955337/be1cca08-4b67-4c92-a29d-758fa965777b)

Here is a different example where the time on the import was updated to the current time...

![Screenshot_20230829_120757](https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/assets/4955337/ce94ab9a-defd-4ffc-8cb5-0f0fdf62fa6b)

...even though the timestamp is `1681324659924` (5 months ago) 

```
{"type":"block","data":{"id":"aent5ct6fjid678h7irtu74e7ry","parentId":"cu56gdg5dnbdumqa4ntkijn4fra","createdBy":"u1b6q77ixji8mtg84agcfbfp6ew","modifiedBy":"u1b6q77ixji8mtg84agcfbfp6ew","schema":1,"type":"comment","title":"https://docs.netgate.com/pfsense/en/latest/recipes/dns-over-tls.html#enable-dns-over-tls-server-optional","fields":{},"createAt":1681324659924,"updateAt":1681324659924,"deleteAt":0,"boardId":"bfy769tekmtgw9gcuzc8b5cqsne"}}
```

## Expected behavior

I think I should be given the option to either preserve timestamps, or completely overwrite them with the current timestamps.

## Screenshots (optional)

See above...

## Edition and Platform

See above...

## Additional context (optional)

See above...

body:
After updating Mattermost (Team Edition) from 7.1.5 (ESR) to 7.8.2 (ESR) or from 7.8.2 to 7.8.9 (ESR) the following error occurs on startup. Running `journalctl -u mattermost -r` gives the following output:
> Aug 20 14:21:54 mattermost mattermost[1370]: {"timestamp":"2023-08-20 14:21:54.498 +02:00","level":"error","msg":"Failed to sync plugin from file store","caller":"app/plugin.go:366","bundle":"plugins/focalboard.tar.gz","error":"installExtractedPlugin: Unable to restart plugin on upgrade., error initializing the DB: Error 1054: Unknown column 'dirty' in 'field list' in line 0: SELECT version, dirty FROM `focalboard_schema_migrations` LIMIT 1"}
> 
> Aug 20 14:21:54 mattermost mattermost[1370]: {"timestamp":"2023-08-20 14:21:54.478 +02:00","level":"error","msg":"Table creation / migration failed","caller":"app/plugin_api.go:976","plugin_id":"focalboard","error":"\"Error 1054: Unknown column 'dirty' in 'field list' in line 0: SELECT version, dirty FROM `focalboard_schema_migrations` LIMIT 1\""}


## Steps to reproduce the behavior

Update Mattermost (Team Edition) from 7.1.5 (ESR) to 7.8.2 (ESR) or 7.8.9 (ESR):
```
sudo su
systemctl stop mattermost

# Backup
cd /opt
rm -rf mattermost-back* mattermost-upgrade
cp -ra mattermost/ mattermost-back-$(date +'%F-%H-%M')/

# Upgrade
wget https://releases.mattermost.com/7.8.9/mattermost-team-7.8.9-linux-amd64.tar.gz
tar -xf mattermost*.gz --transform='s,^[^/]\+,\0-upgrade,'

find mattermost/ mattermost/client/ -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 \! \( -type d \( -path mattermost/client -o -path mattermost/client/plugins -o -path mattermost/config -o -path mattermost/logs -o -path mattermost/plugins -o -path mattermost/data \) -prune \) | sort | sudo xargs rm -r

cp -an mattermost-upgrade/. mattermost/
chown -R mattermost:mattermost mattermost
setcap cap_net_bind_service=+ep ./mattermost/bin/mattermost

# Restart service
systemctl start mattermost

# View log
journalctl -u mattermost -r
```

## Expected behavior
Mattermost should start without errors.

## Edition and Platform
 - Mattermost Boards (plugin)
 - Version: 7.8.2
 - Server OS: Ubuntu 20.04
title: Bug: Upgrading leads to error in focalboard plugin: Error 1054: Unknown column 'dirty' in 'field list' in line 0

triage.skip_reason: Database migration issue requiring careful schema analysis and testing across upgrade paths; risk of data-loss if handled incorrectly
triage.summary: MySQL migration fails on upgrade due to missing 'dirty' column in focalboard_schema_migrations table. The schema_migrations table appears to be missing the 'dirty' column expected by the migrate library, likely caused by an incomplete or incompatible prior migration state when upgrading from older Mattermost/Focalboard versions (e.g. 7.1.5 ESR to 7.8.x ESR).
triage.test_hint: Test upgrade path from Mattermost 7.1.5 ESR to 7.8.2/7.8.9 ESR on MySQL; verify focalboard_schema_migrations table schema before and after upgrade; check migration initialization code to handle missing 'dirty' column gracefully.

body:
[Copied from https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/issues/24148]

#### Summary
Changed date property not reflected in board card

#### Steps to reproduce
1. Create a board.
2. Open a new card for editing.
3. Add a custom date property to the card (and therefore to all cards on the board).
4. Set the value of the custom date property on the new card and close it.
5. Click "Properties" and enable the display of the new property on the board.
6. Open the card back up, change the value of the date property, and close the card.

#### Expected behavior
I expect to see the new value I just entered for the date property to be displayed on the card on the board display.

#### Observed behavior (that appears unintentional) 
The old value of the date field is still displayed on the card on the board display.

The two ways I've found to get the new value to display are:

* quit and restart the app
* disable and re-enable the display of the date property on the board then the displayed value is updated

### But wait there's more

Two other incorrect behaviors I'm observing with date properties which there's a good chance are related to the one described above so I'm adding them here instead of creating new issues:

1. As above, set up a board with a date property which is displayed on cards.
2. Create a new card, set the date property when creating it, and close it.
3. Observe that the value of the date property isn't displayed on the card.
4. If you disable and re-enable displaying the date property on the board, the value shows up.
5. Moving on, drag and drop the card to a different column.
6. Observe that the value of the date property is _erased_. It's not just not displayed on the board; if you open the card you will see that it is _gone_.

Regarding the last observation above, in previous versions I've seen a date property revert to a previous value when a card is moved to a new column, so that's also something to watch out for when fixing these issues.
title: Bug: Changed date property not reflected in board card in macOS app

triage.skip_reason: Multiple interrelated UI/state bugs involving date property display and potential data-loss on drag-and-drop; requires reproduction and investigation across desktop and frontend layers
triage.summary: Date property changes not reflected on board card in macOS app; stale display after edit, missing value on new card, and date value erased on drag-and-drop to new column.
triage.test_hint: Test date property update propagation after card close, verify card display refresh without toggling property visibility, and confirm date value persistence after drag-and-drop between columns.

body:
Hi,

I have been using Focalboard for a year now. I usually login in every few months to update some things. Watchtower is automatically updating the Docker container of Focalboard. However, today I encountered an unexpected issue - I am unable to log in anymore. 

To resolve this, I followed the steps outlined in the "reset password" section of the Focalboard guide (https://www.focalboard.com/guide/admin/). Since the Docker container doesn't have a text editor, I had to install nano and curl. Curl is required for the reset script.

After installing nano and curl, I executed the following script:

bash reset-password.sh name temporarypassword
(Note: I replaced the actual name with 'name' here)

The output I received was:
{"error":"internal server error","errorCode":500} ...

The container's output while trying to reset the password states:

"user not found (name)" "API ERROR." Why has my user disappeared? Is it possible to check which users exist in the db?

body:
## Summary

Within a playbook run, when a tag is used in any of :
1. Checklist title
2. Task title
3. Task description

Users will be able to click the tag to see tagged messages in right sidebar

## How important this is to me and why
One of the shortcomings of playbook runs is the inability to trace (both ways) between checklists/tasks and related messages in an associated channel. Enabling tags would be a "low-hanging-fruit" way to allow playbook run participants to jump to messages related to a checklist/task with one click to get up-to-speed on the related discussion.

Why aren't threads enough? 
1. Threads are easily broken if playbook run participants fail to use Reply even once. And reconnecting messages to a thread is difficult. Tags would make it much easier to bring messages under a checklist/task for easy reference.
2. Connecting a thread to a task can occur through a message link in the task description, but it requires creation of a message for the head of the thread that contains some textual reference to the checklist/task for context (e.g. I've used numbering schemes on checklists+tasks to provide reference IDs). Getting to messages associated with the checklist/task is a two-step process: click on thread head message link and then click on message or replies icon to see thread. Using tags allows users to get to messages related to checklist/task in one click, and would not require creation of a thread head message, so checklist/task tags could be set in the playbook itself, or at least before any messages related to the checklist/task are created.

Importance: High

Use cases:
1. Using playbook runs to structure discussions and provide two-way links between messages and related checklists/tasks.
2. 
6.

## Additional context/similar features
* Boeing is using self-hosted Team(?) version 7.5.2, I believe
* I would prefer Mattermost add a more sophisticated way to connect messages to playbook checklists/tasks. E.g. native checklist/task linking support (similar to channels, messages, boards, cards). From a checklist/task, I'd like to be able to "See checklist/task thread", "Reply to task" to add to task-related thread. From a message, I'd like to be about to "Mark outcome/decision" as a way to flag the message as the "outcome/decision" related to a task.

Any examples of good implementations of this capability.
title: Feature Idea: make tags work in playbook checklists

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring deep integration between playbooks and messaging/tagging systems; needs product design and UI decisions
triage.summary: Allow hashtags/message-tags in playbook checklist titles, task titles, and task descriptions to be clickable, showing tagged messages in the right sidebar for traceability between tasks and channel discussions.

body:
## Overview

I'm running Focalboard in Kubernetes. My database is a separate Postgres 13 VM that uses a Let's Encrypt SSL certificate (setup like [this](https://www.postgresql.org/docs/13/ssl-tcp.html#SSL-SERVER-FILES)).

I was stuck on Focalboard `7.5.2` for a while because of [this](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/4651) bug.  I just tried to upgrade to `7.8.4` and was met with the error below. This occurs on `7.9.3` also.



```
2023/04/07 18:09:59 readConfigFile
2023/04/07 18:09:59 {ServerRoot:http://localhost:8000 Port:8000 DBType:postgres DBConfigString:postgres://username:password@db05.internal.mydomain.com:5432/dbfocalboard?sslmode=verify-full&connect_timeout=10 DBTablePrefix: UseSSL:false SecureCookie:false WebPath:./pack FilesDriver:local FilesS3Config:{AccessKeyID: SecretAccessKey: Bucket: PathPrefix: Region: Endpoint: SSL:false SignV2:false SSE:false Trace:false Timeout:0} FilesPath:/data/files MaxFileSize:0 Telemetry:true TelemetryID: PrometheusAddress: WebhookUpdate:[] Secret: SessionExpireTime:2592000 SessionRefreshTime:18000 LocalOnly:false EnableLocalMode:true LocalModeSocketLocation:/var/tmp/focalboard_local.socket EnablePublicSharedBoards:false FeatureFlags:map[] EnableDataRetention:false DataRetentionDays:0 TeammateNameDisplay:username ShowEmailAddress:false ShowFullName:false AuthMode:native LoggingCfgFile: LoggingCfgJSON: AuditCfgFile: AuditCfgJSON: NotifyFreqCardSeconds:0 NotifyFreqBoardSeconds:0}
info  [2023-04-07 18:09:59.677 Z] Focalboard server                        caller="model/version.go:60" version=7.8.4 edition=linux build_number=dev build_date=n/a build_hash=6fc1d9f8db9d366a16cd0ce18d34a5d7b8d5b1e5
error [2023-04-07 18:09:59.684 Z] Database Ping failed                     caller="server/server.go:220" error="x509: certificate signed by unknown authority"
fatal [2023-04-07 18:09:59.684 Z] server.NewStore ERROR                    caller="main/main.go:136" error="x509: certificate signed by unknown authority"
  main.main
      /go/src/focalboard/server/main/main.go:136
  runtime.main
      /usr/local/go/src/runtime/proc.go:250
  runtime.goexit
      /usr/local/go/src/runtime/asm_amd64.s:1571

```

I had to change `sslmode=verify-full` to `sslmode=require` to get Focalboard to start.  From [this](https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/lib/pq#hdr-Connection_String_Parameters) page for the Go Postgres driver:

> Valid values for sslmode are:
>  *  disable - No SSL
>  *  require - Always SSL (skip verification)
>  *  verify-ca - Always SSL (verify that the certificate presented by the server was signed by a trusted CA)
>  *  verify-full - Always SSL (verify that the certification presented by the server was signed by a trusted CA and the server host name matches the one in the certificate)

It is now working with `7.8.4` and `7.9.3`. Did something change with the included package of `ca-certificates` in the Docker image? The SSL cert I'm using is signed by Let's Encrypt, so it should be accepted everywhere...


## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Run Postgres in a separate VM using a Let's Encrypt SSL certificate
2. Try to run Focalboard `7.8.4` in Docker using `sslmode=verify-full`  in the Postgres connection string
3. Error

## Expected behavior

Focalboard should accept CA certificates from Let's Encrypt.

## Screenshots (optional)

N/A

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Server
 - Version: 7.8.4
 - Browser and OS: Firefox+Chrome on Linux (Kubernetes)

## Additional context (optional)

N/A
title: Bug: `x509: certificate signed by unknown authority`

triage.skip_reason: Requires understanding of TLS/certificate chain handling in database connection setup; environment-specific (Kubernetes + Let's Encrypt + Postgres SSL) and touches security-sensitive DB connection code; needs careful testing and possibly exposing a config option for custom CA certs
triage.summary: Focalboard fails to connect to Postgres when using SSL with a Let's Encrypt certificate, logging 'x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'. The Go runtime's default cert pool may not include the Let's Encrypt intermediate CA, or the DB connection logic does not load the system cert pool correctly when sslmode=verify-full is used.
triage.test_hint: Start Focalboard with a Postgres DSN using sslmode=verify-full pointing to a server with a Let's Encrypt cert; verify connection succeeds. Also test with a custom sslrootcert param in the DSN pointing to the Let's Encrypt CA chain file.

body:
## Summary

I'm just starting using Mattermost Board (Focalboard in Matterrmost) in one of the companies. I'd like to keep all my workflow with this program, but I see a potential issue for the future:
My workflow is not a single project, but contiguous. I won't close it soon, but I plan to work with it for years. So after some time I got dozens of closed tasks (visible in `Done` column), and after a year there will be hundreds.
I'd like to have a function to archive `Done` tasks, not to include them in current view, but have them in history.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

## Additional context/similar features

So far, a kind of "override" is to create a column (e.g. `Archived`), and hide it.

In other programs, the function is implemented:
- Jira: "close" issue
- Wekan, Kanboard: archive task / card
title: Feature Idea: Possibility to archive done tasks

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring UI design decisions, new data model for archiving, and filtering logic; not suitable for auto-attempt
triage.summary: Users want the ability to archive completed/done tasks so they are hidden from the active board view but preserved in history, similar to Jira close or Wekan/Kanboard archive functionality.
triage.test_hint: Verify archived cards are hidden from default board/gallery/table views, verify archived cards are accessible via a dedicated archive view or filter toggle, verify archiving and unarchiving round-trips preserve all card data.

title: Bug (MM Boards Plugin): deleting specific property breaks boards

triage.skip_reason: No reproducible steps identifying which property type triggers the crash; requires server-side investigation on a production instance; plugin process exit (exit status 2) suggests a backend panic or fatal error that needs log analysis and likely a targeted fix

User confirmed it is reproducible by restoring from backup and repeating the deletion. Suspected cause may relate to duplicate card_property IDs shared across templates in PostgreSQL.
triage.test_hint: Add integration test that deletes each property type (text, number, select, multi-select, date, person, file, checkbox, url, email, phone, created-time, last-updated-time, created-by, last-updated-by, relation) from a board with template-derived cards and verifies the plugin remains stable; add a recovery/panic handler around property deletion RPC path and check for nil-dereference on property schema update propagation.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Set the theme to dark theme (not an issue in light/default theme)
2. Open a board where cards use the `Assignee` field
3. Select/create a card and note the text in the `Assignee` field matches the background color

## Expected behavior

In the dark theme, most text should inherit its color using the CSS variable `--center-channel-color-rgb: 220, 220, 220;`. However, this does not seem to be the case for the Assignee field. In my testing, the text in this field is visible in lighter themes. It appears as if the RGB value of `51,51,51` is hardcoded in the styling for that item rather than using the theme variable.

I tried hard reloading with a clean cache, new browsers, and incognito windows, and the issue persisted.

body:
## Summary

Allow for configuration option, where the items in the board cannot be manually removed.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Users cannot tamper and clear items from existing board.
2. The board will be persistent, and the items can be archived, so they are no longer obstructing view, but they will still be there, in case we want to check some discussion, or content below them.
3. `Trust the user, but assume that user wants to harm you` approach.

## Additional context/similar features

Items in Jira cannot be removed, but can be hidden, and found in the future.

triage.test_hint: Run fix-collations migration against a MySQL 8.0.32 instance and verify the charset query handles empty result sets gracefully; add a fallback or nil-check when no rows are returned from charset fetch query.

## Summary

Either update the URL property or create a new Hyperlink property that contains link text and target URL. Ex: `[test link](https://testlink.com)`

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium 

Use cases:
1. Easily display similar/long URLs in various views. Ticket systems usually have identical URLs that only differ in an ending number. Displaying the number as a link is simpler than the entire link.

## Additional context/similar features

Capability exists in card Description, but can't be displayed in all views and isn't the appropriate location for this data.

triage.test_hint: Verify hyperlink property renders display text as clickable link in all views (board, table, gallery), stores both label and URL, and falls back gracefully when only URL is provided.

> {"timestamp":"2023-01-26 05:14:41.199 Z","level":"error","msg":"Error 1062: Duplicate entry 'c3x4b75znn7nnuy3smzt6xoik9a-0000-00-00 00:00:00' for key 'PRIMARY'","caller":"app/plugin_api.go:976","plugin_id":"focalboard"}

Able to edit cards in Boards.

 - Version: v7.5.1

title: Bug: Unable to edit cards in Boards

triage.skip_reason: No reproduction steps beyond the error log; requires DB schema investigation and understanding of why duplicate primary key entries with zero-date timestamps are being generated. Needs human triage.
triage.summary: MySQL duplicate primary key error 'c3x4b75znn7nnuy3smzt6xoik9a-0000-00-00 00:00:00' prevents any card edits (title changes, moves, etc.) on a board, returning HTTP 500. Root cause appears to be a block record with a zero/null delete_at timestamp being inserted or upserted in a way that collides with an existing PRIMARY key.
triage.test_hint: Reproduce by finding a board with a block whose delete_at is '0000-00-00 00:00:00' in MySQL and attempting to update it via PATCH /api/v2/boards/{boardID}/blocks/{blockID}; verify the upsert SQL handles zero-date primary key collisions correctly.

body:
## Summary

I'm implementing Focalboard across my company, and we created our own templates. We don't plan on ever using the stock/example templates that come with it, so it's quite an inconvenient the fact that all our templates are positioned further down the list and we have to scroll every time we create a new board.

If we were able to either delete/hide standard templates or just rearrange them to be listed further down when compared to our custom ones, UX would be greatly improved.

Furthermore, most of our custom templates strongly inherit from the standard ones, so having the option to edit them as well wouldn't be bad at all.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance:Medium

This is, objectively, a UX improvement that can save an organization considerable time depending on how large their team is.

Use cases:
1. User wants a cleaner template selection UI.
2. User will never use Focalboard's standard templates.
3. User wants to customize standard templates to their own cases/realities.

## Additional context/similar features

Looking very briefly at the code, this functionality [Editing/Deleting] would be very similar to the one already present on custom boards. Reordering, on the other hand, would be a different implementation, but definitely not something complex.
title: Feature Idea: Edit/Reorder/Delete Stock Templates

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX decisions required; involves template management policy and potentially backend changes for reordering/deletion of stock templates
triage.summary: Allow users to edit, reorder, or hide/delete built-in stock templates so custom templates can be surfaced higher in the list and stock templates can be customized or removed from view.
triage.test_hint: Verify custom templates appear before stock templates when reordered; verify stock templates can be hidden/deleted per user or org; verify edited stock templates persist correctly.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Create a new card. Add some properties to it and set their values. Add some comments in there as well. Do not set the card title.
2. Go back to kanban view and try deleting the card.
3. Notice the UI didn't show any delete confirmation for this card.
4. Create a new card and repeat step 1 but this time also set a card title.
5. Go back to kanban view and try deleting the card. Notice that this time you see a delete confirmation.

## Expected behavior

Card delete confirmation should show up if there is any data in the card.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Mattermost Boards (plugin)
 - Version: v7.8.0
 - Browser and OS: Firefox on MacOS

## Additional context

The log in webapp is probably if the card has no title and no content, don't show the delete confirmation dialogue. It does not take into consideration the properties and comments in the card.
title: Bug: No card delete confirmation status for some cards

triage.summary: Card delete confirmation dialog is skipped when a card has no title, even if it has properties or comments. The condition for showing the confirmation only checks title and content, ignoring properties and comments.
triage.test_hint: Check the delete confirmation logic in the card/kanban frontend code. The condition should also evaluate whether the card has any non-empty properties or comments before skipping the confirmation dialog. Add unit tests covering cards with no title but with properties and/or comments.

## Summary

With #124 the Dockerfile can now also be used to build arm64 packages. However, the public releases only include builds for amd64 (`focalboard-server-linux-amd64.tar.gz`). It would be great if arm64 binaries could be included, too. This architecture is becoming more and more popular so providing builds could further contribute to Focalboard's reach.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: High (my server runs on arm64 and this prevents me from using Focalboard through e.g. Yunohost)

Use cases:
1. Install Focalboard through Yunohost (https://github.com/YunoHost-Apps/focalboard_ynh/issues/51)

## Additional context/similar features

None.
title: Feature Idea: Release arm server builds

triage.skip_reason: Requires CI/CD pipeline changes and release infrastructure decisions beyond code edits
triage.summary: Add arm64 server builds to the public release artifacts, leveraging existing Dockerfile arm64 support from #124

body:
## Summary

The current UI on the Boards share dialog confuses users and is inconsistent with the behavior of adding explicit members to the board. The team role behaves as an option to "allow team members to join the board", but the UI can make it seem like you are adding the entire team as explicit members of the board, which is not the case. However, when adding individual board members, you are actually adding them as explicit members of the board.

We should consider improving the team role section to make it clearer that it is an option to **open the board to the team**, but not adding the team to the board.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Users granting their entire team to the board
2. Users adding their entire team to the board

## Additional context/similar features

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/93531870/212103221-d73b54e2-50bd-4a55-b748-af49df07efc7.png)
title: Feature Idea: Share boards team access UI is misleading

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX copy and design decision requires human judgment on wording and layout changes
triage.summary: Share board dialog's team access section is misleading — UI implies entire team is added as explicit members rather than simply opening board access to team members. Needs clearer labeling/copy to distinguish 'open to team' from 'add explicit members'.
triage.test_hint: Verify share dialog clearly differentiates team-level open access from individual explicit member additions; check that tooltip or label text accurately describes the team role behavior.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: High. Currently looking to use the Calendar view more extensively, however limited by this.

Use cases:
1. Meeting time.
2. Error recorded at.
3. Report back by.

triage.test_hint: Verify datetime field can be created, edited, saved, and displayed correctly in board and calendar views; ensure timezone handling is correct.

## Summary

When a change is made in Boards it is reflected in the Project Manager of Jira, and the contrary

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Low

Use cases:
1. share project development with non-Jira users
2. consolidate collaboration in one place (communication and project management) for heavy Jira users (developers)

Boards is redundant/not used because all Jira users have already set up their project within Jira. An integration bidirectional would increase the usage of Boards. I estimate it would increase adoption, offering an opportunity to transition conveniently from Jira to Boards. 
Client discussion: Accenture/Octo

body:
Suggested by QA community contributor @jprusch during Boards v7.7 feature testing

## Summary

New boards are always created under the "Boards" category but this is not always the preferred behavior.  If I have created a new Category and then want to create a board under that category, I'd expect it to be added to that category. I'd like this behavior changed as it makes sense to create the board under the category I'm working in.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium.  There is the "Move to" function where Boards can be moved to another category but this is just an extra step that wastes time and shouldn't be required.

Use cases:
1. Creating boards within the Category I'm working in

## Screenshots

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/13020833/211478810-802a3fa4-d8ca-47c8-9b59-ffc7b6f160a8.png)

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/13020833/211478894-4add6b19-0ed5-4da1-9262-763658ce08e9.png)
title: Feature Idea: Create the board in the Category section you are in, not in the "Boards" category

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX behavior decision requiring frontend state tracking of active category and backend wiring; not trivial enough for auto-attempt
triage.summary: When creating a new board, it should be placed under the currently active/selected category instead of always defaulting to the top-level 'Boards' category. Requires tracking the selected category in sidebar state and passing it as context during board creation.
triage.test_hint: Verify that clicking 'Add board' while a custom category is expanded/selected creates the new board under that category, not under 'Boards'. Also verify default behavior when no custom category is selected still falls back to 'Boards'.

body:
## Summary

Add the ability to zoom in on images that are in the card description.

- I am currently viewing a card description that contains images
- I hover over an image with my mouse
- My mouse cursor changes appearance to indicate that the image is clickable
- I click on the image and it opens either:
  - in a popup
  - in a new tab

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: High

I often add lots of images to my cards when using focalboard as a project management tool instead of using it as a kanban. It is very convenient as a knowledge base and I often add big images that have small text, which therefore can't be read from inside a card popup.

Use cases:
1. Being able to read small text on high dimension images
2. Being able to download an image that has been added into a card description

## Additional context/similar features

Currently, the only way to download/zoom a card description image is to use the user's browser's devtools.

Example :
Click the first image that appears on this page : https://lokeshdhakar.com/projects/lightbox2/
title: Feature Idea: zooming-in images in card description

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX decision required for popup vs new tab behavior and lightbox implementation details
triage.summary: Add image zoom/lightbox functionality for images embedded in card descriptions, allowing users to click images to view them full-size in a popup or new tab
triage.test_hint: Verify clicking an embedded card description image opens a lightbox or new tab, cursor changes to pointer on hover, and image can be downloaded

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior
1. Login to Boards as User A and open a board (i.e., `Board 123`)
2. Go the the Share dialog and add User B to `Board 123` as any role
3. In a separate incognito browser, login as User B and go to `Board 123`
4. Go back to the window with User A and remove User B from the board
5. Go to window with User B and see error - user is taken to the template picker
6. As User A, add User B back to `Board 123`
7. Go to window with User B again and see error - `Board 123` is automatically opened for User B after User A added them back to the board
8. Repeat steps 1-7 on the template editor and see the same issue there as well.

## Expected behavior

body:
## Summary
Sync with Notion.  I really love focalboard, and it's simplicity.  I also love notion and it's note-taking features and database functionality.  They recently introduced database sync for Jira (laughable choice I know) and Github Issues & PR.  It would be amazing if focalboard could sync with Notion's database.   Here's a github example https://github.com/makenotion/notion-sdk-js/tree/main/examples/notion-github-sync.
In my opinion there are no good all in one project mangament platforms (chat, wiki/notes/witeboard, kanban), at best the platforms out there are able to combine two of those (like MM & focalboard), but people are left trying to stitch things together (and is a reason why slack is still so populare despitet zero improvements in years).  While MM is the best chat plattform IMO, focalboard isn't as fully featured as others, and the platform as a whole lacks any good capability/integrations with note-taking/md/wiki tools.
As Notion only has those two integrations for db sync right now, I think it would help spread awareness to users about this project and help people see the light (most people I've spoke to love MM, but are unaware about focalboard).

What the new capability is.
Sync Notion database with focalboard

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: High
I really want to use focalboard, as I love Mattermost and the project as a whole- but it just doesn't have enough features & integratitons compared to the alternatives.  This would change that, focalboard would be tigtly integrated with our chat, and make it easier to keep projects updated.

Use cases:
1. Add extra info/attach documents in Notion
2. Keep projects synced, and your team far more organized
3. Allow (some) users to stay inside mattermost while still staying on the same page

## Additional context/similar features

Any examples of good implementations of this capability.
You can try it at notion.so with github, but here's the code: https://github.com/makenotion/notion-sdk-js/tree/main/examples/notion-github-sync

My major concern is how conflicts happen, both on focalboard's end and notion, considering they integrated it with jira I'm tempted to believe it's possible though.
title: Feature Idea: Sync Boards with Notion

triage.skip_reason: Large scope third-party integration with external platform (Notion); requires API design, auth, sync logic, and ongoing maintenance. UI/product decisions involved.
triage.summary: Feature request to sync Focalboard boards/databases with Notion databases, similar to Notion's existing GitHub and Jira integrations.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Go to the root path of the web server, i.e. localhost:8000
2. See a blank page and an error.

## Expected behavior

I see the welcome screen.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Server
 - Version: 7.4.3
 - Browser and OS: Firefox 68, Ubuntu 20.04

## Symptom

Loading any board causes a runtime exception as follows:

```
TypeError: Intl.Locale is not a constructor
```

## Root cause

This is a vanilla web compatibility bug with older versions of Firefox due to missing API implementations. (I am operating Focalboard under a proprietary, restricted environment where a browser upgrade is not feasible.)

## Proposal

There exists a polyfill for this API: https://formatjs.io/docs/polyfills/intl-locale/

I fixed this issue locally with the following patch:

```diff
diff --git a/webapp/package.json b/webapp/package.json
index 25843393..832b49d7 100644
--- a/webapp/package.json
+++ b/webapp/package.json
@@ -27,6 +27,7 @@
                "@draft-js-plugins/editor": "^4.1.2",
                "@draft-js-plugins/emoji": "^4.6.0",
                "@draft-js-plugins/mention": "^5.1.2",
+               "@formatjs/intl-locale": "^3.0.7",
                "@fullcalendar/core": "^5.10.1",
                "@fullcalendar/daygrid": "^5.10.1",
                "@fullcalendar/interaction": "^5.10.1",
diff --git a/webapp/src/main.tsx b/webapp/src/main.tsx
index dd9a3168..e4a9ee32 100644
--- a/webapp/src/main.tsx
+++ b/webapp/src/main.tsx
@@ -1,5 +1,6 @@
 // Copyright (c) 2015-present Mattermost, Inc. All Rights Reserved.
 // See LICENSE.txt for license information.
+import '@formatjs/intl-locale/polyfill'
 import React from 'react'
 import ReactDOM from 'react-dom'
 import {Provider as ReduxProvider} from 'react-redux'
```

I'd like to propose something like this: it offers improved webcompat with minimal additional code, and with minor bundle size cost.
title: Bug: Invalid instantiation of Intl.Locale from formatjs dependency in Firefox

triage.summary: Firefox <75 crashes with 'Intl.Locale is not a constructor' because formatjs uses Intl.Locale which is absent in older Firefox. Fix is to add @formatjs/intl-locale polyfill package and import it at the app entry point (main.tsx).
triage.test_hint: Verify no blank page on Firefox 68; confirm Intl.Locale polyfill is loaded before any formatjs code; check bundle size delta is acceptable.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Go to the root path of the web server, i.e. localhost:8000
2. See a blank page and an error.

## Expected behavior

I see the welcome screen.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Server
 - Version: 7.4.3
 - Browser and OS: Firefox 68, Ubuntu 20.04

## Symptom

Loading the main page causes a syntax error while parsing the main.js bundle due to an illegal regular expression.

```
SyntaxError: invalid identity escape in regular expression
```

## Root cause

This is due to an illegal regular expression escape used here:

https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/blob/v7.4.3/webapp/src/blocks/boardView.ts#L62

## Proposal

A code search suggests that `sortBoardViewsAlphabetically` is not meaningfully used in the codebase; it only has callsites in unit tests.

It seems that it was previously used in `webapp/src/components/sidebar/sidebarBoardItem.tsx`, however as of aa540e73ce669a1acd491a5edd7d55199ef1b87c, its use was decommissioned and the original utility was not removed.

Removing this function altogether remediates the SyntaxError issue in my local environment. I propose this method and associated unit tests simply be removed.
title: Bug: Invalid regular expression exception for emoji replacement in Firefox

triage.summary: Invalid regex escape in boardView.ts causes SyntaxError in Firefox, resulting in a blank page. The `sortBoardViewsAlphabetically` function is unused dead code; removing it and its unit tests fixes the crash.
triage.test_hint: Delete sortBoardViewsAlphabetically function from webapp/src/blocks/boardView.ts and remove associated unit tests. Verify the app loads in Firefox without SyntaxError.

title: Bug: App doesn't start

body:
## Summary
Board templates are only supported in English currently. If users switch their language settings to a non-English language, the standard templates will always appear in English regardless of the language selected. Board users rely on standard templates to help them learn about the product and to get started quickly. 

For non-native English speakers looking to use Boards, this is problematic because it prevents them from learning and adopting the tool in the language they are most comfortable with. The mixed languages also creates a poor and inconsistent experience while using Boards.

#### What the new capability is:
When a non-English language is selected in the settings menu, display the translated standard templates instead of the English templates. All content on the standard templates should be translated, including:

- Board/template name
- Board description
- View names
- All sample card content


## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Non-native English speakers looking to learn more about Boards using the Welcome template
2. Non-native English speakers creating new boards from any of the standard templates

## Additional context/similar features

See [thread](https://community.mattermost.com/core/pl/1bhz6ufmu7be5kkxgqp7kbjzsw) for reference.
title: Feature Idea: Translated standard Boards templates

triage.skip_reason: Large complexity requiring translation of all template content across multiple languages, board/card data structures, and coordination with translators; involves both backend template serving logic and frontend rendering changes plus significant i18n content work
triage.summary: Board templates are always displayed in English regardless of the user's language setting. All template content (board name, description, view names, card content) should be served in the user's selected language.
triage.test_hint: Switch UI language to a non-English locale, open the templates picker, and verify template names, descriptions, view names, and card content are rendered in the selected language.

body: ## Summary It's not immediately obvious in the Settings > Import submenu that only the `Import archive` option actually opens the file dialog for you to upload a `.boardarchive` file. The other menu options are simply links to specific sections in the docs. I'd like to make it more clear in the UI the purpose that the menu options serve. Perhaps an icon that would help infer to users that a menu option is a _link_? cc @wuwinson @asaadmahmood
title: UI Enhancement: use icons in Settings > Import submenu

triage.test_hint: Verify that the Import submenu renders distinct icons for the archive import option vs. documentation link options, and that clicking each behaves correctly (file dialog vs. browser navigation).

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Go to focalboard
2. Open a card
3. Type a comment
4. Delete a single character with backspace
5. Press ctrl+Z

## Expected behavior

I expect ctrl+Z to restore the character i deleted. Instead, nothing happens.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: the one on the community server, as of today
 - Version: v7.4.2
 - Browser and OS: both on Firefox 105.0.3, and on the Mattermost desktop client. OS is Ubuntu 22.04

## Additional context

This is Sev 3 (minor, non-critical functionality), but it can become a bit frustrating when typing long comments in particular. E.g. something gets deleted by mistake, and it cannot be restored in any way. I am currently typing my comments on a text editor first, then copypasting them into the card, in order to avoid running into this issue again.

A few cases in which ctrl+Z didn't work:
- Type a comment -> delete single character -> press ctrl+Z (it should restore the deleted character, but doesn't)
- Type a comment -> press ctrl+backspace to delete the last word -> press ctrl+Z (it should restore the deleted word, but doesn't)
- Type a comment -> press ctrl+Z (should delete the last word or letter that was typed, but doesn't)

Interestingly, there's also a case in which ctrl+Z worked well:
- Type a comment -> select it all with ctrl+A -> press backspace to delete it -> press ctrl+Z (it successfully restores the full deleted comment, as expected)
title: Bug: ctrl+Z doesn't always undo

triage.skip_reason: Undo behavior in rich text/comment editor requires understanding of editor framework internals and has inconsistent repro cases; UI/editor decision complexity
triage.summary: Ctrl+Z undo does not work reliably in card comment editor; fails for single character, word deletion, and general undo, but works after select-all+delete
triage.test_hint: Open a card, type a comment, delete a character with backspace, press Ctrl+Z and verify the character is restored; also test Ctrl+Backspace word deletion undo

body:
## Summary

Currently, when moving a board to another category from the sidebar, I can only select existing categories. There may be cases where I want to move the board to a new category. To do so, I'd first have to create the new category, then go back to the Options Menu (...) from the sidebar and move the board.

If we can add a "Create New Category" option under the existing categories in the menu (similar to Channels), it would allow users to easily move boards to a newly created category.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Moving boards to a new category

### Channels Sidebar
![Screen Shot 2022-10-06 at 4 21 53 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/93531870/194411856-c0b31ac0-b5d0-4fd7-9673-db2c8b5e6376.png)

### Boards Sidebar
![Screen Shot 2022-10-06 at 4 27 23 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/93531870/194411982-7a58b3a1-07a9-4a01-bdce-7408a02f567b.png)
title: Feature Idea: Option to "Create New Category" under the "Move To..." option in the sidebar

triage.summary: Add a 'Create New Category' option at the bottom of the 'Move To...' submenu in the board sidebar context menu, allowing users to create a category and move the board to it in one step instead of two.
triage.test_hint: Verify that clicking 'Create New Category' in the Move To submenu opens the category creation dialog, creates the category, and moves the board to it. Confirm existing category move behavior is unaffected.

body: Broken out of #3812. We have some error handling/routing logic that does variations of `/boards/error` and `/plugins/focalboard/error` that under the right circumstances will do a infinite recursive redirect loop (see video in original issue). To do: - determine condition(s) that cause this breakage - fix logic to prevent unnecessary redirects A thought: might be worth holding off until multi-product architecture ships as we'll no longer be a plugin in the event that changes some of the URLs or client side errors.
title: Investigate `/error` handling logic to prevent recursive redirects

triage.skip_reason: No clear repro steps; requires investigation of routing logic and dependency on upcoming multi-product architecture changes
triage.summary: Infinite recursive redirect loop in /error route handling between /boards/error and /plugins/focalboard/error paths under certain conditions
triage.test_hint: Reproduce by navigating to error route variants and verifying no redirect loop occurs; check React Router error boundary and redirect logic for circular conditions

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behaviour

1. Create or go to a board in table view
2. Ensure that the view has an editable text column
3. Attempt to drag to select text from within that column

## Expected behaviour

The text is selected. Instead, the whole row is picked up and dragged, and if you drop it, it often gets moved even if you drop it in the same place you started.

At a higher level, I'd expect clicking and dragging the mouse to only trigger text selection as long as the cursor is within an editable textbox. I'd only expect the whole row to get dragged when first clicking outside of an editable field.

## Edition and Platform

- Boards plugin on community in MM Desktop App on Mac
- Focalboard 0.15.2 (33) standalone on Mac

## Additional context

This might be related to https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/1620, but I'm not sure if those components handle dragging/selection differently
title: Bug: Unable to drag to select text in table view

triage.summary: Dragging to select text in table view cells triggers row drag-and-drop instead of text selection. The drag handler needs to check whether the pointer is inside an editable input/textarea before initiating row drag.
triage.test_hint: Render a table row with a text cell, simulate a mousedown+mousemove inside the text input, and assert the row drag handler is NOT called; also assert that dragging outside a text input DOES trigger the row drag.

body:
Hello!

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Team Edition + Mattermost Boards (plugin)
 - Version: 7.1.1 + 7.1.0

## Description

We are considering Mattermost with Mattermost Boards Plugin as a solution in our company. The main concern is the email notifications for any actions in cards and due dates. Right now all the communication between Boards and Mattermost (and end user) are based on the messages passed through Boards bot.

If you check all the messages in the direct message history it looks like a flow of separate notifications. It doesn't look like threads or something to group or separate differend boards and cards messages from the complete bot conversation feed.

Also you will nevere get message in email inbox until "you’ve been offline for more than 60 seconds or away from Mattermost for more than five minutes". You cannot control this setting.

The special case is due dates - there is no notifications at all.

It is very important for us while you working on several separate projects at the same time and get a lot of notifications be able to track your tasks events without distracting from your work process. When you get all the info into your GMail inbox it much more easier (also you can search in your inbox to track taks history, for example).

What we expect and why it is important:
1. Be able to control email notifications in Mattermost and especially Mattermost Boards plugin and be able to set it "always" (without rules of away/offline for a certain amount of time). It is important that the end users will be notified guaranteed and know that every notification can be found in their inbox.
2. Receive notification of the due date. Also it will be good to customize notifications i.e. get notification in 1 day or somethig before due date will arrive. Also it can be applied to start date (in case of start date->end date time interval set).

## Additional questions
1. Do you have that functionality in your roadmap?
2. Also how it can be customized on the our end - for example custom bot or something?
title: Feature Idea: email notifications

triage.skip_reason: Large feature request requiring backend scheduling, email infrastructure, and UI settings; involves product/design decisions
triage.summary: Users want email notifications for Boards card actions and due dates, with configurable timing (e.g. 1 day before due date), independent of Mattermost online/offline status thresholds.

body:
## Summary

Currently I am working on make a custom theme for focalboard, but find that the the theme is changed by color variables in inline styles, this is not friendly to secondary development.

light mode

![截图 2022-08-05 17-34-40](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/34258753/183049574-5e616e5d-73ec-4f4d-bfb6-1ab70d6d5257.png)

when change to night mode

![截图 2022-08-05 17-34-58](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/34258753/183049604-7327438d-6d88-4690-bbf9-61a531b190de.png)

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Convenient for third-party theme development

## Additional context/similar features

I checked the related issue and learned that focalboard does not plan to support the import of custom css files in the short term, so I decided to fork this project, and then make some changes I want to by this "hack" way, but now I find that focalboard does not support custom css files. There is so little support that even css variables are provided inline, which frustrates me.

It's not required, and focalboard will always be an excellent project even without support from 3rd party themes. But it's a good idea for the team to let the project team focus on developing the core functionality and then open up permissions for other interested people to develop the theme, which also benefit more users.

So I hope you can think more carefully about this issue.
title: Feature Idea: Add the switching method of class-level night mode and day mode

triage.summary: Request to replace inline CSS variable styles for light/dark mode with class-based theming (e.g., body.dark-mode / body.light-mode) to improve support for third-party theme customization and secondary development.
triage.test_hint: Verify light and dark mode visuals are unchanged after refactor; ensure CSS class toggles on theme switch; confirm no regression in existing theme variables.

Configuring colors on multi-select and select property types 2. Enabling properties on the board view to quickly see assigned property values without needing to open the card ## Additional context/similar features #### ClickUp: Free color selector ![Screen Shot 2022-08-04 at 10 11 43 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/93531870/182986864-f4974780-e9cd-46c5-b697-9f9298f57480.png) #### Asana: 16 color options ![Screen Shot 2022-08-04 at 10 08 36 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/93531870/182986677-de491059-b8f0-4c4e-a312-dd55c9116c1a.png) #### Notion: 10 color options
