triage.test_hint: Verify new colors render correctly on select/multi-select chips in board, table, and card views; ensure color values persist after save and reload.

body:
## Summary

It's not immediately clear in the linking modal/process how linking works, in terms of what it is, and why to do it. Most channels will already have linked boards so the default "landing page" won't be displayed. 

It also seems a bit too easy to link a board to a new channel. I wanted to test it but it seemed like it could really break things for the currently linked channel. For example - how would they know the board that they had linked had been removed and re-linked elsewhere? If they try to link it back, the channel that it's now linked to would lose it without notification. Sort of like a boards tug of war. 

## How important this is to me and why

High - this may impact user experience and cause frustration.

## Additional context/similar features

I propose adding help text to the "Link board" modal. Something along the lines of:

"Linking a board adds it to the channel's RHS for easy access. Read our docs for more information." and the docs can provide a detailed breakdown of how it works and the use cases.
title: Feature Idea: UX improvement for linking channels

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX decision requiring design review and product input on copy and linking behavior
triage.summary: Add help text and clearer UX to the 'Link Board' modal explaining what linking does, why to do it, and warning users about the side effects of re-linking an already-linked board (e.g. the previous channel losing access without notification).
triage.test_hint: Verify help text appears in the link board modal, check that re-linking a board from one channel to another shows a warning or confirmation dialog, and confirm the previously linked channel receives some indication that the board was removed.

triage.test_hint: Check that the server binary reports the correct semver string via version flag/endpoint and that the UI reflects the exact version rather than a month/year string.

body:
## Summary

When pasting/adding images into a card, there's no way to preview that image or even download it. Due to this nature, it can be hard to review attached images or even download them to preview them in another application.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: High

Use cases:
1. When an image is attached to a card as content, when clicking on the image, it should open up another modal with the image preview
2. From there, the user could then be able to view the image in full size or "fitted" to their screen size and allow them to zoom
3. In the preview they could even download the image for a number of reasons

1. When clicking on the three dots beside an image, it would be nice to have a download option - or even implement one that displays when hovering over images to save a few clicks

## Additional context/similar features

A project I'm currently working on requires sharing of mockup images between designers and developers. Unfortunately, while we can attach images, there's no way for the developers to "enhance" the image in the card to view the full design. This has left us with having to share images outside of the card and including a link to that external source. It would be nice to have it as an all-in-one system to remove the need for an external dependency. Other than that, it's been great!!
title: Feature Idea: Image preview/download for attached images

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX decisions required for modal design, zoom behavior, and download flow; medium complexity with multiple interaction patterns
triage.summary: Add image preview modal with zoom/fit controls and download option when clicking or via context menu on attached images in cards
triage.test_hint: Test image click opens modal, modal shows full/fitted image, zoom in/out works, download button fetches correct file, three-dot menu has download option

body:
## Summary

It would be really cool to translate any new or edited issue into focalboard cards. In this way one might have a single board that holds both, the code issues from gitlab, and the tasks discussed and distributed across members from the related Mattermost Channel.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: High

Use cases:
1. A project requires the development of code (e.g., a data analysis in R). This code is hosted on GitLab and an MM channel is linked to that project. Code related issues are generated directly at GitLab or in the IDE that is used for code development. However, the discussion of additional tasks that are not directly linked to the code (e.g., brainstormings, tasks to retrieve additional data) takes place at MM. A focal board at MM that automatically shows the GitLab issues as well as the MM tasks as cards would be great to have the big picture at hand.
2. The same can happen for Manuscript writing. The LaTeX sources are hosted at GitLab, writing issues are distributed as issues among authors, while discussions on what to include in the text, which journal to select, will take place at MM and may also end up in tasks. Having all GitLab issues and assigned MM tasks in one board would make the whole writing process really transparent.

## Additional context/similar features

I did not came across some so far.
title: Translate GitLab issues into FocalBoard cards

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring external GitLab API integration, OAuth/webhook infrastructure, and significant design decisions around data mapping and sync strategy.
triage.summary: Feature request to sync GitLab issues as Focalboard cards, enabling a unified board view of both GitLab issues and Mattermost tasks.

body:
## Summary

Ability to hide certain properties on a per-card level, so users can hide properties not relevant to the specific card. Properties are additive on a board, and if certain properties are needed for a select few cards, they get applied to all cards on the board, but users may not want to see all the properties on every card. Deleting a property on one card also deletes it for all cards.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Multi-purpose board where many properties are available, but not all of them are relevant or needed for every card on the board.

## Additional context/similar features
See this [thread](https://community.mattermost.com/private-core/pl/7hzunk4kuprhpq6cappejnje7o) from @michaelgamble for more details:

> Also completely different topic, I've been thinking about my use of boards / cards - one feature I'm thinking i would like is the ability to define different card types that basically define different sets of properties for each card type... 
> 
> The reason I'm thinking about this is I have a board right now where I'm tracking:
> 
> - Tasks
> - Stories
> - Bugs
> - Component Releases
> - Text Font Releases
> - Icon Releases
> 
> Each of these use cases have a slightly different set of requirements in terms of data fields as properties for example "Icon Releases" would have a URL for GitHub PR - but many of the other ones don't....  IF i want to see them in the same board, I'm forced to keep adding more and more and more properties to a card in order to manage the different types and its very quickly feeling like it could grow out of hand... 
> 
> I'm not sure what the solution is but in quickly thinking about it, makes me think the solution might lay in the "having a card on multiple boards" explorations you guys are approaching.. the only problem though is i'm pretty sure the requirement you've currently set out is that they all need to have all properties additively matching...
> 
> The reason I'm bringing this up here is maybe there is a case for not adding all the property fields, but some other in-between solution maybe (have not put a lot of thought into this)... perhaps identifying which fields will be common on the board that has cards from multiple sources??
> 
> Anyways food for though.. just wanted to share... oh and also i was thinking of the parallel of jira and issue types while visualizing this
title: Feature Idea: Hide properties on cards/card templates

## Summary

A way to limit the maximum number of cards that can be moved to a column in board view, to prevent project overload and help teams focus on completing existing tasks before moving on to new ones.

Use cases:
1. Setting WIP limits on card status, so only a set number of cards can be in "In Progress" or "In Review"
2. Setting WIP limits on assignees, so an assignee cannot have more than X number of cards assigned to them at any one time.

## Additional context/similar features

- https://djaa.com/patterns-of-kanban-maturity/

body:
## Summary
The text property currently doesn't support text wrapping or multi-line text, so longer text becomes difficult to read. Full text is only visible as a tooltip on hover. To improve the text property, we can consider some of the following options:

1. Text wrapping with an auto-expanding field, so users don't have to keep scrolling to the right and the entire text value is always visible on the card
2. Expand/minimize button, so user can see the full text when they want to without impacting the card layout
3. Auto-expanding to a limit, with button to "Show All" - Combo of option 1 and 2

Open to other suggestions as well!

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Inputting long text values
4. Users who use the `Text` property as a note field

## Additional context/similar features
![Screen Shot 2022-05-26 at 5 41 43 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/93531870/170584795-509b16c1-2ada-45aa-bdb0-fb8cb5555502.png)

triage.test_hint: Render a card with a long text property value and verify wrapping or expand/collapse behavior works correctly across board and table views.

body:
## Summary

I found we've been using other tools for these retrospective / quadrant view, and realized that this would be a perfect use case for boards.. if each quadrant could be like the current kanban, where you can add items, order them, and drag and drop them into quadrants.. board cards would be perfect.

The things that makes boards more powerful then other tools in my opinion is four fold:

1. being able to have a continued conversation on a card
2. being able to create multiple views for taking action on items as outcomes of the retrospective
3. being able to have integration for the sake of cross posting
4. the power of being able to replicate the card into other boards (coming in that future feature)

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: High/Medium/Low

Use cases:
1. Retrospective quadrants
2. market fit quadrants
3. magic quadrants
4. the list goes on and on...

## Additional context/similar features

Right now we use figjam for retrospectives, but the usefulness of it ends because the follow up needs to happen outside of that ecosystem.. in our case inside the MM ecosystem.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/31866506/170327533-13408569-ac22-4590-9cf2-4dcbf8528bcd.png)
title: Feature Idea: Retrospective / Quadrant View

triage.skip_reason: Large new view type requiring significant UI/UX design decisions and substantial frontend/backend work; not suitable for automated attempt
triage.summary: Feature request for a new Quadrant/Retrospective board view where cards can be placed and dragged across four configurable quadrants, similar to FigJam retrospective boards but integrated with Focalboard's card features (comments, multi-view, integrations).

body:
## Summary

Firstly thank you all for building such an amazing tool.
Our team is now using focalboard as our daily agile kanban. We show this in a pretty big TV and do daily scrum in front of it. However its header and side panel do affect its useful viewport. Only ~70% of the screen is being used.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. See summary.

## Additional context/similar features
We're currently inject a script at our nginx side
```
sub_filter '</body>' '</body><script>$script</script>';
sub_filter_once on;
```
The script is actually a simple JS
```
document.getElementsByClassName("top-head")[0].hidden = true; 
document.getElementsByClassName("Sidebar octo-sidebar")[0].style.display = "none"; 
document.getElementsByClassName("Kanban")[0].style.marginLeft = 0;
```
We understand it's really a hack. Just for your reference.
title: Feature Idea: Show a kanban in fullscreen mode

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX decision requiring design input and cross-platform considerations; fullscreen mode involves sidebar, header, and layout changes with non-trivial interaction design
triage.summary: Add a fullscreen/presentation mode for kanban boards that hides the sidebar and top header to maximize viewport usage, particularly useful for display on large screens or TVs during standups.
triage.test_hint: Verify sidebar and top-head are hidden in fullscreen mode, margin/layout adjusts correctly, and a toggle mechanism (keyboard shortcut or button) enters/exits fullscreen mode without breaking navigation.

body:
We should have the `npm run i18n-extract` step ran in CI similarly to linting e.g. fail CI if there are i18n changes that weren't committed.

Rough CI steps:

1. `npm run i18n-extract`
2. Check if changes e.g. `git status --porcelain` or `git diff --exit-code`

- https://remarkablemark.org/blog/2017/10/12/check-git-dirty/
- https://unix.stackexchange.com/questions/155046/determine-if-git-working-directory-is-clean-from-a-script
title: Code Debt: Check `npm run i18n-extract` in CI

triage.summary: Add `npm run i18n-extract` step to CI pipeline that fails the build if extracted i18n strings differ from committed strings, using `git diff --exit-code` or `git status --porcelain` to detect uncommitted changes.
triage.test_hint: Verify CI fails when i18n strings are not extracted/committed and passes when they are up to date.

body:
While completing #2900 I found the root cause of #2898 to be that `Utils.buildURL` was modified and doesn't build absolute URLs in non-plugin installs even if the 2nd arg `absolute` is present and `true`. At a glance, the pattern inside the codebase seems to be to use `buildURL` to build a relative URL and append it to a URL with a base instead of building an absolute URL string all in one go.

e.g.

```ts
const url = new URL(window.location.origin)
url.pathname = Utils.buildURL('/foo?bar=baz', true)
```

I tracked the regression in `buildURL` to this diff:

https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/pull/2406/files#diff-9e0f028747bfe61f41e35d96170706c27451268275f8dd0303a77986ae329916R520-R523 (`blame` source: https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/blame/9001c3576f1cd13da6953b5c193e0cd7bd712de6/webapp/src/utils.ts#L520)

The intention of this ticket is to gauge whether some sort of refactor to `buildURL` and it's usages is worth it and then the scope of how to proceed with that.
title: Code Debt: `Utils.buildURL` doesn't build absolute URLs in non-plugin installations

triage.skip_reason: Requires careful audit of all buildURL call sites across the codebase and decision on refactor scope; risk of regressions in both plugin and non-plugin modes
triage.summary: Utils.buildURL ignores the `absolute=true` argument in non-plugin installations due to a regression introduced in PR #2406, causing absolute URL construction to silently fail. Many call sites work around this incorrectly, requiring a coordinated refactor.
triage.test_hint: Unit-test buildURL with absolute=true in non-plugin mode and verify it returns a full URL including origin; integration-test affected flows (e.g. share links, redirects) in standalone vs plugin deployments.

title: Bug: Personal server > Log errors after dropping db

triage.summary: After dropping the DB and restarting, the server logs repeated 401 debug errors ('unable to get the session for the token: sql: no rows in result set') instead of gracefully handling the missing session by redirecting the client to re-authenticate or suppressing the noise at an appropriate log level.
triage.test_hint: Verify that when a session token references a non-existent DB row, the server returns 401 without spamming debug logs, and that the client is prompted to re-login rather than retrying indefinitely.

body:
The `latest` Docker tag is a side-effect of the latest _build_ from `main`, not the latest _release_. We're looking to update it so `latest` is triggered off of _releases_ so it's reasonably more stable. This stemmed from #2592.

The pattern is to have building/tagging/releasing defined in GitLab CI so we'll need to migrate from our config in hub.docker.com. Relevant community thread: https://community-daily.mattermost.com/private-core/pl/bfx4n8dpu3gymybsdm545mpurw
title: Bug: Normalize & migrate Docker tags to GitLab CI

triage.skip_reason: Requires access to internal GitLab CI config, Docker Hub settings, and coordination with release pipeline; not safe to auto-attempt infrastructure changes
triage.summary: Migrate Docker image build/tag/release pipeline from Docker Hub automated builds to GitLab CI, and fix `latest` tag to track releases instead of main branch builds.
triage.test_hint: Verify that after a release tag is pushed, the `latest` Docker image reflects the release version, and that main branch builds only produce a `main` or SHA-based tag.

body:
Big thanks for your awesome work in advance!

## Summary

Currently there is no overwrite protection when two people edit one block at the same time: if you defocus a block it gets auto-saved and your local state overwrites potential edits by others. As I think true collaboration (editing at the same time, seeing changes live, ...) is very difficult to implement and comes with other downsides (stable internet connection, performance, ...?) **I would suggest to just lock a block, when someone else has the same block in focus.** 

## How important this is to me and why

_Importance: High_
To me and my team this issue is of extraordinary importance. It is not only the loss of data but also the trust in the tool that makes this a problem for us. See the example:

Use cases:
1. Person A has a block opened and in focus
2. Person B typed loads of content in the same block (like a transcript)
3. A now closes the block to save the content
4. B does not get the update as the block is still open, closes the block and therefore overwrites all edits by A 


## How it could be solved

So I imagine (from a user's perspective, I don't know about the technical details) a lock icon appearing on the left of the block, when someone is already editing this block, hindering me to enter the block at all. 


I use focalsboards as part of a mattermost install on yunohost (v 6.5.0)
Thanks a lot and keep up the good work!
title: Feature Idea: Locking blocks for better collaboration

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring real-time presence tracking, WebSocket/signaling infrastructure, UI lock indicators, and conflict resolution logic across frontend and backend. Significant architectural work.
triage.summary: Feature request to lock blocks being edited by another user to prevent concurrent edit conflicts and data loss. Proposes showing a lock icon when a block is in focus by someone else.

body:
## Summary

- Allow setting zoom level as we can do currently with CTRL + Mouse Scroll buttons.

- I think adding zoom level adjustment will increase the productivity and visibility of boards. 

- As it occurred to me when we have more columns in board we have to scroll on the right side to see the entire board but instead if zoom level is present then it will improve visibility of entire board properly. 

- My suggestion is if this feature is going to be implemented then it should be placed around top right corner near feedback placeholder.

- Zoom level should be from 10% - 100% [or maximum zoom can be more than 100%].

- It should also retain zoom level after application is closed.

- Currently we can do this adjustment via CTRL + Mouse Scroll buttons, but unfortunately this gets reset on restart.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:

1. When more columns are present in board. In my case I have (5 columns) very low, low, medium, high, super high priorities in tasks and 8 columns in task status [backlog, in development, analysis, in progress, testing, finished, not finished, parked]. Viewing by priority or status requires horizontal scrolling.

2. When working on generally popular screen resolution of 1366 X 768 with above conditions.

3. When we need entire board to be visible without horizontally scrolling for more fields.

Above mentioned some of the use cases can be fulfilled by adding zoom level adjustment feature.
title: Feature Idea: Adding Application Zoom Level Adjustment

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX design decision required; large scope involving persistent zoom state, UI controls, and cross-platform behavior (web vs desktop); requires product team input on placement and zoom range.
triage.summary: User requests a persistent zoom level control (10%-100%+) accessible via UI (suggested: top-right corner) to improve visibility of boards with many columns, replacing the current ephemeral CTRL+scroll zoom that resets on restart.
triage.test_hint: Verify zoom persists across restarts; verify board layout renders correctly at min/max zoom levels; verify zoom control appears in correct UI location.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

Related to #2714 

**Share Modal**
1. Go to share modal
2. Go to the last visible member of the board and change their role
3. Scroll to bottom of role selector menu
4. See error - there is no padding below the menu

**Card View Modal**
1. Go to card view modal
2. Create a select or multi-select property
3. Add 10+ values for the property (enough so when the menu is expanded, it reaches to the bottom of the card view modal)
4. Select the property field to assign a value
5. Scroll to bottom of the menu
6. See error - there is no padding below the menu

## Expected behavior

Should be some padding below the menu

## Screenshots (optional)

![Screen Shot 2022-04-01 at 2 58 49 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/93531870/161326035-6a348b7e-c102-4a13-b36f-5567a8471b1c.png)

![Screen Shot 2022-04-01 at 3 04 19 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/93531870/161325921-81540bca-789d-4020-83c3-9ac463bdfb6e.png)

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Mattermost Boards (plugin)
 - Version: permissions-feature-branch
 - Browser and OS: Chrome on Mac
title: Permissions Branch: No padding below modal menus

triage.summary: Missing bottom padding in dropdown/role-selector menus inside Share Modal and Card View Modal when scrolled to the bottom.
triage.test_hint: Verify padding-bottom is present on the scrollable container of the role selector in Share Modal and the select/multi-select property menu in Card View Modal by inspecting CSS; add or adjust padding-bottom in the relevant SCSS/CSS files.

## Summary
Currently the portable distribution of Focalboard defaults to storing data My Documents. This is not very portable. Specifying an alternative location would be useful.

What the new capability is.
Specify via configuration file or environment variable the location for Focalboard's data directory.

## How important this is to me and why
Fixing the data location to My Documents in the portable distribution isn't very portable.

Importance: High/Medium/Low
High

Use cases:
1. Move install and data together via USB device
title: Feature Idea: Ability to specify data location for personal standalone install

triage.skip_reason: Requires design decisions around config file schema and environment variable naming, plus cross-platform path handling; not trivially automatable

triage.test_hint: Verify that setting the data path via config file and environment variable correctly redirects database and file storage to the specified directory on Windows, macOS, and Linux.

body:
## Summary

Pretty much inspired by #1756 ("_Auto-sync card with GitHub_"):

> Created from [this thread](https://community.mattermost.com/core/pl/d99j4u4eu3dkueg1ta5qrn9jxe). It would be great to have a card be automatically created when an issue is created on a Github repo; and conversely, a Github issue be created when we create a card in MM.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

## Additional context/similar features

- It should allow to use self hosted instances
- [GitLab's Issues API is well documented](https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/api/issues.html)
- [Developers can reference issues in their commits](https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/user/project/issues/managing_issues.html#closing-issues-automatically) and if a corresponding GitLab issue would be closed, the twin issue in Focalboard could be automatically closed as well (this would also be a tiny workaround for #624 since issues might have a direct GitLab reference)
title: Feature Idea: Auto-sync card with GitLab

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring bidirectional GitLab API integration, webhook handling, and self-hosted instance support; requires product/design decisions
triage.summary: Auto-sync Focalboard cards with GitLab issues bidirectionally, including support for self-hosted GitLab instances and automatic card closure when linked GitLab issues are closed.

This [project](https://github.com/zxch3n/PomodoroLogger) does it but it is local only

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Go to Mattermost Board
2. Create Multiple Tasks with a variation of Priorities including at least 1, 2, 9 and 10
3. Set the board to sort by priority
4. See Error: Sorted Order = 1, 10, 2, 9...

## Expected behavior

Expected Sort Order = 1, 2, 9, 10
Expected logic to place 10 after 9, not between 1 and 2. Suspect that 20 would also be incorrectly placed between 2 and 3 rather than after 19.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Mattermost Boards (plugin)
 - Version: 5.0.4
 - Browser and OS: Windows

## Additional context (optional)

Add any other context about the problem here, and any notes about the severity:

* Sev 3: Affects minor, non-critical functionality
title: Bug: MM Boards Priority Sequencing > 9 Error

triage.summary: Priority field sorting uses lexicographic string comparison instead of numeric comparison, causing values like 10 to sort between 1 and 2 rather than after 9.
triage.test_hint: Add unit tests for the sort comparator on numeric/priority fields verifying that [1,2,9,10] sorts correctly as integers, not strings.

body:
## Summary

In a chroot webhosting environment it is not reliably possible to listen on an ip, because other users could use the same port. This can be easily solved with a unix socket

Which is the new capability.

focalboard-server optionally listens on a unix socket

## How important is it for me and why?

Importance: Medium

Any examples of good implementations of this capability.
```
{
    "serverRoot": "unix:///tmp/.service/html%2Ffocalboard.sock",
    "port": none,
    "dbtype": "sqlite3",
    "dbconfig": "./focalboard.db",
    "postgres_dbconfig": "dbname=focalboard sslmode=disable",
    "useSSL": false,
    "webpath": "./pack",
    "filespath": "./files",
    "telemetry": false,
    "prometheus_address": ":9092",
    "session_expire_time": 2592000,
    "session_refresh_time": 18000,
    "localOnly": false,
    "enableLocalMode": false,
    "localModeSocketLocation":"/tmp/.service/html%2Ffocalboard-admin.sock"
}
```
title: Feature Idea: launch on unix socket

triage.skip_reason: Requires backend networking changes and config schema updates; UI/UX decisions around config format needed
triage.summary: Allow focalboard-server to optionally listen on a Unix domain socket instead of a TCP port, useful for chroot/shared hosting environments where port conflicts are a concern.
triage.test_hint: Add integration test verifying server starts and accepts HTTP requests over a configured Unix socket path; verify TCP mode still works when socket config is absent.

body:
New user here.
Deployed Mattermost on dedicated server (Esxi VM)

**Summary**
While adding a new CARD on BOARD, Icons aren’t working properly.
Only RANDOM icon is affected when clicking several times on the “random” button.
Selecting a particular icon from available ones isn’t working.

**Steps to reproduce**
Not sure if this issue is easy to reproduce or if other users met this issue.
Search through Discussion forum didn’t yield any result.

**Expected behavior**
Icons should update correctly in a snappy way…

body:
## Summary

Currently, the only attachments that can be uploaded to a card are image files. These images are embedded into the card description, but cannot be enlarged, downloaded, or edited. Other file types such as PDF's and videos are not supported either.

Cards would be more functional if they supported:

1. Opening/enlarging attached images
2. [Toggling through and previewing multiple attached images](https://community.mattermost.com/core/pl/98e7c9khmtnyxfcjupeo7jnciy)
3. [Pasting images/screenshots onto cards](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/262)
4. Editing attached images
5. [Uploading generic file types such as PDFs and video files](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/111)
6. Downloading attached files
7. Directory view of all attached files on the board or across the team
8. Uploading/downloading multiple files at the same time

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Viewing or downloading images attached to the card
9. Attaching documents or other files as a reference for the card
10. Editing/cropping images for gallery view
title: Feature Idea: Card Attachment Improvements

triage.skip_reason: Large feature umbrella with multiple sub-features (image lightbox, multi-file support, paste upload, edit, bulk ops, directory view) requiring significant backend and frontend work plus UI/UX decisions.
triage.summary: Comprehensive card attachment improvements: image lightbox/zoom, multi-image carousel, paste-to-upload, image editing, generic file type support (PDF/video), file download, board-level file directory, and bulk upload/download.

body:
Currently is seems users can only subscribe to individual cards. This is nice, but not at all a viable solution for large boards and/or large teams where people need or want to be notified about all changes. For example I just had 3 people join a board that has 181 cards. In order to get notifications they would need to click on 543 cards and follow them individually. That's a minimum of 1629 clicks and a lot of scrolling. When they are done they still won't be notified of new cards and will need to find and follow each as they get added. Every time a new team member joins they have to go through the whole thing.

I suggest a mechanism to follow a whole board instead of only following on a per-card basis.

Somewhat related, issue #563 suggests a notification channel per board.
title: Feature Idea: Follow boards instead of just cards

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring backend subscription model changes, frontend UI decisions, and notification system redesign
triage.summary: Allow users to follow/subscribe to an entire board to receive notifications for all card changes and new card additions, instead of having to follow each card individually.

body:
## Summary

Checklist items are currently only one line long, so if the text goes beyond the width of the modal, then the user has to highlight and scroll to see the full text content.

Long text on checkboxes should wrap to the next line if if exceeds the width of the card modal, so users can easily see the content on the checkboxes without needing to highlight and scroll.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Checkboxes are used to create subtasks and checklists, so supporting text wrapping will make the feature more usable.

Use cases:
1. [Long text on checkboxes](https://community.mattermost.com/core/pl/z4fix56jzpfxjk6zd58wa1nato)
title: Feature Idea: Wrap checkbox text

triage.summary: Checkbox/checklist items in card modals do not wrap long text, requiring users to scroll horizontally to read full content. Text should wrap to multiple lines when it exceeds the modal width.
triage.test_hint: Render a checklist item with a very long text string in the card modal and verify the text wraps to the next line instead of overflowing or requiring horizontal scroll.

body:
## Summary

It would be lovely to have the ability to _import_ redmine issues as focal board cards.
Perhaps based on projects/categories (or a custom field)

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

At our organisation, we use mattermost for all our internal comms and Redmine for project management. We have just begun to integrate focalboard (or rather mattermost boards) into our workflows. This integration would vastly expand the use-cases of focal board for us.

Use cases:
1.  For teams looking to move their project management from redmine to focal board
2.  For teams needing a constant flow from different redmine projects to different focalboard/mattermost boards
3. The work on such a plugin will easily lend itself to adaptations to other integrations -- a mattermost command, a teello API script, etc...

## Additional context/similar features

The [mattermost plugins for redmine](https://github.com/altsol/redmine_mattermost) has all the foundations for extracting information from the Redmine API and importing into the Mattermost chat, it can probably re-used and modified to integrate with Boards.
title: Feature Idea: Webhook/Integration with Redmine

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring external API integration with Redmine; involves design decisions around import mapping, authentication, and plugin architecture
triage.summary: Request to import Redmine issues as Focalboard cards, supporting project/category-based mapping and ongoing sync flows

body:
We need to make the permissions model is working as expected.

Specifically, a team member should be able to join any public board for that team. 

Also need to ensure private boards are private (ie, System Admins cannot join.) However, there also needs to be a way to recover a private board if the board admin is no longer a user. How can System Admin update board permissions?
title: Permissions Branch - Determine missing features of permissions model

triage.skip_reason: Complex permissions model design decisions requiring architectural choices and UI decisions around admin recovery flows
triage.summary: Permissions model gaps: team members should auto-join public boards; private boards must be inaccessible to System Admins by default; need a recovery mechanism for private boards whose admin has left the system

body:
## Summary

It's related to dates but not quite to [the calendar view](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/338).

I just stumbled over [Timestripe](https://timestripe.com/) which is basically a Kanban board but each of the columns is grouping cards by a certain time.
For instance like this:

![grafik](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/372620/154719045-ac0acc26-eefa-466f-bb2a-6713c727ca2d.png)

Or an overview per week:

![grafik](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/372620/154719734-a270491d-6d93-4aa1-acaa-d5be6f768201.png)

Or see Trello's take on this:

![Calendar_2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/372620/165177679-8f53145b-461a-423a-9ea9-9cac6e9ecf96.jpg)

![Calendar_3](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/372620/165177789-5d9ec1be-ae2b-436b-a73e-0da75e2f78b4.jpg)

In my opinion Focalboard is able to do even more than Timestripe in general but the dynamic grouping gives a different user experience for a Kanban board than the current calendar view.

Maybe this is even possible somehow and I'm just missing the bit how to configure it properly.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: it's just a UX improvement (but obviously so good, that [Timestripe](https://timestripe.com/) made it their business model) and [Trello made it into a popular extension](https://trello.com/power-ups/55a5d917446f517774210011/calendar-power-up).

Use cases:
1. A good time based overview

## Additional context/similar features

The calendar view is somehow similar in regards of having a timely overview
title: Feature Idea: automatic time based grouping views (calendar / date related view)

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring significant UI/UX design decisions and complex frontend/backend work for dynamic time-based grouping logic

## Steps to reproduce the behavior

## Expected behavior

The card should move directly to the dropped position

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/46905241/153519856-3a0fd910-a008-46d9-bfca-fd1ee70e68d1.mov

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Mattermost plugin
 - Version: v0.14
 - Browser and OS: Chrome on Mac

## Additional context

In the browser network tab, notice that PATCH Block is called twice. This is because on [onDropToCard in kanban.tsx](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/blob/main/webapp/src/components/kanban/kanban.tsx#L172), the property change and sort order are two separate API calls. Propose refactoring to call UpdateBlocks in a single call.
title: Perf: Drag and drop cards in Kanban board view shows intermediate sort state

triage.skip_reason: Requires understanding of drag-and-drop state management and merging two separate API calls into one atomic UpdateBlocks call; UI/UX behavior decision involved
triage.summary: Dragging a card in Kanban view causes a visible intermediate sort state because onDropToCard fires two separate PATCH Block API calls (property change + sort order) instead of one atomic UpdateBlocks call, resulting in a brief incorrect card position flash.
triage.test_hint: Drag a card onto another card in a column with 5+ cards and verify the card moves directly to the target position without first appearing at the top or bottom; confirm only a single network PATCH/PUT request is made for the combined update.

title: Bug: 502 Bad Gateway on Personal Server (Ubuntu)

triage.skip_reason: No reproducible test case; environment-specific issue (MariaDB vs MySQL on personal server, systemd restart failure). Requires local environment debugging and likely user-side configuration fix.
triage.summary: Focalboard personal server fails to start (exit-code 1) after system restart on Debian 10 with MariaDB, resulting in 502 Bad Gateway. Root cause unclear from truncated logs but likely a DB connection or driver compatibility issue with MariaDB.
triage.test_hint: Check focalboard server logs for full error after startup failure; verify MariaDB is running before focalboard starts (add After=mysql.service or mariadb.service to systemd unit); confirm MariaDB user/password/db still valid after restart.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Create a card, and mention a user (e.g. yourself) in the card with `@username`
2. You receive a notification (as expected)
2. Duplicate the board
3. You receive another notification
4. Duplicate the card
5. You receive another notification

## Expected behavior

Should not notify mentioned users on duplicated boards or duplicated cards.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Mattermost plugin
 - Version: v0.14.0
 - Browser and OS: Chrome on macOS
title: Bug: Duplicating a board or card notifies users who are mentioned in card descriptions

triage.summary: Duplicating a board or card triggers @mention notifications for users mentioned in card descriptions, same as creating a new mention. Duplicate operations should not fire mention notifications.
triage.test_hint: When duplicating a board or card, assert that no mention notifications are sent to users referenced in card description fields.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Go to the template selector
2. Select "+New Template"
3. Enter a long name for the template, e.g. _Another template with a very long name_
4. Return to template selector
5. Observe that the template name is truncated
6. Move the cursor over the template title

## Expected behavior

Full title should be displayed on hover

## Screenshots

![template_titles](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15314183/152404797-ec050ecf-23eb-49c5-a41b-e04094e13dfb.jpg)

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Desktop / Personal Server / Mattermost plugin
 - Version: v0.14
 - Browser and OS: Chrome on Windows
title: Bug: Template selector > No way to see full text for long template titles

triage.summary: Template selector truncates long template titles with no tooltip on hover to show full text.
triage.test_hint: Add a title attribute or tooltip to the template name element in the template selector so hovering shows the full title when truncated.

body:
## Summary

Well I tried to export images I uploaded to the board but there is no download or export button. Export to csv does not export assets. Export to .focalboard file makes it impossible to unzip file with 7z, winrar, unarchiver, jszip and other tools. So I tried to open devtools but there is no way I could do it: hotkey does not work, right click is disabled, no option in menu. Browser version in localhost requires an account and I don't know what is this, I don't have an account, I just want to access my board.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Export image
2. Change functionality
3. Debug app in production

## Additional context/similar features

please add
title: Feature Idea: Allow to open devtools in app or allow to register in localhost in browser to open devtools there

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX decision required: enabling devtools in production desktop app is a policy choice; also touches multiple unrelated issues (devtools access, image export, archive format compatibility)
triage.summary: User requests ability to open DevTools in the desktop app (e.g. via menu option or keyboard shortcut) and easier localhost browser access without account registration. Underlying motivation is lack of image export functionality and inability to inspect/debug the app.

body:
Ensures that every form element has a visible label and is not solely labeled using hidden labels, or the title or aria-describedby attributes ([more info](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.3/label-title-only?application=AxeChrome))


1. Board title
   - Element location: 
 `input[placeholder="Untitled\ board"]`
   - Element source: 
`<input class="Editable title" placeholder="Untitled board" title="Meeting Notes" spellcheck="true" value="Meeting Notes">`
2. View selector
   - Element location: 
 `input[placeholder="Untitled\ View"]`
   - Element source: 
`<input class="Editable undefined" placeholder="Untitled View" title="By type" spellcheck="true" value="By type">`
3. Board column headers
   - Element location: 
 `input[title="Ad\ Hoc"]`
   - Element source: 
`<input class="Editable undefined" placeholder="New Select" title="Ad Hoc" spellcheck="true" value="Ad Hoc">`
title: Bug: Accessibility > Form elements should have a visible label

triage.summary: Accessibility: form elements (board title input, view selector input, board column header inputs) rely solely on title or aria-describedby attributes for labeling instead of visible labels, violating WCAG/axe label-title-only rule.
triage.test_hint: Add aria-label or associate a visible <label> element to each affected input (board title, view selector, column headers) and verify with axe DevTools or jest-axe that no label-title-only violation is reported.

title: Bug: Accessibility > Ensure controls are not nested

triage.summary: MenuWrapper div has role='button' causing nested interactive controls (div[role=button] wrapping button elements) which breaks screen reader accessibility across Sidebar and ViewHeader components.
triage.test_hint: Remove role='button' and aria-label='menuwrapper' from MenuWrapper div elements, or restructure so the inner button is the sole interactive control; verify with axe accessibility checker that nested-interactive violations are resolved.

body: Element is in tab order and does not have accessible text ([more info](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.3/link-name?application=AxeChrome)). Fix at least one of these issues: - Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers - aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty - aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty - Element has no title attribute Element location: `.help-button` Element source: `` ![help_button](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15314183/152164236-c2d6627b-6156-4f00-9579-36a4bc3121fb.jpg)
title: Bug: Accessibility > Help button should have discernible text

triage.summary: Help button in global header lacks accessible text; screen readers cannot identify the link because it has no aria-label, title, or visible text.
triage.test_hint: Add aria-label='Help' (or i18n equivalent) to the help-button anchor element and verify with axe or a screen reader that the link is now announced correctly.

body:
## Summary

The current `Date` property type works great for specifying specific dates or date ranges but it doesn't allow me to capture the more generic concept of duration. I currently am using the `Number` property type with a label that specifies a fixed unit of time but this is not very clean when durations are too large or small and it also does not display the units of time in every view which make it hard to interpret (in the calendar for instance)

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. In order to track how long you expect a given issue to take, it would be useful to have a property type that understood the concept of time, allowed for better interfaces for setting durations and showed units of time in all views

For example - issues with expected work hours of 100 or 0.25 - depending on the view, I might prefer to see `4 day, 4 hours` instead of 100 hours and `15 minutes` instead of 0.25 hours. Similarly - adding some friendly input support would be helpful to auto convert between units (all of these should be accepted and mean the same : 720m, 12h,  0.5d).
title: Feature Idea: Consider adding a Duration Property Type

triage.skip_reason: New property type requires significant frontend and backend work including UI design decisions for input/display formats and unit conversions
triage.summary: Add a Duration property type that stores time durations and displays them with appropriate units (minutes, hours, days), supporting friendly input conversion between units (e.g. 720m = 12h = 0.5d)

body:
## Summary

Being able to sort tasks based on multiple criteria especially when dealing multiple custom properties would be extremely useful.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Improved ease of use
2. Multiple sort criteria makes prioritising based on multiple factors useful during  discussions

## Additional context/similar features

Using the filter-style UI can be reused which results in sorting order instead just filter
title: Feature Idea: Multiple sorting options

triage.skip_reason: Multi-sort is a significant UI and data-model change requiring design decisions on sort order precedence, UI interactions, and persistence
triage.summary: Allow users to define multiple sort criteria on a board view, similar to how multiple filters are stacked, so cards can be ordered by several properties simultaneously.

body: My windows in dark mode, as soon as i open focal board its theme is in "system theme", even if i change it to "default theme" it reverts back to  "system theme" after app restart .
title: Bug: App theme auto change on app close

triage.summary: Theme preference is not persisted; app always reverts to 'system theme' on restart instead of retaining user-selected theme.
triage.test_hint: Set theme to 'default theme', restart the app, and verify the selected theme is retained rather than reverting to system theme.

body:
Not sure if this would be considered an enhancement but here it is!

## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Open card modal
2. Add new description
3. Click on the kebab-menu of the last item and try to delete it
4. You have to scroll to see the entire popup menu

## Expected behavior

I expected the menu to pop above the item so that it's entirety is visible and easy to access.

## Screenshots

![focalboard-card-modal-popup-menu-hidden-at-bottom](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/13135150/151336449-5ae5e263-1038-4a97-8388-36093334a291.gif)

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Desktop
 - Version: v0.12.1
 - Browser and OS: Vivaldi 5.0.2497.48 (Stable channel) stable (64-bit) on Ubuntu 20.04.3 LTS

## Additional context

There should be a lot of content already in the modal so that it overflows out of the modal (need to scroll).
title: Bug: Card Modal Popup Menu Hidden At Bottom

triage.summary: Kebab/context menu in card modal appears below the triggering item when near the bottom of the modal, requiring the user to scroll to see all options. The menu should flip upward (or use viewport-aware positioning) when there is insufficient space below.
triage.test_hint: Render a card modal with enough content to overflow, open the kebab menu on the last item, and assert the menu is fully visible within the viewport without scrolling.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Create to Project Tasks
2. Go to All Tasks view
3. Sort by Priority ascending/descending

## Expected behavior

When sorted (ascending) Low priority tasks should be placed lower then the Medium priority tasks

## Screenshots

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/26669926/151328569-7b7a1b73-ec1e-4e78-a52b-50fd2eb8b5dd.png)

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Desktop
 - Version: v0.12.1
 - Browser and OS: Edge 96 on Windows
title: Bug: Wrong sorting by priority behavior

triage.summary: Priority select property sorts lexicographically instead of by semantic priority order (Low/Medium/High), causing incorrect ascending/descending sort results in board views.
triage.test_hint: Add unit tests for priority property sort comparator ensuring High > Medium > Low ordering; verify ascending sort places Low below Medium and Medium below High in the All Tasks view.

body: When the main account was being setup the Name was entered incorrectly and there appears to be no way to change the name.  I would recommend having a user profile option so this can be self-maintained.  However, in the meantime, is there a way to manually change this?
title: Changing User Name

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX decision required for profile management feature; no clear repro or spec
triage.summary: No user profile editing capability exists; users cannot change their display name after initial account setup. Request for self-service profile management UI.

1. Go to a card
2. Type a comment (Do not click 'Send')
3. Close the Card
4. Reopen the Card
5. Comment is lost

## Expected behavior

Should either auto save or warn user changes will be lost.
title: Bug: Comments are lost when closing card

triage.test_hint: Type a comment without sending, close the card, reopen it and verify either the draft is preserved or a confirmation dialog is shown.

## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Go card
2. Click on link in comment
3. 2 new tabs opens in the browser

## Expected behavior

When I click on the link, I expect only 1 tab to open in my browser.

## Screenshots

![focalboard-card-comment-link-icon-broken](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/13135150/151193285-267b984b-93f8-4bc3-8a39-49fa946984cb.gif)

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Desktop
 - Version: v0.12.1
 - Browser and OS: 5.0.2497.48 (Stable channel) stable (64-bit) on Ubuntu 20.04.3 LTS
title: Bug: Links in card descriptions open 2 new tabs on Linux Personal Desktop App

triage.summary: On Linux Personal Desktop App, clicking a link in a card description/comment opens two browser tabs instead of one. Likely caused by both an Electron shell.openExternal call and a default anchor tag handler firing simultaneously.
triage.test_hint: Verify that clicking a hyperlink in a card description on the Linux Electron desktop app opens exactly one external browser tab. Check for duplicate event listeners or double handling of link clicks in the Electron main/renderer process (e.g., new-window event plus default anchor navigation both triggering openExternal).

## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Clone the repo at tag `v0.12.1`
2. Make the following changes
<details>
<summary>Changes</summary>

```diff
diff --git a/Makefile b/Makefile
index 0db63694..a5e661cf 100644
--- a/Makefile
+++ b/Makefile
@@ -40,6 +40,11 @@ server-linux: ## Build server for Linux.
        $(eval LDFLAGS += -X "github.com/mattermost/focalboard/server/model.Edition=linux")
        cd server; env GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64 go build -ldflags '$(LDFLAGS)' -o ../bin/linux/focalboard-server ./main

+server-linux-rpi: ## Build server for Linux.
+       mkdir -p bin/linux
+       $(eval LDFLAGS += -X "github.com/mattermost/focalboard/server/model.Edition=linux")
+       cd server; go build -ldflags '$(LDFLAGS)' -o ../bin/linux/focalboard-server ./main
+
```

```diff
diff --git a/docker/Dockerfile b/docker/Dockerfile
index c6b177ab..2d4c3510 100644
--- a/docker/Dockerfile
+++ b/docker/Dockerfile
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
 ### Webapp build
-FROM node:16.3.0 as nodebuild
+FROM node:16.3 as nodebuild

 WORKDIR /webapp
 ADD webapp/ /webapp
@@ -13,7 +13,7 @@ FROM golang:1.16.5 as gobuild
 WORKDIR /go/src/focalboard
 ADD . /go/src/focalboard

-RUN  make server-linux
+RUN make server-linux-rpi
 RUN mkdir /data

 ## Final image
```

body:
## Summary

Currently, I can view each single board within each single channel. And within a card, I can have a property to assign a card to somebody.

I would like to see all the cards assigned to me, within 1 single view.

## How important this is to me and why
We use cards to manage todos. This is more efficient to have 1 single view, compared to looking into each single board.

Use cases:
1. We have 2 channels: _Project_ and _Kitchen_
2. In _Project_, 2 cards (TaskA and TaskB) are assigned to me, within the property _Who_
3. In Kitchen, 1 card (TaskC) is assigned to me, within the property _Who_
4. I would love to have a view showing TaskA, TaskB and TaskC together. A simple table would be a great start. And a calendar view would be even better (not a priority though)

## Additional context/similar features
Trello does something similar, as far as I understand: https://help.trello.com/article/819-viewing-all-of-your-cards
title: Feature Idea: View all my cards from all boards

triage.skip_reason: Large cross-board aggregation feature requiring significant backend query design, new frontend view, and UX decisions about property mapping across heterogeneous boards.
triage.summary: Add a global 'My Cards' view that aggregates cards assigned to the current user across all boards/channels into a single unified view.

## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Open a card
2. Create a date property and edit it
3. Select a date
4. Enable "Date range" and select an end date
5. Click on the "today" button - it sets start date to today
7. Click "today" again - it sets the end date to today
8. Click "today" again - it clears the date
9. Click "today" again - it re-sets the date to just today, without a range

## Expected behavior

Clicking "today" the third time should not clear the date.

## Screenshots

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/46905241/148566850-962820c6-14d3-422c-8223-8bdcfa259b5e.mov

If only a single date is selected to begin with, clicking "today" multiple times does not clear the picker.
title: Bug: Date picker - clicking "today" multiple times when date range is set clears the date

triage.test_hint: Unit test for DateRange picker: set a date range, click 'today' three times and assert date is not cleared after the third click; it should remain set with start and end both equal to today.

title: Bug: using domain for mysql ,the docker not started

triage.summary: MySQL DSN parsing fails when using a hostname (domain) instead of localhost; the connection string format 'user:pass@host:port/db' is not being converted to the correct Go MySQL DSN format 'user:pass@tcp(host:port)/db', causing the driver to treat the host as a network protocol name.
triage.test_hint: Add a unit test that parses a MySQL DSN with a remote hostname (e.g. 'boards:boards@abc.dba.com:3306/boards') and verifies it is correctly rewritten to 'boards:boards@tcp(abc.dba.com:3306)/boards' before being passed to the database driver.

body:
## Summary

The point is not to lose focus on text fields when you press `Caps Lock`. I and probably many more who use several languages configure switching the keyboard layout to the caps lock key for speed and convenience instead of the common `Alt` + `Shift`.

### Expected Behavior:

I print text on a line, switch to another layout by pressing `Caps Lock`, and continue typing text.

### Current behavior:

I am typing text, there is a need to change the layout, I hit on the `Caps Lock` and the cursor loses focus. This results in the text being saved to the database (with a small lag). You have to click on the text field again to continue typing!

Due to the fact that you have to switch the layout very often, this causes very significant inconvenience in work.

The idea of ​​not losing focus when pressing the `Caps Lock` will also be useful in cases where the key is used for its intended purpose — to write characters in uppercase.
title: Enhancement: Don't lose focus on text areas when press Caps Lock

triage.summary: Pressing Caps Lock causes text fields to lose focus, interrupting typing and triggering premature database saves. Need to intercept/ignore the Caps Lock keydown event (or the blur triggered by it) in text area components to preserve focus.
triage.test_hint: Simulate a CapsLock keydown event on a text input component and assert that it does not lose focus (document.activeElement remains the input).

body:
## Summary

As the server gets more sophisticated, we recommend / require multi-user environments to use Postgres instead of SQLite. E.g. there are bugs like https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/1841 which are caused by concurrency issues on SQLite. 

As a result, we should default Personal Server installations to Postgres instead of SQLite.
title: Update the dockerfile to deploy Postgres instead of SQLite

triage.summary: Update the Dockerfile and related configuration to default to PostgreSQL instead of SQLite for Personal Server deployments, addressing concurrency issues present with SQLite in multi-user environments.
triage.test_hint: Verify the updated Dockerfile builds successfully, spins up a Postgres container, and that Focalboard connects and operates correctly against Postgres; confirm SQLite-related concurrency bugs (e.g. #1841) are no longer reproducible.

body:
**Summary**
I am using mattermost via reverse proxy. The URL to access mattermost is:
`https://mydomain/myhost/mattermost`

By selecting Boards on menu, Dashboard is displayed successfully. But URL is set as `https://mydomain/boards/dashboard` in URL bar.

Reloading the page, reverse proxy returns HTTP 404.

**Steps to reproduce**
Run Boards under reverse proxy emvironment.
Mattermost: 6.1.0
nginx(reverse proxy): 1.12.2

**Expected behavior**
URL is set as `https://mydomain/myhost/mattermost/boards/dashboard`

**Observed behavior**
URL is set as `https://mydomain/boards/dashboard`
title: Bug: Boards set incorrect URL at URL bar

triage.skip_reason: Routing/URL prefix handling depends on deployment configuration and reverse proxy setup; requires understanding of how the app constructs base URLs and may involve both frontend routing and backend config — not safely auto-fixable
triage.summary: When Focalboard/Boards is accessed via a reverse proxy with a subpath (e.g. /myhost/mattermost), the app incorrectly sets the URL bar to an absolute path omitting the subpath prefix, causing 404 on page reload.
triage.test_hint: Test with a reverse proxy serving Mattermost at a non-root subpath and verify that navigating to Boards sets the full correct URL including the subpath prefix, and that reloading the page does not 404.

title: Bug: Mattermost Boards misbehaving after upgrade to V6

triage.skip_reason: No clear repro steps beyond version upgrade; root cause (server not binding to port) needs backend investigation and likely involves plugin lifecycle or WebSocket connection handling changes in MM v6
triage.summary: After upgrading Mattermost Server to v6.x, the Boards plugin fails to bind to any port, causing RPC failures, health check failures, and UI changes not reflecting without a page refresh (WebSocket updates broken).
triage.test_hint: Test plugin startup and port binding behavior under Mattermost v6.x plugin API; verify WebSocket/RPC lifecycle during plugin activation and deactivation.

As of v0.10, a user can only add/delete/duplicate views from the dropdown menu within the specific view's page. This menu should be accessible on the sidebar so users can manage their views more easily and intuitively.

body:
## Summary

Currently, the `create_at` and `update_at` columns in various tables are nullable on the database level. Ideally, the columns should never have a null value and should reflect the correct creation/update time.

Following changes are needed to support this-
1. Update `create_at` and `update_at` columns in all focalboard tables (except blocks history table) to be filled by current timestamp wherever the value is null.
2. Add a not-null constraint on `create_at` and `update_at` columns in all focalboard tables (except blocks history table).

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium
title: Add database level defau;t values for create at and update at columns

triage.skip_reason: Requires careful database migration across multiple tables and DB engines (postgres/mysql/sqlite); risk of data loss if nulls exist and migration is incomplete; needs human review of migration scripts
triage.summary: Add NOT NULL constraints and default values for create_at and update_at columns across all focalboard tables (except blocks_history), including a migration to backfill any existing nulls.
triage.test_hint: Write migration tests verifying: (1) existing null values are backfilled before constraint is applied, (2) NOT NULL constraint is present after migration, (3) new inserts without explicit timestamps receive current timestamp defaults, across postgres/mysql/sqlite.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Go to a board
2. Select  "Export to CSV" or "Export to board archive"
3. See error in popup window:

```txt
Failed to open URI.
The specified location is not supported.
data:text/csv;charset=utf-8,Name ...
```

or

```txt
Failed to open URI.
The specified location is not supported.
data:text/json, ...
```

4. The Snackbar does not get that there was an error and says "Export Complete!"

---

1. Select "Settings"
2. Select  "Export archive"
3. See error in popup window:

```txt
Failed to open URI.
Operation not supported.
data:text/json, ...
```

## Expected behavior

In the Windows App Store installation on my Windows PC a `.csv` or `.focalboard` file is created.
The bug makes it impossible to export boards.

## Screenshots

Not necessary since the error popup window was transcribed.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Desktop
 - Version: v0.10.0 (built by myself), v0.9.2 (downloaded from GitHub releases)
 - Browser and OS: Manjaro Linux (21.2.0, kernel 5.14.13-1-MANJARO, XFCE 4.16, Pacman)

## Additional context

To run the local desktop build I run:

```sh
make prebuild
make linux-app
cd linux
./bin/focalboard-app
```

because the in the README.md described `linux/dist/focalboard-app` path does not exist and running `linux/bin/focalboard-app` starts a completely white window.
title: Bug: Exporting boards on Manjaro Linux XFCE with Personal Desktop Edition results in an "Failed to open URI." error

triage.skip_reason: Requires testing on Linux desktop environment with specific GTK/xdg-open URI handling; UI/platform behavior decision needed for data-URI download fallback
triage.summary: On Linux desktop (Manjaro XFCE), exporting boards fails with 'Failed to open URI / The specified location is not supported' because the Electron/desktop app attempts to open a data: URI via xdg-open which is not supported on this platform. Windows handles it correctly. A platform-aware download mechanism (e.g. writing file to disk then opening, or using Electron's dialog.showSaveDialog) is needed instead of data: URI navigation.
triage.test_hint: Test CSV and board archive export on Linux desktop build; verify a file is saved to disk without error dialog; also verify snackbar correctly reflects failure state when export fails.

body:
## Summary

While adding Unit Tests on [this issue](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/832), I found that we are importing `@testing-library/react` in all the test files to extend the RTL (React Testing Libray) custom jest matchers. `jest` actually provides an easier way to handle this where we can add a setup file that is present for all the tests before they start.

This way, we can add the RTL import there and avoid all tests importing the same.

[Reference](https://jestjs.io/docs/configuration#setupfilesafterenv-array)

## How important this is to me and why

Nothing customer-facing but as a developer, this makes me write tests seamlessly without worrying about those special imports. I would consider this as `High` 

## Additional context/similar features

Not specific ones but I have seen many open source projects using common files for jest RTL imports. 

Would be willing to submit a PR if this sounds good 🙂
title: Feature Idea: add common jest import file to configure RTL imports

triage.summary: Add a Jest setupFilesAfterFramework config file to globally import @testing-library/react matchers, removing the need for per-test-file RTL imports.
triage.test_hint: Verify existing tests still pass after removing per-file RTL imports and confirm the setup file is picked up by running the full jest suite.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Go to [LICENSE.txt](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/blob/main/LICENSE.txt)
2. See the terms `You are licensed to use compiled versions of Focalboard produced by Mattermost, Inc. under an MIT LICENSE`
3. MIT is a **source code** license specifically granting permission to "modify, merge, publish, distribute, sublicense, and/or sell copies of the Software". You are using it on specific binaries (that I cannot "modify, merge, ..." without making them *other* binaries)
4. None of the MIT license terms mean anything in this context. The rest of the MIT license is about the copyright notice text, which is not included in your binary (how can text be included in binaries?)

## Expected behavior

Your licensing terms let me know how your software is licensed.

## Additional context

This is like selling someone a toaster and telling them they have to abide by US driving laws when using it. What does it mean? Do I have to use turn-signals before removing toast? Do I need a state-issued ID to operate it? I can't even consider using it.

**If you mean to say that I can use those binaries unmodified for any purpose, just say that. Don't tell me I can use them under the terms of the Civil Aviation Law rules, none of which apply to your tool.**
title: Bug: license terms

triage.summary: LICENSE.txt applies MIT license terms to compiled binaries, which is semantically incorrect — MIT is a source code license and its terms are meaningless when applied to binaries. The license file should clearly state the actual terms for binary usage.
triage.test_hint: Review LICENSE.txt and clarify binary vs source licensing terms; ensure the language accurately reflects what users are permitted to do with compiled binaries.

## Summary

I'm working on multiple machines and therefore having the possibility to synchronize is very important for me. I wish a similar kind of synchronization as [Joplin](https://joplinapp.org/) has where I choose between different options (self-hosted Nextcloud server in my case). A web-only solution wouldn't work for me, since I'm quite often work without internet connection. If synchronization is already supported, let me know, I couldn't find anything.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: High

Use cases:
1. Working on multiple devices with no internet connection from time to time.

body:
## Summary

linux-app should use `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` and `$HOME/.config` to save everything that requires write access.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: High

Use cases:
1. Distribute as a package
2. Per-user config and database

## Additional context/similar features

I've spent some time working on packaging focalboard into the following formats: rpm, deb, flatpak
While everything is going as planned there's a major issue; apps don't have write access to their installed location by default (in focalboard's case, `/opt/focalboard-app`).

While there might be workarounds, the best practice would be to move anything that requires write access to `.config` (`dbconfig` & `filespath` (?)). Doing so will also allow each user to have their own .db as well as ease backups and restores.

From a look at the source, it seems that `config.json` is being ignored (for the app at least).

Here's some solutions I came up with:

- Use the values from the config.json in combination with the hardcoded ones.
- Use cli flags or env vars to override them
- Change the hardcoded value to use `~/.config`
```go
// linux/main.go
...
func runServer(port int) (*server.Server, error) {
	logger, _ := mlog.NewLogger()
+	configFolder := os.Getenv("XDG_CONFIG_HOME")
+	if len(configFolder) == 0 {
+		configFolder = os.Getenv("HOME") + "/.config"
+	}

+	configFolder = configFolder + "/focalboard-app"

+	if _, err := os.Stat(configFolder); os.IsNotExist(err) {
+		_ = os.Mkdir(configFolder, 0755)
+	}

	config := &config.Configuration{
		ServerRoot:              fmt.Sprintf("http://localhost:%d", port),
		Port:                    port,
		DBType:                  "sqlite3",
+		DBConfigString:          configFolder + "/focalboard.db",
		UseSSL:                  false,
		SecureCookie:            true,
		WebPath:                 "./pack",
		FilesDriver:             "local",
+		FilesPath:               configFolder + "/focalboard_files",
		Telemetry:               true,
		WebhookUpdate:           []string{},
		SessionExpireTime:       259200000000,
		SessionRefreshTime:      18000,
		LocalOnly:               false,
		EnableLocalMode:         false,
		LocalModeSocketLocation: "",
		AuthMode:                "native",
	}
...
```

Some notes on them:
- The third one could be used with #1018 which would handle the creation of the config folder
- #1256 (for linux-app) would also be useful for per-user configuration (plus wouldn't require root to edit it)
- The third solution would unfortunately break existing installations as the hardcoded path would change
- Both the first and second solutions are easy to use with the packages

After a solution to this is found, I'll be able to continue working on packaging focalboard in more formats (rpm, deb, flatpak, appimage, snap).
title: Feature Idea: linux-app should use ~/.config for anything that needs write access

triage.summary: Linux desktop app should store writable data (DB, files) under $XDG_CONFIG_HOME (~/.config/focalboard-app) instead of the install directory, enabling proper packaging (rpm/deb/flatpak) and per-user data isolation.
triage.test_hint: On Linux, launch the app and verify focalboard.db and focalboard_files are created under $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/focalboard-app (or ~/.config/focalboard-app when XDG_CONFIG_HOME is unset). Also verify app starts correctly when install dir is read-only.

body:
Nice work Boards team on the v0.9.1 enhancements! Posting here to share a few thoughts on further improvements you may or may not have already considered.

1. Welcome to Boards screen seems like it could be scaled up quite significantly

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14333569/135539472-f305dc5f-d48d-4655-a0e0-0877b8d7a455.png)

2. With no CTA button on the empty screen for a workspace I kind of feel lost with what to do and where I am when I open the board for an empty channel. I'd propose just to duplicate the "+ Add Board" button as a CTA on the empty screen and maybe add an illustration to make it feel friendly and more inviting with a clear action to take (see example proposal). Especially on big monitors the add boards button is reeeally out of the way (see pic). 

Example of empty state with large screen:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14333569/135539919-9a420bbb-c40b-4769-b948-3ee187ac0a37.png)

Proposal (rough wireframe):
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14333569/135540100-ff628aca-3819-46c7-955e-1fdc99f48f8a.png)

3. If boards do exist in a channel it would REALLY improve UX if we just opened the first board in the sidebar rather than showed a white screen with instructions to select a board

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14333569/135539877-329ffae1-4cd5-4c16-92c8-259aadb1d813.png)

4. Not sure what the effort involved, but sorting the boards in the dashboard and switcher by recently viewed would make both these features significantly more useful. Typing to search is nice, but if I can just see what I'm looking for at a glance it would be better. I bet it's unlikely that users would be actively working in more than a few boards, so surfacing the most active ones would be a big UX win

5. The dashboard view doesn't seem to scroll if there's a lot of channels with boards in them. If the dashboard instead became a "Recently viewed" list with a max number (ie 10), you might not have to support scrolling at all. I know there are some more improvements to the dashboard coming, so just sharing some ideas. 

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14333569/135540874-3f103b41-a825-4559-8aeb-afbe354df08d.png)

6. I was confused to see the dashboard view had tiles for channels with 0 boards in them. Should we just hide those tiles?

7. I found the reference to "change channels" near the switcher and "All Channels" in the dashboard to be confusing. I guess we are purposely trying to make the connection between boards and channels by doing this.

8. Tiles in the dashboard view did not have an elevation or hover effect
title: FTUE boards UX enhancements

triage.skip_reason: Multiple distinct UX enhancements requiring UI/UX design decisions, visual assets, and significant frontend changes across several components
triage.summary: FTUE (First-Time User Experience) UX improvements: scale welcome screen, add empty-state CTA with illustration, auto-open first board when boards exist, sort boards by recently viewed in dashboard/switcher, fix dashboard scrolling with many channels

body:
**Summary:**
If I add last updated by to an existing card, I am probably doing it because I want to see when the card was last updated.
However, adding that field causes the last updated date/time to reset to when the property was added.
Having this change not effect last update date/time would allow the user to view when a card was last updated at any point rather than having to have the property already exist.

Importance: Low - Nice to have but not essential


**Additional context / similar features:**

IMO - these fields shouldn't be properties in general, or should be baked in fields somewhere ("view card details in the ... menu?") as well as being properties.  I'd personally prefer they were baked into the card interface because generally these are useful things to know and are related to auditing - not assignment or part of my workflow.
title: Feature Idea: Adding "Last Edited By" Property should not update last edited by field.

triage.skip_reason: Requires backend logic change to distinguish metadata-only operations from content edits; involves UI/UX decisions about which operations should update audit fields
triage.summary: Adding 'Last Edited By'/'Last Edited At' properties to a card should not itself trigger an update to those audit fields, since the user is adding them retrospectively to view existing audit data.
triage.test_hint: When adding a last-edited-by or last-edited-at property to a card, verify the stored last_edit_at timestamp and last_edit_by user do not change after the property is added.

**Steps to reproduce the behavior:**
1. On a board and a gallery view, create a card with some text at first and then a Markdown image tag, with the format `![](IMAGE_URL)`
2. From the gallery view, drag and drop it to change its order with other cards, starting the drag action from the text of the card. The order change should happen successfully
3. Do exactly the same thing, but starting the drag action clicking on the image. After dropping the card, the URL changes to the linked image one, out of Boards

**Expected behavior:**
The drag&drop action should work as described in 2.

**Screenshots:**
This is an example of a card with text and an image that was used to reproduce the problem:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/223323/134032792-b15f5bf7-d7e7-4dba-9cfa-0858901383d3.png)

 - Edition: Mattermost plugin
 - Version: v0.9.1 RC1

title: Bug: drag&drop fails if you drag from a Markdown image

triage.summary: Dragging a gallery card starting from a Markdown-rendered image causes the browser to follow the image URL instead of performing the card drag-and-drop reorder. Need to call preventDefault/stopPropagation on dragstart for img elements inside card previews, or set draggable=false on rendered images in card content.
triage.test_hint: In gallery view, create a card with a markdown image, attempt to drag the card starting from the image element, verify card reorders correctly and URL does not change to the image URL.
