body:
**Steps to reproduce the behavior:** 1. Create a new board 2. Add a description 3. Within the description, insert a large image using a Markdown expression like `![astrofish](https://media.snipettemag.com/seance-uploads/2021/8/ocean-genes/1vobi2tLbk2oQknaeBF3HGQ.jpeg)` 4. The image now spills out of the screen because its dimensions are larger than the screen width. Additionally, one can't scroll down to see the cards below, because not enough space is reserved for scrolling down that far. **Expected behavior:** The maximum width for images should be restricted, as happens for images *within* a Markdown card. Also, enough space should be reserved on the bottom so one can scroll down to the bottom of the page. (Right now, the page height seems to be getting calculated without taking the image height into account).
Screenshots (click to expand) Board containing oversized image: ![Board containing oversized image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7288084/133374789-eb00c6cc-8879-440b-964b-04846d9e0d1c.png) Scroll restriction: I am not able to scroll the page beyond this point to see the table and cards below: ![Board scrolled down as low as possible, but cards still not visible](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7288084/133374850-4a9df373-f5bc-4cf8-bd34-5b2b771a94b8.png) :point_up: can't scroll more beyond this :( For comparison, here is the board without the card: ![Working board without card](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7288084/133374941-dcd49dc6-79b7-46bc-a2f6-bdced63546d0.png) Inline images work fine when inside a card (as opposed to in the main board description): ![Image embedded in card and working fine (view from beginning)](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7288084/133375023-5c601b5d-9124-4c1a-8feb-0b510a25f3ee.png) ![Image embedded in card and working fine (view after scrolling down)](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7288084/133375031-3a912d12-b86a-4709-9f25-ca6ca4a59178.png)
**Platform:** - Browser and version: Firefox 91.0.2 - OS and version: Manjaro 21.1.2 "Pahvo" - Focalboard version: Mattermost plugin, v0.8.2 **Additional context:** This seems to be something that can be solved by adjusting the image class. For instance, simply adding the CSS rule ```css img { max-width: 100%; } ``` got the image back down to a reasonable size, although I still wasn't able to scroll down enough to reach the bottom of the page. The images within a card uses a rule for `.octo-block img`, which I'm guessing images in the board description are missing because they aren't part of an `octo-block`. Expanding the full rule to include board description cards makes things even better, but I am *still* not able to scroll down, so that's the main issue to be investigated. PS: If someone were to give me pointers, I'd be happy to have a go at debugging this myself :slightly_smiling_face:
title: Bug: Inline images in board description overflow from screen

triage.summary: Inline images in board description overflow horizontally beyond screen width and cause incorrect page height calculation, preventing scrolling to cards below. Fix requires applying max-width:100% (and possibly max-height with overflow handling) to images rendered in the board description markdown component, mirroring the CSS already applied to images inside card markdown views.
triage.test_hint: Insert a large external image via markdown in a board description, verify image is constrained within viewport width, and verify cards below the description are reachable by scrolling.

body:
**Summary:**
When running as a plugin, Boards should use the Mattermost language setting if none is currently set and that language translation is available. It should fall-back to English as the default if the language is not available.

**Additional context / similar features:**
For now, the user can choose a language in Boards independent of Mattermost, but Boards should default to the Mattermost language setting for consistency.

Also, to clarify the expected behavior:
* If a user select a language (e.g. Spanish) in Mattermost Channels then switches to Boards for the first time, the interface should be in that language (Spanish).
  * Unless the translation is not available, in which case default to English.
* If the user chooses a different language in Boards (e.g. Indonesian), Boards will display in that language, but this will not affect the Channels setting
  * From this point on, Boards will show in that explicit language setting (Indonesian) regardless of the Mattermost setting
* Channels will always show the language in the Mattermost setting
title: Honor the Mattermost language setting if possible

triage.skip_reason: Requires UI/UX decisions and integration with Mattermost plugin API for language detection; non-trivial state logic
triage.summary: When running as a Mattermost plugin, Boards should default to the Mattermost language setting on first use, falling back to English if the translation is unavailable. Once a user explicitly sets a language in Boards, that choice takes precedence over the Mattermost setting.
triage.test_hint: Verify that a first-time Boards user sees the same language as their Mattermost setting; verify fallback to English for unsupported languages; verify that an explicit Boards language selection persists and overrides the Mattermost setting on subsequent visits.

body:
**Summary:**

The Focalboard plugin v0.8.2 on my Mattermost Teams Edition 5.38.0 installed with docker behind an Apache2 reverse proxy installation has websocket connection interruptions.
Mattermost works perfectly without any issue, chat is updated live and have been working fine since the beginning.
Focalboard works but does not save everything, probably due to the websocket issue.

**Steps to reproduce the behavior:**

1. Install Focalboard plugin v0.8.2 via MarketPlace
2. Update the apache configuration following the documentation [here](https://www.focalboard.com/download/mattermost/#with-apache-unofficial)
3. Activate it
4. Clic on Focalboard icon in a channel

**Expected behavior:**
Focalboard should not show the banner about websocket connection loss.

**Screenshots:**

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/25644865/131575838-6b2346fe-e745-44a7-96c5-c2ccb0bac946.png)

**Platform:**
 - Browser and version: Chrome 92
 - Mattermost Server v5.38.0 (docker)
 - Mattermost Desktop 4.7.1
 - Focalboard plugin v0.8.2
 - OS and version: macOS 11.5.2

**Additional context:**

Here is the relevant part of my Apache2 configuration:
```apache
    RewriteEngine On
    RewriteCond %{REQUEST_URI} /api/v[0-9]+/(users/)?websocket|/plugins/focalboard/ws/* [NC,OR]
    RewriteCond %{HTTP:UPGRADE} ^WebSocket$ [NC,OR]
    RewriteCond %{HTTP:CONNECTION} ^Upgrade$ [NC]
    RewriteRule .* ws://127.0.0.1:8065%{REQUEST_URI} [P,QSA,L]

    RewriteCond %{REQUEST_URI} ^/plugins/focalboard
    RewriteRule ^ http://%{HTTP_HOST}/mattermost%{REQUEST_URI} [R=301]

    RewriteCond %{REQUEST_URI} ^/mattermost
    RewriteRule .* http://127.0.0.1:8065%{REQUEST_URI} [P,QSA,L]

    <Location /mattermost>
            Require all granted
            ProxyPass http://127.0.0.1:8065/
            ProxyPassReverse http://127.0.0.1:8065/
            ProxyPassReverseCookieDomain 127.0.0.1 dsimb.inserm.fr
    </Location>
```
title: Bug: plugin websocket connection interrupted behind Apache2 reverse proxy

triage.skip_reason: Requires infrastructure/proxy environment to reproduce; fix likely involves documentation update or WebSocket URL path change in plugin code; needs human judgment on correct Apache config and potential server-side path adjustments
triage.summary: Focalboard plugin WebSocket connection drops when Mattermost is deployed behind an Apache2 reverse proxy. The WebSocket upgrade rewrite rule for /plugins/focalboard/ws/* is not matching correctly, causing repeated connection loss banners and potential data not being saved.
triage.test_hint: Verify WebSocket handshake succeeds end-to-end with Apache2 proxy config; check that the ws:// rewrite rule correctly captures the plugin WebSocket path and does not conflict with the /plugins/focalboard redirect rule.

body:
### Summary
Please allow us to add userscripts and userstyles to the DOM.

### How important this is to me and why:
Importance: Medium

### Use cases:
There are loads of feature requests and bugs in the issue tracker. Obviously there are many great ideas but not enough time to implement them all.

Some apps allow you to use custom userscripts (using tampermonkey, for example) and custom userstyles (using stylish, for example) to mod the interface. I expect that would lead to less feature requests for little UI tweaks and thus allow devs to focus on the important stuff.

For example, I added a [feature request](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/1055) to rename the "NO STATUS" column. I expect many don't care for that, so it may take forever to be implemented, if ever. But if I could use a userscript, I'd just find that node in the DOM and either delete it or rename it - I wouldn't bother asking for it as a formal feature.

### Implementation
The easiest way to implement this is to allow for `custom.js` and `custom.css` files that are appended to the DOM at the very end of the loading process. They could be placed in the same location as `/opt/focalboard/config.json`.

**So if `/opt/focalboard/custom.js` and `/opt/focalboard/custom.css` are found, they're appended to the DOM, else not.**

### Additional context
If this is implemented, we could share our useful js/css in a github gist, until they make their way into the actual code.

Note that custom js/css can be used right now, but only for you - it wouldn't work for focalboard's other users. Hence this feature request.
title: Feature Idea: Custom css and js

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX and security implications require human design decisions; serving arbitrary JS/CSS files raises XSS and CSP concerns
triage.summary: Allow server-side custom.js and custom.css files to be served and injected into the DOM, enabling user-defined styling and scripting without code changes.
triage.test_hint: Verify custom.js and custom.css are loaded when present in config directory; verify app loads normally when files are absent; check CSP headers are not broken.

body:
**Summary:**
I'd like to be able to filter by dates, numbers, regex patterns on strings / urls / etc.

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: High

To be able to use focalboard to store data, filtering is a key requirement for me. For example if I want to track tasks, having due dates on those tasks, being able to build a view that shows only tasks that are overdue seems crucial.

Use cases:
1. Show only tasks overdue
2. Show only tasks that can be started after X date
3. Add a numerical priority field and show only tasks with prio > 3

**Additional context / similar features:**
Trello has filtering capabilities of some kind, I'm not familiar with asana / notion but I'd assume they do.

FYI, if this is already implemented, then I'm reporting a bug that on the standalone desktop app on macOS I can only seem to filter by status fields and not others.
title: Feature Idea: Filter by more field types

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring new filter logic for multiple field types (date, number, regex); needs design decisions on filter operators per type
triage.summary: Allow filtering board views by date fields (e.g. overdue, after X), number fields (e.g. priority > 3), and regex/pattern matching on text/URL fields. Currently only status fields are filterable in the desktop app.
triage.test_hint: Verify filter panel exposes date, number, and text field types; test date comparisons (before/after/overdue), numeric comparisons (gt/lt/eq), and string pattern matching across gallery, board, and table views.

body:
**Summary:**
I'd like to have an overview of all my projects on a single board. Then I'd like to be able to click from that board directly to another board for each project. This would be possible in the web based version using simple URLs, but not in the desktop version.

In the spirit of mattermost/mattermost-server#23273 it would be cool to have a property type which contains a link to another board. Both as a choice (so I can choose one of my existing boards as a dropdown value) and as a "link" which allows me to click to that board.

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: Low

Use cases:
1. Maintaining an overview board that shows an overview of individual projects, each which have their own board
2. Being able to take 1 list of tasks and break it out into a list of boards (so each task gets a whole board)
3. Maintaining a list of persons assigned to boards by having a meta board that shows the person and the board per card
4. Adding metadata to boards by having a single overview board that tracks boards and extra metadata
5. Allowing for custom "navigation" scenarios, where users can be sent to a single board to start with and from there find their way to other boards

**Additional context / similar features:**
Not sure if this is implemented elsewhere or not.
title: Feature Idea: Property type that is a link to another board

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring new property type, UI for board selection dropdown, cross-board navigation logic, and desktop app deep-link support. Requires product/design decisions.
triage.summary: Add a new card property type that links to another board, rendered as a clickable dropdown selector populated with existing boards, enabling cross-board navigation from overview/meta boards.
triage.test_hint: Verify board-link property renders as dropdown of available boards, selecting a board stores its ID, clicking the link navigates to the correct board in both web and desktop, and the property survives board rename/deletion gracefully.

The commands on [this page](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/blob/main/docker/README.md) need to be updated. This works for me.
```
git clone https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard.git
sudo docker build -f docker/Dockerfile -t focalboard .
```

**Platform:**
 - OS and version: Arch Linux, kernel 5.13.7-arch1-1
title: Bug: Docker build error

triage.summary: Docker build fails when run from the docker/ subdirectory because the Dockerfile uses paths relative to the repo root. The README instructions need to be updated to run `docker build -f docker/Dockerfile -t focalboard .` from the repo root instead.
triage.test_hint: Run the corrected docker build command from the repo root and verify it completes without 'file not found in build context' errors.

body:
**Summary:**
The plugin for the Mattermost app currently opens as a "separate window" to mattermost, however this is not selectable from the windows taskbar. Instead, you have to open the mattermost application and then the focalboard window pops up on top.

**How important this is to me and why:**
If I've got a project that I'm working on, it would be nice to have the board for that project available all the time, so that I can make focus directly on that board instead of having to open mattermost, then focus on the board.

It would also be useful to be able to switch between channels within the focalboard window.


Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. User has a project that they want to focus on without being distracted by the mattermost window in the background
2. User wants to go directly to the focalboard project instead of opening Mattermost, then focalboard.
3. User does not want to have focalboard project appear in the way of their mattermost window when they return to mattermost.

**Additional context / similar features:**
good implementation / workaround of this capability: Using mattermost from a browser. You can open the focalboard plugin in a separate tab, put it in to a separate window, etc.
title: Feature Idea: Enable plugin as a separate window to Mattermost

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX decision requiring desktop app architecture changes and product design input; touches Mattermost desktop app windowing model which is out of Focalboard's direct control
triage.summary: Allow Focalboard plugin to open as a standalone selectable window on the OS taskbar within the Mattermost desktop app, rather than a floating overlay that requires Mattermost to be focused first.

body:
**Summary:**
Deleting a card component doesn't update the card's updated at timestamp

**Steps to reproduce the behavior:**
1. Open a card.
2. Add some items to it such as comments or text. Notice that adding these will update the card's updated at timestamp.
3. Now delete some added comments. Notice that doing so brings back the previous updated at for the card.

**Expected behavior:**
Deleting a card component should update car's updated at timestamp to the moment something was deleted.

**Screenshots:**
NA

**Platform:**
 - Browser and version: all browsers
 - OS and version: all OS

**Additional context:**
Card's updated at is calculated from the most recently updated component in the client. Since deleted components are not sent in the blocks API, the client is not aware of their existence. Hence, the outdated updated at time.
title: Bug: Deleting a card component doesn't update the card's updated at timestamp

triage.skip_reason: Requires understanding of backend API design and whether to send deleted block timestamps or compute updated_at server-side; not a trivial fix
triage.summary: Deleting a card component (e.g. comment, text block) does not update the card's 'updated at' timestamp. The client calculates updated_at from the most recently updated component, but deleted blocks are not returned by the blocks API, so the previous (stale) timestamp from surviving blocks is shown instead.
triage.test_hint: Add a comment to a card, note the updated_at timestamp, then delete the comment and verify the card's updated_at reflects the deletion time rather than reverting to the prior timestamp.

body:
**Summary:**
Copy a card from one board to another or even better to sync the card to another board, so you can have it on two boards simultaneously and only need to update it in one place.  

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. OKRs/Roadmaps: PMs or Eng can have their own roadmap/OKR for their area of ownership and sync a card to a combined board
2. Release management: We can have a focalboard to track all features that are getting into a release from roadmaps. 
3. Mulitple teams working on similar items: (Potentially could be used as an epic once we can do task linking) Task for tracking design, then a task for implementation by the development team, then a task for docs all on their own focalboards. 
4. Personal task management: Getting a task card from a project board to my own task tracking board for personal time allocation. 

**Additional context / similar features:**
Productboard will do a sync with Jira tasks, so I am loosely basing off of that functionality.
title: Feature Idea: Copy or Sync card to another board

triage.skip_reason: Large cross-board sync/copy feature requiring significant backend and frontend design decisions around data consistency, conflict resolution, and real-time sync semantics.
triage.summary: Allow users to copy a card to another board or sync a card bidirectionally across boards so updates in one place propagate to all linked instances. Use cases include OKR/roadmap aggregation, release management, cross-team epics, and personal task management.

body:
**Summary:**
After getting logged out for an expired session, I logged back in, but after doing that, I got sent back to the error page

**Steps to reproduce the behavio*u*r:**
1. Get logged out of Focalboard due to an expired session
2. Open Focalboard from the button in Mattermost (as on community.mattermost.com)
3. Get sent to the error page (https://focalboard-community.octo.mattermost.com/error?id=no_workspace)
4. Click on "log in again" and log back in

**Expected behavio*u*r:**
That I'd be sent to the board link I had clicked on from within Mattermost. Instead, I was sent back to https://focalboard-community.octo.mattermost.com/error?id=no_workspace after logging back in

**Screenshots:**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3277310/124664143-01f25580-de79-11eb-9761-4447a0ebafa1.png)

**Platform:**
 - Firefox 89.0.2
 - macOS 10.15.7
title: Bug: Returned to error page when logging in after an expired session

triage.summary: After session expiry, the login redirect target is set to the error page URL instead of the original board URL, so the user lands back on the error page after re-authenticating.
triage.test_hint: After session expiry, verify that the post-login redirect points to the originally requested board URL, not the error page URL. Check the logic that stores the redirect/return URL when the user is sent to the error page and ensure it preserves the originating board path.

body:
**Summary:**
Be able to assign a card to a user. There are several ways we could implement this:
1. Add an "assigned to" property to each card (so every card has this)
2. Add a new "assigned to" property type (so it can be optionally added to a card)

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: High

Use cases:
1. Show task assignees for projects
2. Notify assignees of changes

**Additional context / similar features:**
A related feature is adding a "due date" property, so that assignees can be notified of pending deadlines.

This is related to the [user property type feature](https://focalboard-community.octo.mattermost.com/workspace/qgsck6cts3fwpqwyjiupjm5cde/shared?id=47aa9bb4-6967-4a96-83c7-11bd6b20f1eb&v=ca1a5441-4c1c-4d0e-856e-88cc744576c8&r=762db9c6-305c-4d13-8ce6-9f11e5232c24&c=2b8cc90a-e91c-4d62-8d95-2f219ab248a7).

Filing this issue to solicit feedback. Are there any other typical use cases you have around this?
title: Feature Idea: Assigning cards to users

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring UI decisions, backend schema changes, and notification system integration; needs design discussion and community input
triage.summary: Add an 'assigned to' property type allowing users to be assigned to cards, enabling task ownership and future notification of assignees on changes or deadlines.

body:
**Summary:**
Focalboard should also have a timeline / roadmap

**How important is this::**
It's tough to define the importance but let me put it this way...
everyone else you compare yourself to, has it (sometimes with third-party tools).

On the Focalboard website the catch is "Focalboard is an open source, self-hosted alternative to Trello, Notion, and Asana."
Since I tried Trello and Asana within the last months I wanted to test focalboard next. Sadly the timeline / gantt / roadmap is an essential feature (for me). Since there is none, I just can't jump in and use it.

Importance: Medium / High

Use cases:
1. Timeline view (Gantt chart)
2. Project roadmap / status
3. "project planning" in general?

**Additional context / similar features:**
I didn't try Notion but they have one too apparently: [https://www.notion.vip/meet-notions-timeline-view/](https://www.notion.vip/meet-notions-timeline-view/)
TeamGantt for Trello looks stunning: [https://www.teamgantt.com](https://www.teamgantt.com)
Asana has a great timeline implemented: [https://asana.com/product/timeline](https://asana.com/product/timeline)
title: Feature Idea: Timeline / Gantt

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring significant UI and backend design decisions; timeline/Gantt view is a substantial engineering effort with unclear scope and UX requirements.
triage.summary: Request to add a Timeline/Gantt chart view for project planning and roadmapping, similar to features in Notion, Asana, and Trello integrations.

body:
**Summary:**
 Two webapp tests seem to be flaky-

```
1) Create and delete board / card
       Can create a table view:
     AssertionError: Timed out retrying after 4000ms: Expected to find element: `.ViewHeader .Editable[title='Table view']`, but never found it.
      at Context.eval (http://localhost:8088/__cypress/tests?p=cypress/integration/createBoard.js:163:59)

  2) Create and delete board / card
       Can rename the table view:
     AssertionError: Timed out retrying after 4000ms: Expected to find element: `.ViewHeader .Editable[title='Table view']`, but never found it.
      at Context.eval (http://localhost:8088/__cypress/tests?p=cypress/integration/createBoard.js:170:8)
```

More details of the failed CI run can be found here -https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/runs/2952731939?check_suite_focus=true#step:12:139

We need to verify the behaviour and fix the tests.
title: Investigate possible flaky webapp tests

triage.skip_reason: Flaky test investigation requires observing non-deterministic CI behavior and UI timing; needs human verification of root cause before fixing
triage.summary: Two Cypress integration tests in createBoard.js are intermittently failing due to timeout waiting for `.ViewHeader .Editable[title='Table view']` element, suggesting a race condition or timing issue in the table view creation/rename flow.
triage.test_hint: Review cypress/integration/createBoard.js around lines 163 and 170; add appropriate cy.intercept() waits or increase retry logic to ensure the table view header is fully rendered before assertions. Check if async state updates or animations are causing the element to appear after the default 4000ms timeout.

body:
**Summary:**
Each card can have an auto incrementing number similar to issues in Github/Jira in order to better reference cards when developing software (in Commits or MRs/PRs)

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Software development
2. Talking about/referencing issues within Mattermost or in Meetings

**Additional context / similar features:**
- Jira does this with adding project ID and a auto-incrementing number before each ticket
 Example: `https://jira.example.com/browse/OWSWS-422` -> ID = `OWSWS-422`
- Trello has this at the beginning of the unique identifier in the URL
 Example: `https://trello.com/c/[boardid]/5893-weddingvideo-n2` -> ID = `5893`
title: Feature Idea: Show ID/Number on each card

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX decisions required and involves schema changes for auto-incrementing card IDs
triage.summary: Add auto-incrementing numeric IDs to cards for easier cross-tool referencing (similar to Jira/GitHub issue numbers), enabling users to cite cards in commits, PRs, or Mattermost messages.
triage.test_hint: Verify new cards receive sequential IDs per board, IDs are displayed on card UI, and existing cards get backfilled or assigned IDs without collision.
